flavorText: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

name: Burmy
flavorText: If its cloak is broken in battle, it quickly remakes the cloak with materials nearby.

name: Paras
flavorText: Whether it's due to a lack of moisture or a lack of nutrients, in Alola the mushrooms on Paras don't grow up quite right.

name: Blastoise

abilities.name: Rain Dance
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach 1 Water Energy card to 1 of your Water Pokémon. (This doesn't use up your 1 Energy card attachment for the turn.) This power can't be used if Blastoise is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Water Energy after the 2nd doesn't count.

flavorText: A brutal Pokémon with pressurized water jets on its shell. They are used for high-speed tackles.

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Bye-Bye Heal
attacks.text: Heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Close examination of its large eyes reveals that each eye is composed of a myriad of tiny eyes.

name: Seedot

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It attaches itself to a tree branch using the top of its head. Strong winds can sometimes make it fall.

name: Weedle

attacks.name: Leaf Munch
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Grass Pokémon, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

name: Blastoise δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Metal type.

abilities.name: Shield Veil
abilities.text: Each of your Active Pokémon has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Enraged Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on Blastoise to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Charizard

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: When expelling a blast of superhot fire, the red flame at the tip of its tail burns more intensely.

name: Dialga

abilities.name: Time Aura
abilities.text: As long as Dialga is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Burn
attacks.text: Discard all Metal Energy attached to Dialga.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

name: Pachirisu

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Pachirisu does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

name: Tangrowth

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Grass Knot
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can't even see its eyes.

name: Rampardos

attacks.name: Assurance
attacks.text: As long as the Defending Pokémon's remaining HP is 60 or less, this attack's base damage is 60 instead of 30.

attacks.name: Hasty Headbutt
attacks.text: Rampardos does 20 damage to itself. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its powerful head butt has enough power to shatter even the most durable things upon impact.

name: Nuzleaf
flavorText: The sound of its grass flute makes its listeners uneasy. It lives deep in forests.

abilities.name: Delta Protection
abilities.text: Any damage done to Camerupt by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that has δ on its card is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Bagon
flavorText: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

attacks.name: Back Burner
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Darkrai

attacks.name: Darkness Roar
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 or his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sleep Hole
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage. If the Defending Pokémon is not Asleep, that Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

abilities.name: Reactive Barrier
abilities.text: As long as Mew has any React Energy cards attached to it, prevent all effects, excluding damage, done to Mew by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Celebi

abilities.name: Time Travel
abilities.text: If an opponent's attack would Knock Out Celebi, flip a coin. If heads, Celebi isn't Knocked Out and you shuffle it and all cards attached to it into your deck. This power doesn't work if Celebi is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Psychic Damage
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, put 1 damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon wanders across time. Grass and trees flourish in the forests in which it has appeared.

name: Paras
flavorText: Whether it's due to a lack of moisture or a lack of nutrients, in Alola the mushrooms on Paras don't grow up quite right.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 50 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: While young, they live together deep in the mountains, training themselves in how to fight with their scythes and move at high speeds.

name: Manaphy

attacks.name: Deep Sea Swirl
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.

flavorText: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

name: Dark Blastoise

attacks.name: Hydrocannon
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Dark Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack. You can't add more than 40 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Rocket Tackle
attacks.text: Dark Blastoise does 10 damage to itself. Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Dark Blastoise during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: An aggressive Pokémon that tackles anything it sees.

name: Empoleon

attacks.name: Ice Blade
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Aqua Jet
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

name: Haunter

abilities.name: Transparency
abilities.text: Whenever an attack does anything to Haunter, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of that attack, including damage, done to Haunter. This power stops working while Haunter is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Because of its ability to slip through block walls, it is said to be from another dimension.

name: Dark Espeon

attacks.name: Gouge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Psysplash
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy card attached to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

flavorText: The tips of its split tail quiver whenever it uses its psychic abilities to read its opponent's next move.

name: Victreebel

attacks.name: Pollen Hazard
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Stick and Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

name: Tropius

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It flies by flapping its broad leaves and gives the sweet, delicious fruit around its neck to children.

name: Regigigas

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Double Stomp
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

name: Gardevoir δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Psychic Metal type.

abilities.name: Energy Jump
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move an Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Gardevoir is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psychic Rage
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on Gardevoir to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Black Magic
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 20 more damage times the number of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

name: Nidoking

abilities.name: Pheromone Stamina
abilities.text: Nidoking gets +20 HP for each Nidoqueen you have in play.

attacks.name: Venomous Horn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its tail is thick and powerful. If it binds an enemy, it can render the victim helpless quite easily.

attacks.name: Green Dance
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Grass Pokémon, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If you put any Grass Pokémon into your hand, you may switch Bellossom with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Full Bloom
attacks.text: If you have at least 3 Bellossom in play, this attack does 50 damage plus 50 more damage.

name: Ivysaur

attacks.name: Strange Scent
attacks.text: Each player flips a coin. Each player who gets heads chooses a total of 3 damage counters from among his or her Pokémon and removes them. (If the player's Pokémon have fewer total damage counters than that, he or she removes all of them.)

flavorText: A sure sign spring is on its way is when the seed on this Pokémon's back flowers.

name: Cherubi

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

name: Roserade

attacks.name: Paralyze Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is also Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

name: Bellossom

attacks.name: Grass Knot
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Colorless in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: When the heavy rainfall season ends, it is drawn out by warm sunlight to dance in the open.

name: Torterra

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rumble Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Some Pokémon are born on a Torterra's back and spend their entire life there.

name: Dark Houndoom
rules: This Pokémon is both Fire Darkness type.

attacks.name: Shakedown
attacks.text: Choose a card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

attacks.name: Dark Fire
attacks.text: You may discard a Fire Energy or Darkness Energy attached to Dark Houndoom. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Wormadam

attacks.name: Solar Ray
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Cutter
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Scrunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Chansey during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Chansey does 80 damage to itself.

flavorText: A rare and elusive Pokémon that is said to bring happiness to those who manage to catch it.

name: Scyther
flavorText: Its two sharp scythes are more than just weapons. It uses them with dexterity to dress its prey before eating.

name: Charizard

abilities.name: Resolute Flame
abilities.text: This Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each of their Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX in play.

attacks.name: Fiery Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its wings can carry this Pokémon close to an altitude of 4,600 feet. It blows out fire at very high temperatures.

name: Jumpluff

attacks.name: Fluffy Transport
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Solar Step
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of your remaining Prize cards.

flavorText: Even in the fiercest wind, it can control its fluff to make its way to any place in the world it wants.

name: Arcanine

abilities.name: Security Guard
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, all of your Pokémon take 30 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Legends tell of its fighting alongside a general and conquering a whole country.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Cling
attacks.text: After your attack, remove from Sceptile the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon. If Sceptile has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Kakuna

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

attacks.name: Cheer Up
attacks.text: Draw a card. If you have Plusle in play, draw 2 cards instead.

attacks.name: Negative Ion
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Power Bolt
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mend
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Fighting Energy card and attach it to Golem. If you do, remove 2 damage counters from Golem.

attacks.name: Enraged Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on Golem to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Kricketune

attacks.name: White Noise
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Draining Cut
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: There is a village that hosts a contest based on the amazingly variable cries of this Pokémon.

name: Dark Blastoise

attacks.name: Hydrocannon
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Dark Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 40 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Rocket Tackle
attacks.text: Dark Blastoise does 10 damage to itself. Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Dark Blastoise during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: An aggressive Pokémon that tackles anything it sees.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 Energy cards from your discard pile and attach them to Mewtwo.

flavorText: Years of genetic experiments resulted in the creation of this never-before-seen violent Pokémon.

name: Gallade

attacks.name: Sonic Blade
attacks.text: Put damage counters on the Defending Pokémon until it is 50 HP away from being Knocked Out. If you do, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic Cut
attacks.text: You may choose as many of your face-down Prize cards as you like and put them face up. If you do, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage for each Prize card you chose. (These cards remain face up for the rest of the game.)

flavorText: A master of courtesy and swordsmanship, it fights using extending swords on its elbows.

name: Jumpluff

attacks.name: Mass Attack
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Jumpluff by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Once it catches the wind, it deftly controls its cotton-puff spores—it can even float around the world.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Iron Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.

name: Vileplume-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Fragrant Flower Garden
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Massive Bloom
attacks.text: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Allergic Explosion-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Paralyzed, and Poisoned. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Mothim

attacks.name: Silver Wind
attacks.text: During your next turn, if an attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Raging Scales
attacks.text: If Mothim has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 damage plus 40 more damage.

flavorText: It loves the honey of flowers and steals honey collected by COMBEE.

name: Eldegoss V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Happy Match
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Float Up
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

name: Team Aqua's Manectric
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Darkness type.

abilities.name: Power Shift
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move any number of basic Energy cards attached to 1 of your Pokémon with Team Aqua in its name to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Team Aqua's Manectric is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Thunderspark
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) that has Energy cards attached to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Roselia

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Roselia that drink nutritionally rich springwater are said to reveal rare coloration when they bloom.

name: Jumpluff

abilities.name: Leave It to the Wind
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Blown by the seasonal winds, it circles the globe, scattering cotton spores as it goes.

abilities.name: Happy Healing
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), choose 1 of your Benched Pokémon and flip a coin. If heads, count the number of Energy attached to Blissey and then remove that many damage counters from the chosen Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Blissey is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Smash Bomber
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Starmie.

name: Kabutops

attacks.name: Primal Scythe
attacks.text: You may discard Helix Fossil, Dome Fossil, or Old Amber from your hand. If you do, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.

abilities.name: Psychic Float
abilities.text: If you have any Psychic Energy attached to your Active Pokémon, the Retreat Cost for that Pokémon is 0.

attacks.name: Double Leg Hammer
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Shelgon

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Within its rugged shell, its cells have begun changing. The shell peels off the instant it evolves.

name: Espeon

attacks.name: Solar Suggestion
attacks.text: Move up to 4 damage counters from any of your Pokémon to any of your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It uses the fine hair that covers its body to sense air currents and predict its enemy's actions.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Take Away
attacks.text: Shuffle Erika's Dragonair and all cards attached to it into your deck. Then, your opponent shuffles his or her Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Grip and Squeeze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: One solid blow from its horns is enough to split apart a large tree. Its greatest rival in Alola is Vikavolt.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The sharp scythes on its forearms become increasingly sharp by cutting through hard objects.

name: Treecko

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Small hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Ominous Eyes
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Should a strange light be seen flickering in an abandoned building, Gastly is lurking there.

attacks.name: Summon Storm
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 20 damage to each other Pokémon (even your own). Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack.

attacks.name: Dragon Tornado
attacks.text: Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

name: Oddish

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: During the day, it stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys. (Any cards attached to Deoxys, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Delta Reduction
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Deoxys by attacks in reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Pinsir-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Guillotine-GX
attacks.text: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Spinarak

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

name: Shiftry

abilities.name: Leaf Draw
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Grass Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Deranged Dance
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe's mind and take preemptive action.

name: Clefable

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefable's type is still Colorless.)

attacks.name: Minimize
attacks.text: All damage done by attacks to Clefable during your opponent's next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A timid Fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen. It will run and hide the moment it senses people.

name: Kakuna

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.

flavorText: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

name: Yanma

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

abilities.name: Ancient Shell
abilities.text: As long as you have Omanyte or Omastar in play, damage done to Kabutops by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Kabutops.

attacks.name: Extra Claws
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Luxray

attacks.name: Flash
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Gadget Bolt
attacks.text: If Luxray has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, you may do 100 damage instead of 60 to the Defending Pokémon. If you do, discard that Pokémon Tool card.

flavorText: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

name: Yanmega

attacks.name: Windfall
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Surprise Strike
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It prefers to battle by biting apart foes' heads instantly while flying by at high speed.

name: Ninetales

abilities.name: Roast Reveal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Fire Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards. This power can't be used if Ninetales is affected by a Special Condition.

flavorText: Its nine beautiful tails are filled with a wondrous energy that could keep it alive for 1,000 years.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.

name: Gardevoir

abilities.name: Telepass
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your opponent's discard pile for a Supporter card and use the effect of that card as the effect of this power. (The Supporter card remains in your opponent's discard pile.) You can't use more than 1 Telepass Poké-Power each turn. This power can't be used if Gardevoir is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psychic Lock
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, your opponent can't use any Poké-Powers on his or her Pokémon.

flavorText: It will try to guard its trusted Trainer with its life. It has the ability to see the future.

name: Servine

attacks.name: Wring Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They avoid attacks by sinking into the shadows of thick foliage. They retaliate with masterful whipping techniques.

name: Hitmonlee

attacks.name: Stretch Kick
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When in a hurry, its legs lengthen progressively. It runs smoothly with extra long, loping strides.

name: Dark Dragonite

abilities.name: Summon Minions
abilities.text: When you play Dark Dragonite from your hand, search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Giant Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Sometimes called "The God of Destruction." Its wings are able to support it in spite of its massive weight.

name: Roserade

attacks.name: Inviting Poison
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Flower Tornado
attacks.text: Move any number of Grass Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: With the movements of a dancer, it strikes with whips that are densely lined with poison thorns.

name: Karrablast
flavorText: For some reason they evolve when they receive electrical energy while they are attacking Shelmet.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Recharge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Pikachu. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Pikachu in order to use this attack.

flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, they attract so much electricity that they can cause lightning to strike.

name: Delibird

attacks.name: Present
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It carries food rolled up in its tail. It has the habit of sharing food with people lost in mountains.

name: Raticate

attacks.name: Sharp Teeth
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon sometimes hunts for food in rivers, swimming using the small webs between the toes on its rear legs.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Panic
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Using psychokinesis gives it a headache, so it normally passes the time spacing out and doing as little as possible.

name: Infernape

attacks.name: Meteor Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Flare Blitz
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy attached to Infernape.

flavorText: It uses a special kind of martial arts involving all its limbs. Its fire never goes out.

attacks.name: Cheer Up
attacks.text: Draw a card. If you have Minun in play, draw 2 cards instead.

attacks.name: Positive Ion
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Body Bolt
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Poké-Bodies. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Dark Hypno
rules: This Pokémon is both Psychic Darkness type.

attacks.name: Dark Link
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an attack on 1 of your Pokémon in play that has Dark in its name (excluding this one). Dark Link copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) (No matter what type that Pokémon is, Dark Hypno's type is still Psychic Darkness.) Dark Hypno performs that attack.

attacks.name: Black Magic
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

name: Rotom

abilities.name: Type Shift
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Rotom's type is Psychic until the end of your turn.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Trainer, Supporter, and Stadium card in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Its electric-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

abilities.name: Intimidating Fang
abilities.text: As long as Feraligatr is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Pull Away
attacks.text: If your opponent has 5 of more cards in his or her hand, your opponent discards a number of cards until your opponent has 4 cards left in his or her hand.

attacks.name: Tonnage
attacks.text: You may do 50 damage plus 30 more damage. If you do, Feraligatr does 30 damage to itself.

name: Porygon-Z

abilities.name: Dimension Transfer
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. This power can't be used if Porygon-Z is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Suspicious Beam β
attacks.text: If Porygon-Z has no Rainbow Energy attached to it, Porygon-Z does 20 damage to itself and Porygon-Z is now Confused.

flavorText: Its programming was modified to enable it to travel through alien dimensions. Seems there might have been an error…

name: Crobat

attacks.name: Triggered Poison
attacks.text: If your opponent attaches an Energy card to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn, that Pokémon becomes Poisoned.

attacks.name: Cross Attack
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. If you get 2 or more heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It flies so silently through the dark on its four wings that it may not be noticed even when nearby.

name: Petilil

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Although the leaves on its head are bitter enough to cause dizziness, they provide relief from weariness—even more so when boiled.

name: Team Aqua's Sharpedo
rules: This Pokémon is both Water Darkness type.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Team Aqua's Sharpedo.

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.text: Team Aqua's Sharpedo can't attack during your next turn.

name: Charizard

abilities.name: Energy Burn
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may turn all Energy attached to Charizard into Fire Energy for the rest of the turn. This power can't be used if Charizard is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard in order to use this attack.

flavorText: Spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. Known to unintentionally cause forest fires.

abilities.name: Dragon Intimidation
abilities.text: If Garchomp is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Garchomp is Knocked Out), choose an Energy card attached to the Attacking Pokémon and put it into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Guard Claw
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Garchomp by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Speed Impact
attacks.text: Does 120 damage minus 20 damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Deafen
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Trainer cards or Stadium cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Second Strike
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Tropical Wind
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 2 damage counters from each Active Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if a Pokémon has only 1). If tails, each Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

abilities.name: Energy Trans
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), move a Grass Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Sceptile is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

name: Dark Feraligatr

abilities.name: Scare
abilities.text: As long as Dark Feraligatr is your Active Pokémon, all of your opponent's Baby Pokémon Powers stop working and your opponent's Baby Pokémon can't attack. This power stops working while Dark Feraligatr is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Crushing Blow
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those cards and discard it.

flavorText: Its powerful muscles allow it to move at a speed that belies its bulk.

attacks.name: Copy
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Copy copies that attack. This attack does nothing if Mew doesn't have the Energy necessary to use that attack. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Mew performs that attack.

attacks.name: Extra Draw
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon-ex in play, search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to Mew. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Dark Charizard

attacks.name: Continuous Fireball
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Fire Energy cards attached to Dark Charizard. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads. Discard a number of Fire Energy cards attached to Dark Charizard equal to the number of heads.

flavorText: Seemingly possessed, it spews fire like a volcano, trying to burn all it sees.

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Ambush Bite
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its tentacles to lash itself to trees, it lies in wait for prey, luring it close with sweet-smelling drool.

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Close examination of its large eyes reveals that each eye is composed of a myriad of tiny eyes.

abilities.name: Super Dynamo
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Electrode is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, choose a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Electrode is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mud Splash
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Mothim

abilities.name: Wormadam First
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Wormadam to another of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It flutters around at night and steals honey from the Combee hive.

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Swift Sting
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has full HP, this attack does 40 more damage, and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: Its best attack involves flying around at high speed, striking with poison needles, then flying off.

attacks.name: Aqua Sonic
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Yanma

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It can hover in one spot by flapping its wings at high speed. It flits about to guard its territory.

attacks.name: Psychic Boom
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mind Trip
attacks.text: If Grumpig and the Defending Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Dark Gengar

abilities.name: Deep Sleep
abilities.text: As long as any Dark Gengar are in play, a player flips 2 coins for each of his or her Pokémon that is Asleep at the end of each turn. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep. This power stops working if Dark Gengar is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Pull In
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, you may choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon (before doing damage or other effects of this attack). Either way, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It absorbs the heat in the air around it. If you suddenly feel cold, it's because a Gengar has appeared.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Jump Kick
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It slams foes by sharply uncoiling its rolled ears. It stings enough to make a grown-up cry in pain.

attacks.name: Thrash Tackle
attacks.text: Gyarados does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Fire Dance
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Multi Burn
attacks.text: If Flareon has 3 or more different types of basic Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

abilities.name: Pure Body
abilities.text: To attach a Fire Energy card from your hand to Entei, you must discard an Energy card attached to Entei. (Attach the Fire Energy, and then discard an Energy from Entei.)

attacks.name: Burning Fang
attacks.text: You may flip a coin. If heads, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Entei and the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Dark Dragonite

abilities.name: Summon Minions
abilities.text: When you play Dark Dragonite from your hand, search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Giant Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Sometimes called "The God of Destruction." Its wings are able to support it in spite of its massive weight.

abilities.name: Fortitude
abilities.text: If Giovanni's Machamp would be Knocked Out by an opponent's attack, flip a coin. If heads, Giovanni's Machamp is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP become 10 instead. This power can't be used if Giovanni's Machamp is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Hurricane Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Mismagius

abilities.name: Magical Trans
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Psychic Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Mismagius is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psychic Pulse
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

name: Forretress

attacks.name: Mirror Shot
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Everyone Explode Now
attacks.text: Does 30 damage times the number of Pineco and Forretress you have in play. This attack does 30 damage to each of your Pineco and Forretress in play.

flavorText: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

name: Salamence

abilities.name: Scornful Storm
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent discard cards from his or her hand until he or she has 4 cards left in his or her hand.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: As a result of its long-held dream of flying, its cellular structure changed, and wings grew out.

name: Gloom

attacks.name: Stinky Scent
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Smells incredibly foul! However, around one out of a thousand people enjoy sniffing its nose-bending stink.

name: Drifloon

attacks.name: Blowing Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to it on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Ominous Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: A Pokémon formed by the spirits of people and Pokémon. It loves damp, humid seasons.

name: Dragonite

abilities.name: Special Delivery
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card. If you do, choose a card from your hand and put it on top of your deck. This power can't be used if Dragonite is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Supersonic Flight
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon can fly is spite of its large bulk. It is said to be able to circumnavigate the earth in just 16 hours.

name: Ledyba

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Ledyba.

flavorText: When it senses enemies approaching, this Pokémon releases an orange fluid from the joints in its legs and flees.

name: Mankey

attacks.name: Three-Step Strike
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It can spontaneously become enraged. Everyone near it clears out as it rampages, and the resulting loneliness makes it angrier still.

abilities.name: Psychic Connect
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Psychic Energy attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Gardevoir is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Energy Burst
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Gardevoir and the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic Protection
attacks.text: Gardevoir has no Weakness during your opponent's next turn.

name: Shaymin-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.text: Does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Flaaffy

attacks.name: Attract Current
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Electromagnetic Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Flaaffy does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: If its coat becomes fully charged with electricity, its tail lights up. It fire hair that zaps on impact.

name: Grovyle

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Petal Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: No matter how much time and money is spent raising it, its flowers are the most beautiful when they bloom in the wild.

name: Snivy

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: They photosynthesize by bathing their tails in sunlight. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.

name: Ariados

abilities.name: Poisonous Nest
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this Ability. Both Active Pokémon (except for Grass Pokémon) are now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Impound
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.

name: Jolteon δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Metal type.

attacks.name: Delta Search
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Holon Energy card and attach it to Jolteon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Return Spark
attacks.text: You may return an Energy card attached to Jolteon to your hand. If you do, this attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Torchic

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Exeggcute

abilities.name: Propagation
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Beautifly.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Support Navigation
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Lapras onto your Bench from your hand, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Cottonee
flavorText: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

name: Yanmega

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its jaw power is incredible. It is adept at biting apart foes while flying by at high speed.

name: Dark Persian

attacks.name: Fascinate
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Popular with women because of its beautiful fur. The leader of the Rockets keeps one as a pet.

name: Combee

attacks.name: Sting Missile
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a set of three. When they sleep, they gather up and form a giant hive of 100 Combee.

name: Pachirisu

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Smash Short
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage. Discard that Pokémon Tool card, look at your opponent's hand, and discard any Pokémon Tool cards of the same name you find there.

flavorText: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Flash Needle
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. If all of them are heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has three poisonous stingers on its forelegs and its tail. They are used to jab its enemy repeatedly.

name: Mothim

attacks.name: Cloak Party
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Burmy and Wormadam, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: While it loves floral honey, it won't gather any itself. Instead, it plots to steal some from Combee.

name: Hypno

attacks.name: Prophecy
attacks.text: Look at up to 3 cards from the top of either player's deck and rearrange them as you like.

attacks.name: Dark Mind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

name: Gastrodon East Sea

abilities.name: Osmotic Pressure
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Gastrodon West Sea in play, you may move up to 3 damage counters from Gastrodon East Sea to 1 of your Gastrodon West Sea.

attacks.name: Dwindling Wave
attacks.text: Does 80 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Gastrodon East Sea.

flavorText: It has a pliable body without any bones. If any part of its body is torn off, it grows right back.

name: Lucario

attacks.name: Feint
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Aura Sphere
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has the ability to sense the auras of all things. It understands human speech.

name: Paras
flavorText: No matter how much it eats, the mushrooms growing on its back steal away most of the nutrients it consumes.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tail Shock
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Tangrowth

attacks.name: Hundred Furious Lashes
attacks.text: Does 30 damage times the amount of Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. This Pokémon can't use Hundred Furious Lashes during your next turn.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Even if one of its arms is eaten, it's fine. The Pokémon regenerates quickly and will go right back to normal.

name: Charmander
flavorText: The flame on its tail indicates Charmander's life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefairy's type is still Colorless.)

flavorText: Its magical and cute appeal has many admirers. It is rare and found only in certain areas.

abilities.name: Psychic Trace
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Claydol is your Active Pokémon, you may shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand. This power can't be used if Claydol is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Ancient Mantra
attacks.text: If Claydol has any Psychic Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If Claydol has any Fighting Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Shelmet

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When it and Karrablast are together, and both receive electrical stimulation, they both evolve.

name: Beedrill

abilities.name: Elusive Master
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is the last card in your hand, you may play it onto your Bench. If you do, draw 3 cards.

flavorText: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

name: Yanmega

abilities.name: Sonic Vision
abilities.text: If you have exactly 4 cards in your hand, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon's attacks.

attacks.name: Assault Boom
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Lure Poison
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Spit Squall
attacks.text: Your opponent puts the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her hand.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees on marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

name: Turtwig

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Hold Still
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: In the distant past, they were fairly strong, but they have become gradually weaker over time.

name: Noctowl

abilities.name: Night Sight
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card. This power can't be used if Noctowl is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.

flavorText: Its eyes are specially adapted. They concentrate even faint light and enable it to see in the dark.

name: Sewaddle

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth.

name: Delibird

attacks.name: Present
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If exactly 1 is heads, this attack does 40 damage. If exactly 2 are heads, remove 3 damage counters from the Defending Pokémon. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them. If all 3 are heads, this attack does 60 damage. If all 3 are tails, remove all damage counters from the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefairy's type is still Colorless.)

flavorText: Its magical and cute appeal has many admirers. It is rare and found only in certain areas.

abilities.name: Evolutionary Flame
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Magmortar from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this power. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Fire Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Flame Ball
attacks.text: You may move a Fire Energy card attached to Magmortar to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Vaporeon but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 40 damage in this way.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.

name: Rapidash

abilities.name: Fiery Spirit
abilities.text: Rapidash can't be Confused.

attacks.name: Ring of Fire
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: At full gallop, its four hooves barely touch the ground because it moves so incredibly fast.

name: Team Aqua's Walrein
rules: This Pokémon is both Water Darkness type.

attacks.name: Power Blow
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Team Aqua's Walrein.

attacks.name: Hydro Reverse
attacks.text: You may return any number of basic Water Energy cards attached to all of your Pokémon to your hand. If you do, this attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy you returned.

abilities.name: Energy Jump
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move an Energy card from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Alakazam is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

abilities.name: Flare Condition
abilities.text: As long as Arcanine has any Fire Energy attached to it, Arcanine has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Burn Out
attacks.text: You may do 30 damage plus 30 more damage. If you do, Arcanine is now Burned.

attacks.name: Flames of Rage
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Arcanine. This attack does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Arcanine.

name: Paras

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Burrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Loathe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Even though it appears to be eggs of some sort, it was discovered to be a life-form more like plant seeds.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Multiply
attacks.text: Search your deck for Exeggcute and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Six of them together form a full-fledged Pokémon. It's often hunted by Crabrawler, but uses psychokinesis to drive it off.

name: Charizard δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Metal type.

abilities.name: Peal of Thunder
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Charizard from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose as many Energy cards as you like, and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Discard the other cards.

attacks.name: Metal Burn
attacks.text: Discard all Metal Energy attached to Charizard.

name: Wurmple

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: Often targeted by bird Pokémon, it desperately resists by releasing poison from its tail spikes.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

abilities.name: Let Loose
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Giratina from your hand onto your Bench, you may use this power. Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws up to 4 cards. (You draw your cards first.)

attacks.name: Earth Power
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Tangrowth

attacks.name: Grass Knot
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.

abilities.name: Pollen Defense
abilities.text: If an attack does damage to Erika's Vileplume while it's your Active Pokémon (even if it's Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. This power works even while Erika's Vileplume is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: If Erika's Vileplume does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove a number of damage counters from Erika's Vileplume equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Erika's Vileplume has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Flames of Rage
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy cards attached to Arcanine in order to use this attack. This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Arcanine.

flavorText: A legendary Pokémon famous for its beauty. It looks almost as if it flies when it runs.

name: Victini

attacks.name: Quick Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being's entire body will be overflowing with power.

name: Yanmega BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Yanmega BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Barrier Break
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Derail
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy card, if any, attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swift Blow
attacks.text: Does 60 damage plus 20 damage for each React Energy card attached to Machamp.

name: Parasect

attacks.name: Colorful Spores
attacks.text: Choose 3 of your Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a different type of basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.

name: Porygon-Z

abilities.name: Conversion
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a basic Energy card from your hand. Porygon-Z is the same type as that Energy Card until the end of your turn. This power can't be used if Porygon-Z is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Tri Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Additional software was installed to make it a better Pokémon. It began acting oddly, however.

name: Combusken

abilities.name: Natural Cure
abilities.text: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, remove all Special Conditions from it.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: During a battle, the hot flame in its body increases. Its kicks have outstanding destructive power.

name: Chikorita

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.

name: Alolan Exeggutor

attacks.name: Tropical Shake
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each type of basic Energy card in your discard pile. You can't add more than 100 damage in this way.

flavorText: As it grew taller and taller, it outgrew its reliance on psychic powers, while within it awakened the power of the sleeping dragon.

name: Whimsicott

attacks.name: Cotton Ride
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.

flavorText: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it'll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

abilities.name: Chain of Events
abilities.text: As long as Minun is your Active Pokémon, whenever your other Active Pokémon, if any, attacks, you may use Cheer On after the first attack (you still need the necessary Energy to use Cheer On). You can't use Cheer On more than once in this way even if your other Active Pokémon has the Chain of Events Poké-Body.

attacks.name: Cheer On
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon (including Minun).

attacks.name: Special Circuit
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. If you choose Pokémon that has a Poké-Power or Poké-Body, this attack does 40 damage instead. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Servine

attacks.name: Wring Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They avoid attacks by sinking into the shadows of thick foliage. They retaliate with masterful whipping techniques.

name: Sceptile

abilities.name: Nurture and Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.

flavorText: It agilely leaps about the jungle and uses the sharp leaves on its arms to strike its prey.

name: Ledyba
flavorText: When the weather turns cold, lots of Ledyba gather from everywhere to cluster and keep each other warm.

name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Blockade
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Supporter cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It is called "The Walking Jungle." If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an Exeggcute.

name: Quagsire

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Due to its relaxed and carefree attitude, it often bumps its head on boulders and boat hulls as it swims.

name: Pansage
flavorText: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. Eating a leaf from its head whisks weariness away as if by magic.

name: Vespiquen

attacks.name: Gather Order
attacks.text: Search your deck for as many Combee as you like and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Damage Beat
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding.

name: Charmeleon
flavorText: It lashes about with its tail to knock down its foe. It then tears up the fallen opponent with sharp claws.

name: Parasect

abilities.name: Panic Spores
abilities.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Confused Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Mysterious Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

name: Dark Marowak
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Darkness type.

attacks.name: Brick Smash
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hard Bone
attacks.text: Discard a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from your hand or this attack does nothing.

abilities.name: Energy Recharge
abilities.text: When you play Arcanine from your hand to evolve your Active Pokémon, you may flip 3 coins. For each heads, choose a basic Energy card from your discard pile and attach it to Arcanine.

attacks.name: Shake
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

attacks.name: White Flames
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy cards attached to Arcanine.

name: Vileplume

abilities.name: Disgusting Pollen
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Basic Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Downer Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys. (Any cards attached to Deoxys, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Crystal Laser
attacks.text: During your next turn, Deoxys's attacks do 40 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Serperior

attacks.name: Leaf Storm
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from each of your Grass Pokémon.

flavorText: They raise their heads to intimidate opponents but only give it their all when fighting a powerful opponent.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Tempting Eyes
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: After lulling its enemies with its large eyes, this Pokémon sings them to sleep with a relaxing melody.

name: Buizel

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Super Fast
attacks.text: If you have Pachirisu in play, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Buizel during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It swims by rotating its two tails like a screw. When it dives, its flotation sac collapses.

name: Latias

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sky Heal
attacks.text: If Latios is on your Bench, heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is covered in a down that can refract light in such a way that it becomes invisible.

name: Exeggcute
flavorText: Six of them form a single Pokémon. Should one of the six be lost, the next morning there will once more be six.

name: Roselia

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It uses the different poisons in each hand separately when it attacks. The stronger its aroma, the healthier it is.

name: Accelgor

attacks.name: Retribution
