attacks.text: If an Escavalier you had in play was Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, put all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Signal Beam
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: When its body dries out, it weakens. So it wraps a membrane around itself for protection while it spits poison.

name: Phantump

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: These Pokémon are stumps possessed by the spirits of children who died in the forest. Their cries sound like eerie screams.

attacks.name: Super Hypno Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Spiky Shell
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon at the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Pop
attacks.text: Put 7 damage counters on Forretress. Move all Energy cards attached to Forretress to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. (Ignore this effect if you don't have any Benched Pokémon.)

name: Onix

attacks.name: Screech
attacks.text: During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 20 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It usually lives underground. It searches for food while boring its way through the ground at 50 miles per hour.

abilities.name: Energy Return
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), choose an Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and return it to your hand. This power can't be used if Espeon is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Damage Blast
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

name: Team Magma's Aggron
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Darkness type.

attacks.name: Crush
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to Team Magma's Aggron. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Land Stream
attacks.text: You may discard any number of basic Energy cards attached to Team Magma's Aggron. If you do, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each basic Energy card you discarded.

name: Sceptile-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Unseen Claw
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Bounce Back
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Smack Attack
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Bastiodon GL.

name: Dark Dugtrio

abilities.name: Sinkhole
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent's Active Pokémon retreats, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this power does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.) This power stops working while Dark Dugtrio is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Knock Down
attacks.text: Your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if heads, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: Digs large sinkholes in the ground and tries to trap its enemies in them.

name: Latias δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Metal type.

abilities.name: Delta Aura
abilities.text: If you have Latios or Latios ex in play, the attack cost of Latias's Extra Crush is now Lightning MetalColorless.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters fro Latias.

attacks.name: Extra Crush
attacks.text: If your opponent has no Pokémon-ex in play, this attack does nothing.

name: Scyther
flavorText: The sharp scythes on its forearms become increasingly sharp by cutting through hard objects.

name: Gastrodon West Sea

attacks.name: Raging Flood
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Gastrodon West Sea. Then, remove 2 damage counters from Gastrodon West Sea.

attacks.name: Wild Waves
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: It has a pliable body without any bones. If any part of its body is torn off, it grows right back.

attacks.name: Intimidate
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon's maximum HP is 50 or less, it can't attack Giovanni's Nidoking during your opponent's next turn. (Benching or evolving either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage; if tails, this attack does 40 damage.

name: Entei

abilities.name: Howl
abilities.text: When you play Entei from your hand, you may discard the top 5 cards of your deck. (If you have fewer cards in your deck than that, discard all of them.) If any of those are Fire Energy cards, attach them to any of your Fire Pokémon of your choice. Using this power ends your turn.

attacks.name: Searing Flames
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy cards attached to Entei or this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Volcanoes erupt when it barks. Unable to restrain its extreme power, it races headlong around the land.

name: Solrock

abilities.name: Heal Block
abilities.text: If you have Lunatone in play, damage counters can't be removed from any Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Damage counters can still be moved.)

attacks.name: Sun Flash
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

name: Groudon

attacks.name: Volcano Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 4 cards of your opponent's deck. If tails, discard the top 4 cards of your deck.

flavorText: Said to have expanded the lands by evaporating water with raging heat. It battled titanically with Kyogre.

name: Greninja

abilities.name: Evasion Jutsu
abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Furious Shurikens
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Shake It Off
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Colorless Pokémon your opponent has in play.

flavorText: It is called "The Walking Jungle." If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an Exeggcute.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

abilities.name: Dark Protection
abilities.text: Any damage done to Crawdaunt by your opponent's attacks is reduced by 10 for each Darkness Energy attached to Crawdaunt (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't reduce more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Gible
rules: Oran Berry: Remove 1 damage counter from Gible at the end of your turn.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

abilities.name: Competitiveness
abilities.text: If you don't have a Supporter card in play, each of Probopass's attacks does 30 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Top Chop
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Snivy

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: They photosynthesize by bathing their tails in sunlight. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.

name: Cranidos

attacks.name: Steamroll
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its skull is as hard as iron.

name: Dark Houndoom

attacks.name: Eerie Howl
attacks.text: If your opponent's Bench isn't full, look at his or her hand. If your opponent has any Baby Pokémon or Basic Pokémon there, choose 1 of them and put it on his or her Bench. Then, switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Fire
attacks.text: If there are any Darkness Energy cards attached to Dark Houndoom, discard 1 of them and this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage (plus 10 more damage for the Darkness Energy you discarded). If there aren't any, this attack does 30 damage.

flavorText: The flame it breathes is actually a volatile poison it produces internally that ignites when exposed to air.

name: Turtwig
flavorText: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

name: Parasect

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 60 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 120 damage.

attacks.name: Mushroom Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The large mushroom on its back controls it. It often fights over territory with Shiinotic.

name: Double Rainbow Energy
rules: Double Rainbow Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex). While in play, Double Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides 2 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.) Damage done to your opponent's Pokémon by the Pokémon Double Rainbow Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance). When the Pokémon Double Rainbow Energy is attached to is no longer an Evolved Pokémon, discard Double Rainbow Energy.

abilities.name: Primal Veil
abilities.text: As long as Armaldo is your Active Pokémon, each player can't play any Supporter Cards.

name: Bellsprout

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its bud looks like a human face. Because of the bud, it is rumored to be a type of legendary mandrake plant.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Link Needle
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage for each Beedrill (excluding this one) you have in play.

name: Bellossom

abilities.name: Hustle Step
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Bellossom is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Dance 'til Dawn
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. Bellossom is now Asleep.

flavorText: Bellossom gather at times and seem to dance. They say that the dance is a ritual to summon the sun.

name: Aggron

attacks.name: Heap Up
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for all Energy cards and show them to your opponent. If you find any Metal Special Energy cards there this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage. Put all of those Energy cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hard Metal
attacks.text: You may do 60 damage plus 40 more damage. If you do, Aggron does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: While seeking iron for food, it digs tunnels by breaking through bedrock with its steel horns.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Basic Energy cards attached to Entei or this attack does nothing.

name: Pansage

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. Eating a leaf from its head whisks weariness away as if by magic.

name: Snivy

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Twin Play
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Scyther and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: While young, they live together deep in the mountains, training themselves in how to fight with their scythes and move at high speeds.

name: Weepinbell

attacks.name: Growth
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Razor Leaf
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It spits out Poison Powder to immobilize the enemy and then finishes it with a spray of Acid.

name: Incineroar V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Grand Flame
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flare Blitzer
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Team Magma's Claydol
rules: This Pokémon is both Psychic Darkness type.

abilities.name: Magma Switch
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move an Energy card attached to your Pokémon with Team Magma in its name to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Team Magma's Claydol is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Dark Hand
attacks.text: If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Dark Raichu

attacks.name: Surprise Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, flip another coin. If the second coins is heads, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If the second coin is tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Stores up electricity in its body, then suddenly releases it to surprise and shock everyone.

name: Alolan Meowth

attacks.name: Spoil the Fun
attacks.text: If you go second, this attack does 60 more damage during your first turn.

flavorText: When its delicate pride is wounded, or when the gold coin on its forehead is dirtied, it flies into a hysterical rage.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Iron Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to Raichu.

flavorText: If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.

name: Rowlet
flavorText: This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

name: Kabutops

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Remove a number of damage counters from Kabutops equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Kabutops has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: Its sleek shape is perfect for swimming. It slashes prey with its claws and drains the body fluids.

name: Blaziken

abilities.name: Firestarter
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Stream
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.

abilities.name: Sooth Dust
abilities.text: As long as Butterfree is your Active Pokémon, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Serperior

abilities.name: Royal Heal
abilities.text: At any time between turns, heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Tornado
attacks.text: Move as many Grass Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: They raise their heads to intimidate opponents but only give it their all when fighting a powerful opponent.

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Butterfree. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

attacks.name: Rainbow Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The poison scales on its wings are waterproof, allowing this Pokémon to be active even on rainy days.

abilities.name: Beating Wings
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Pidgeot is your Active Pokémon, you may shuffle 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. This power can't be used if Pidgeot is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Sharp Beak
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Simisage

attacks.name: Fire's Power
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

name: Dark Octillery
rules: This Pokémon is both Water Darkness type.

attacks.name: Black Suction Cups
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Defending Pokémon. Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ink Blast
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Dark Octillery but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

name: Detective Pikachu

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: He loves to show off his vast knowledge. This expressive Pikachu is like a middle-aged man.

name: Swadloon

attacks.name: Protect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

name: Hitmonchan
flavorText: While seeming to do nothing, it fires punches in lightning-fast volleys that are impossible to see.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The aura that emanates from its body intensifies to alert others if it is afraid or sad.

name: Octillery

attacks.name: Switch Cannon
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch Octillery with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ink Bomb
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It traps foes with the suction cups on its tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Overhead Throw
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Heavy Suplex
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It swings its long antlers wildly to attack. During cold periods, it hides deep in forests.

name: Dark Golbat

abilities.name: Sneak Attack
abilities.text: When you play Dark Golbat from your hand, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If you do, Dark Golbat does 10 damage to that Pokémon. Apply Weakness and Resistance.

attacks.name: Flitter
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: Sneaks up silently on its prey and attacks it from behind, sometimes killing it with one bite.

abilities.name: Type Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Mew is the same type as that Pokémon (all if that Pokémon is more than 1 type) until the end of your turn. This power can't be used if Mew is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.text: If Mew and the Defending Pokémon have a different amount of Energy attached to them, this attack's base damage is 20 instead of 50.

name: Roserade

attacks.name: Whiplash
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Luring prey with a sweet scent, it uses poison whips on its arms to poison, bind, and finish off the prey.

name: Heracross

attacks.name: Giga Horn
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: No matter how heavy its opponents, it flings them far away with its prized horn.

name: Chikorita

attacks.name: Mini Drain
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Take up to 2 Lightning Energy cards from your discard pile and attach them to Lt. Surge's Electabuzz.

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Lt. Surge's Electabuzz in order to use this attack. Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Lightning Energy cards you discarded. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Alakazam

abilities.name: Power Cancel
abilities.text: Once during your opponent's turn, when your opponent's Pokémon uses any Poké-Power, you may discard 2 cards from your hand and prevent all effects of that Poké-Power. (This counts as that Pokémon using its Poké-Power.) This power can't be used if Alakazam is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psychic Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Alakazam by attacks from your opponent's Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its superb memory lets it recall everything it has experienced from birth. Its IQ exceeds 5,000.

name: Latios δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Metal type.

abilities.name: Delta Aura
abilities.text: If you have Latias or Latias ex in play, the attack cost of Latios's Psychic Force is now Lightning MetalColorless.

attacks.name: Psychic Force
attacks.text: If your opponent has no Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon in play, this attack does nothing.

name: Dark Porygon2

abilities.name: Spatial Distortion
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, choose a Stadium card from your discard pile and put it into play. (If there's already a Stadium card in play, discard it.) This power can't be used if Dark Porygon2 is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Curve Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done by attacks to Dark Porygon2 during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: Advances in technology have allowed it to evolve. It occasionally displays behavior not found in its programming.

name: Charizard-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flare Blitz-GX
attacks.text: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Poison Payback
abilities.text: If Cacturne is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Cacturne is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies or any other effects on that Pokémon.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Bullet Seed
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Six of them form a single Pokémon. Should one of the six be lost, the next morning there will once more be six.

name: Sunkern

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It may plummet from the sky. If attacked by a Spearow, it will violently shake its leaves.

name: Ledian

attacks.name: Mach Punch
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When the stars flicker in the night sky, it flutters about, scattering a glowing powder.

attacks.name: Volt Tackle
attacks.text: Pikachu does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Grovyle.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Combusken.

name: Simisage

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Stadium Drain
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

attacks.name: Dragon Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, if any of your current Active Pokémon does damage to any Defending Pokémon, the attack does 30 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

abilities.name: Eerie Aura
abilities.text: Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon that remains Asleep between turns.

attacks.name: Darkness Sleep
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, that Pokémon is now Asleep.

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys. (Any cards attached to Deoxys, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.text: Switch Deoxys with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Head Crack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

flavorText: Each of its three heads has its own thoughts. When they want to go in different directions, Exeggutor becomes unable to move.

abilities.name: Lonesome
abilities.text: As long as you have less Pokémon in play than your opponent, your opponent can't play any Trainer cards (except for Supporter cards) from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Houndoom.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Roof Fling
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put your opponent's Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your opponent's hand.

flavorText: One solid blow from its horns is enough to split apart a large tree. Its greatest rival in Alola is Vikavolt.

name: Hitmonchan
flavorText: While seeming to do nothing, it fires punches in lightning-fast volleys that are impossible to see.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Powerful Needles
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: With noises that could be mistaken for the rattles of maracas, it creates an upbeat rhythm, startling bird Pokémon and making them fly off in a hurry.

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.text: Murkrow can't be Asleep.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

name: Silcoon

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.

flavorText: It wraps silk around the branches of a tree. It drinks rainwater on its silk while awaiting evolution.

name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Psychic Research
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Supporter card and use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack. (The Supporter card remains in your discard pile.)

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: It is highly perceptive of its Trainer's feelings. It dances when it is feeling happy.

name: Ho-Oh

abilities.name: Phoenix Turn
abilities.text: Once during your opponent's turn, if Ho-Oh would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, you may flip a coin. If heads, Ho-Oh isn't discarded. Instead, remove all damage counter, Special Conditions, and other effects from Ho-Oh. Then, discard all cards attached to Ho-Oh (except for Energy cards). This counts as Ho-Oh being Knocked Out and your opponent takes a Prize card.

attacks.name: Rainbow Wing
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of different types of basic Energy cards attached to Ho-Oh.

flavorText: Its feathers are in seven colors. It is said that anyone seeing it is promised eternal happiness.

abilities.name: Top Accelerator
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may reveal the top card of your deck. If that card is a basic Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If that card isn't a basic Energy card, discard it. This power can't be used if Salamence is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Shoot Through
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Psychic Removal
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Powerful Hand
attacks.text: Count the number of cards in your hand. Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. You can't put more than 7 damage counters in this way.

abilities.name: Call the Boss
abilities.text: When you play Giovanni's Persian from your hand, you may search your deck for the Trainer card named Giovanni, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

name: Latios

attacks.name: Sky Blade
attacks.text: If Latias is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A highly intelligent Pokémon. By folding back its wings in flight, it can overtake jet planes.

attacks.name: Over Slash
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon).

attacks.name: Dark Wing Flaps
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Shadow Steal
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 20 more damage for each Special Energy card in your opponent's discard pile.

attacks.name: Darkness Chant
attacks.text: Count the number of Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards in your discard pile. Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. You can't put more than 6 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon in this way.

abilities.name: Water Immunity
abilities.text: You can't attach Water Energy cards from your hand to Articuno.

attacks.name: Freeze Solid
attacks.text: If there are any Water Energy cards in your discard pile, flip a coin. If heads, attach 1 of them to Articuno.

attacks.name: Ice Cyclone
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your Pokémon in play. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Super Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Discard any number of Energy cards attached to any of your Pokémon. Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy cards discarded this way. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Called Shot
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage times the amount of Grass Energy attached to Exeggutor. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

name: M Sceptile-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ancientTrait.name: θ Stop
ancientTrait.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jagged Saber
attacks.text: You may attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your hand to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, heal all damage from that Pokémon.

name: Seedot

attacks.name: Bide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, if this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's next turn, it is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

flavorText: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by your opponent's attacks.

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Dugtrio does 10 damage to itself.

name: Wurmple

attacks.name: Flock
attacks.text: Search your deck for Wurmple and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.

name: Serperior

abilities.name: Royal Heal
abilities.text: At any time between turns, heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Tornado
attacks.text: Move as many Grass Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: They raise their heads to intimidate opponents but only give it their all when fighting a powerful opponent.

name: Grotle

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It knows where pure water wells up. It carries fellow Pokémon there on its back.

name: Espeon

attacks.name: Solar Suggestion
attacks.text: Move up to 4 damage counters from any of your Pokémon to any of your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It uses the fine hair that covers its body to sense air currents and predict its enemy's actions.

name: Trevenant

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 30 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

attacks.name: Horn Leech
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is said to devour anyone daring to ravage the forest. To the creatures dwelling in the forest, it offers great kindness.

name: Butterfree V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dizzying Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

name: Rowlet

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

name: Weedle

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

name: Rowlet & Alolan Exeggutor-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Super Growth
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Grass Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. If that Pokémon is now a Stage 1 Pokémon, search your deck for a Stage 2 Pokémon that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Calming Hurricane
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tropical Hour-GX
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), your opponent shuffles all Energy from all of their Pokémon into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Caterpie

abilities.name: Adaptive Evolution
abilities.text: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

flavorText: For protection, it releases a horrible stench from the antenna on its head to drive away enemies.

name: Venusaur-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Jungle Hammer
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

attacks.name: Nimble
attacks.text: If you have Turtwig in play, remove from Croagunk the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it makes an eerie blubbering sound for intimidation.

name: Servine

abilities.name: Serpentine Strangle
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When it gets dirty, its leaves can't be used in photosynthesis, so it always keeps itself clean.

name: Virizion-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Verdant Wind
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Grass Energy attached to it can't be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Emerald Slash
attacks.text: You may search your deck for 2 Grass Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Treecko

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Exciting Shake
attacks.text: During your next turn, flip 6 coins instead of 2 for this Pokémon's Prickly Needles attack.

attacks.name: Prickly Needles
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

abilities.name: Electric Barrier
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon (excluding any Manectric) by attacks.

attacks.name: Power Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Attract Current
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Incite
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Supporter cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.

attacks.name: Giant Wave
attacks.text: Floatzel GL can't use Giant Wave during your next turn.

name: Bayleef

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The buds that ring its neck give off a spicy aroma that perks people up.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Lapras but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A Pokémon that has been overhunted almost to extinction. It can ferry people across water.

attacks.name: Dragon Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Full Retaliation
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on all of your Magikarp.

attacks.name: Pulverize
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has at least 2 damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Raikou does 20 damage to itself.

name: Yanma
flavorText: Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won't miss prey—even those behind it.

name: Leavanny

attacks.name: Coordinate
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon that have no Pokémon Tools attached to them. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Leaf Storm
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from each of your Grass Pokémon.

flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves by using the sticky silk secreted from its mouth.

name: Marowak δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Metal type.

attacks.name: Energy Bone
attacks.text: Choose a number of your opponent's Pokémon up to the amount of Energy attached to Marowak. This attack does 20 damage to each of them.

attacks.name: Metal Crusher
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Metal Pokémon, this attack's base damage is 90.

abilities.name: Venom Spray
abilities.text: When you play Beedrill from your hand to evolve your Active Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Needle Rush
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. If you get 1 heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage. If you get 2 heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage. If you get 3 heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 50 more damage. If you get 4 heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 90 more damage.

name: Torterra

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.

name: Team Magma's Groudon
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Darkness type.

abilities.name: Power Saver
abilities.text: As long as the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) that has Team Magma in its name is 3 or less, Team Magma's Groudon can't attack.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Pulverize
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has at least 2 damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Victini ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

attacks.name: Infinity
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each basic Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.

flavorText: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being's entire body will be overflowing with power.

attacks.name: Repeating Drill
attacks.text: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Victreebel

abilities.name: Fragrance Trap
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Corrosive Acid
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Once ingested into this Pokémon's body, even the hardest object will melt into nothing.

abilities.name: Safeguard
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Dewgong by your opponent's Pokémon-ex.

attacks.name: Cold Breath
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Volbeat

attacks.name: Luring Glow
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Signal Beam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It communicates with others by lighting up its rear at night. It loves Illumise's sweet aroma.

name: Foongus

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

abilities.name: Super Suction Cups
abilities.text: As long as Cradily is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Lure Poison
attacks.text: Before using this effect, you may switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon, if any. The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Cradily (remove 1 if there is only 1).

name: Beautifly

attacks.name: Triple Energy
attacks.text: Search your deck for 3 different types of basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Drainpour
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Despite its looks, it is aggressive. It jabs with its long, thin mouth if disturbed while collecting pollen.

name: Politoed

abilities.name: Leap Frog
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose a Water Pokémon on your Bench and switch it with your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Politoed is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Big Chorus
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Water Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Whenever three or more of these get together, they sing in a loud voice that sounds like bellowing.

name: Ho-oh

attacks.name: Stoke
attacks.text: You may search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to Ho-Oh. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Sacred Fire
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

attacks.name: Dive Bomb
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world's skies continuously on its magnificent seven colored wings.

name: Dark Slowking
rules: This Pokémon is both Psychic Darkness type.

abilities.name: Cunning
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top card of your opponent's deck. Then, you may shuffle his or her deck. This power can't be used if Dark Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Litter
attacks.text: You may discard a combination of up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards and Rocket's Secret Machine cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage for each card you discarded.

name: Gabite

attacks.name: Burrow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Gabite by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Distored Wave
attacks.text: Before doing damage, remove 2 damage counters from the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It habitually digs up and hoards gems in its nest. Its loot is constantly targeted by thieves.

name: Butterfree VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Toxbreeze
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

name: Hitmontop

attacks.name: Triple Kick
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Close Combat
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Hitmontop by attacks is increased by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

abilities.name: Primal Pull
abilities.text: As long as Claydol is your Active Pokémon, each player's Evolved Pokémon pays Colorless more Energy to use its attacks.

attacks.name: Muddy Eye
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of basic Energy cards attached to Claydol and the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Nerve Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Hyper Needle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. Either way, you can't use this attack again as long as Koga's Beedrill stays in play (even putting Koga's Beedrill on the Bench won't let you use it again).

name: Combee
flavorText: It collects and delivers honey to its colony. At night, they cluster to form a beehive and sleep.

name: Dark Scizor

attacks.name: Threaten
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Trainer cards there, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.

flavorText: Nothing can withstand the pressure of the vise-like grip of this Pokémon's steel claws.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Houndoom.

attacks.name: Dark Impact
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't use any Poké-Powers until the end of your opponent's next turn.

name: Grovyle
flavorText: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

name: Sunflora

attacks.name: Solar Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, ignore all Energy in the attack costs of Grass Pokémon and Fire Pokémon. (This includes Pokémon that come into play on that turn.)

flavorText: As the hot season approaches, the petals on this Pokémon's face become more vivid and lively.

name: Sewaddle

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

name: Shieldon

attacks.name: Hard Face
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Shieldon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Shield Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon that lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its facial hide is extremely hard.

name: Dark Gyarados

abilities.name: Final Beam
abilities.text: When Dark Gyarados is Knocked Out by an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this power does 20 damage for each Water Energy attached to Dark Gyarados to the Pokémon that Knocked Out Dark Gyarados. Apply Weakness and Resistance. This power doesn't work if Dark Gyarados is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Normally found only in the deep ocean, it has recently been seen in shallow waters as well.

name: Stantler

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Frighten Horn
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon isn't an Evolved Pokémon, that Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Staring at its antlers creates an odd sensation as if one were being drawn into their centers.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Bite Together
attacks.text: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: They lay their eggs deep inside their nests. When attacked by Heatmor, they retaliate using their massive mandibles.

name: Jumpluff

abilities.name: Cotton Spore
abilities.text: Whenever Jumpluff would be damaged by your opponent's attack, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Jumpluff by that attack.

attacks.name: Cottonweed Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. For each heads, this attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Blown by seasonal winds, it circles the globe, scattering cotton spores as it goes.

name: Metapod

abilities.name: Adaptive Evolution
abilities.text: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

flavorText: It is waiting for the moment to evolve. At this stage, it can only harden, so it remains motionless to avoid attack.

name: Sceptile

ancientTrait.name: Ω Barrier
ancientTrait.text: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Power Poison
attacks.text: Discard 1 Energy attached to this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It agilely leaps about the jungle and uses the sharp leaves on its arms to strike its prey.

name: Tepig

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It blows fire through its nose. When it catches a cold, the fire becomes pitch-black smoke instead.

name: Nuzleaf

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.

flavorText: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

abilities.name: Stench
abilities.text: As long as Muk is your Active Pokémon, each player's Pokémon can't use any Poké-Powers.

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Cacnea

attacks.name: Swagger
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It prefers harsh environments such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: First Aid
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Jigglypuff.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Jigglypuff does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: When its huge eyes light up, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

name: Spiritomb

abilities.name: Spooky Whirlpool
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Spiritomb from your hand onto your Bench, you may use this power. Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 6 cards.

attacks.name: Color Tag
attacks.text: Choose Grass Fire Water Lightning Psychic Fighting Darkness Metal or Colorless type. Put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon your opponent has in play of the type you chose.

flavorText: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Pantomime
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch 1 of your face-down Prize cards with the top card of your deck.

attacks.name: Juggling
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its pantomime skills are wonderful. You may become enraptured while watching it, but next thing you know, Mr. Mime has made a real wall.

name: Yanma

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

name: Sizzlipede
flavorText: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

name: Silcoon

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It drinks dew that collects on its silk and waits for evolution. Its hard cocoon repels attacks.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Genesect

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for 2 Grass Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Jet Impact
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

name: Tentacruel

attacks.name: Tentacle Grip
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Water Energy attached to Tentacruel. For each heads, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon attacks its prey with its eighty toxin-producing tentacles.

name: Metagross

abilities.name: Gravitation
abilities.text: Each Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets -20 HP. No more than 20 HP can be reduced by all Gravitation Poké-Bodies.

attacks.name: Geo Impact
attacks.text: If you have a Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that is the same type as the Defending Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Metang combined to form it. With four brains, it has the intelligence of a supercomputer.

name: Alolan Dugtrio

attacks.name: Gold Rush
attacks.text: Discard any number of Metal Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: This Pokémon are cherished in the Alola region, where they are thought to be feminine deities of the land incarnate.

name: Surskit

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: When this Pokémon senses danger, a sweet fluid oozes from the tip of its heads. The taste of it disgusts bird Pokémon.

name: Swalot

attacks.name: Damage Roller
attacks.text: Put damage counters on the Defending Pokémon until the Defending Pokémon has the same remaining HP as Swalot. (If the Defending Pokémon has the same or less remaining HP as Swalot, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Entangle Tackle
attacks.text: You may do 50 damage plus 30 more damage. If you do, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon, and don't apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.

flavorText: It swallows anything whole. It sweats toxic fluids from its follicles to douse foes.

attacks.name: Attract Current
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Multi Pulse
attacks.text: If Jolteon has 3 of more different types of basic Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Tangrowth

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crosswise Whip
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.

name: Wimpod

attacks.name: Scamper Away
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake.

name: M Venusaur-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crisis Vine
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Grip and Squeeze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Guillotine Hug
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: It gets into territorial disputes with Vikavolt. For some reason, it apparently gets along well with Heracross in Alola.

name: Serperior

attacks.name: Coil
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 60 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike during your next turn.

flavorText: It can stop its opponents' movements with just a glare. It takes in solar energy and boosts it internally.

abilities.name: Green Essence
abilities.text: As long as Sceptile is in play, each of your Active Pokémon that has Grass Energy attached to it can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Luxray

abilities.name: Gleam Eyes
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Luxray from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at your opponent's hand. If your opponent's Bench isn't full, choose 1 Basic Pokémon from your opponent's hand, and put it onto his or her Bench. Then, switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lightning Star
attacks.text: Move all Lightning Energy attached to Luxray to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Ignore this effect if you don't have any Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has eyes that can see through anything. It spots and captures prey hiding behind objects.

name: Machamp

abilities.name: Strikes Back
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent's attack damages Machamp (even if Machamp is Knocked Out), this power does 10 damage to the attacking Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.) This power can't be used if Machamp is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed when your opponent attacks.

flavorText: Using its amazing muscles, it throws powerful punches that can knock its victim clear over the horizon.

name: Forretress

attacks.name: Mirror Shot
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Everyone Explode Now
attacks.text: Does 30 damage times the number of Pineco and Forretress you have in play. This attack does 30 damage to each of your Pineco and Forretress in play.

flavorText: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

name: Shuckle

abilities.name: Shell Barricade
abilities.text: As long as Shuckle is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to Shuckle by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The berries it stores in its vase-like shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.

name: Flygon δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Grass Metal type.

abilities.name: Delta Supply
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a basic Energy card or a δ Rainbow Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon that has δ on its card. This power can't be used if Flygon is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Magmar
flavorText: Its entire body is burning. When it breathes, the temperature rises. When it sneezes, flames shoot out!

name: Sewaddle

attacks.name: Leaf Boomerang
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

name: Pansage

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It shares the leaf on its head with weary-looking Pokémon. These leaves are known to relieve stress.

abilities.name: Psymimic
abilities.text: Once during your turn, instead of Alakazam's normal attack, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks. Alakazam copies that attack including its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type that Pokémon is, Alakazam's type is still Psychic.) This power can't be used if Alakazam is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Syncroblast
attacks.text: If Alakazam and the Defending Pokémon don't have the same number of Energy cards attached to them, this attack's base damage is 20 instead of 80.

name: Ampharos

attacks.name: Gigaspark
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The tail's tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

name: Espeon

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses the fine hair that covers its body to sense air currents and predict its enemy's actions.

name: Clefable

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefable's type is still Colorless.)

attacks.name: Minimize
attacks.text: All damage done by attacks to Clefable during your opponent's next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A timid Fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen. It will run and hide the moment it senses people.

name: Pheromosa & Buzzwole-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jet Punch
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Elegant Sole
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Elegant Sole attack's base damage is 60.

attacks.name: Beast Game-GX
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card. If this Pokémon has at least 7 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), take 3 more Prize cards instead. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Chespin
flavorText: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Aggron.

attacks.name: Double Lariat
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.

name: Leafeon

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It basically does not fight. With cells similar to those of plants, it can perform photosynthesis.

name: Bulbasaur
flavorText: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. The plant sprouts and grows with this Pokémon.

name: Heatran

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Basic Energy cards attached to Heatran. (If you can't discard cards, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

attacks.name: Splashing Turn
attacks.text: You may switch Palkia G with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pearl Breath
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Form Variation
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a Basic Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex and Ditto) and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Place Ditto in the discard pile.

attacks.name: Energy Ball
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Ditto but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more then 20 damage in this way.

name: Amoonguss

abilities.name: Sporprise
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. If you do, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magneton does 80 damage to itself.

flavorText: Formed by several Magnemites linked together. It frequently appears when sunspots flare up.

name: Mew

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Devolution Beam
attacks.text: Choose an evolved Pokémon (your own or your opponent's). Return the highest Stage Evolution card on that Pokémon to its player's hand. That Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned, or anything else that might be the result of an attack (just as if you had evolved it).

flavorText: So rare that it is still said to be a mirage by many experts. Only a few people have seen it worldwide.

name: Tangrowth

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Tangrowth by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Swallow Up
attacks.text: Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Tangrowth. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Tangrowth's, this attack does 120 damage instead.

flavorText: Its arms are made of plants that bind themselves to things. They grow back right away if cut.

name: Pansage
flavorText: It shares the leaf on its head with weary-looking Pokémon. These leaves are known to relieve stress.

name: Forretress

abilities.name: Spikes
abilities.text: During your opponent's turn, whenever 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon becomes his or her Active Pokémon, Forretress does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.) This power stops working if Forretress is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with his or her Active Pokémon, then, if you have any Benched Pokémon, you switch 1 of them with your Active Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside the armor is a total mystery.

name: Fennekin
flavorText: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Oshawott

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

abilities.name: Water Cyclone
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Water Energy attached to 1 of your Active Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Swampert is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Spinning Tail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Aqua Sonic
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Sewaddle
flavorText: This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth.

name: Illumise

attacks.name: Pheromotion
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its fragrance attracts a swarm of Volbeat, so they draw over 200 patterns in the night sky.

name: Celebi-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Time Recall
abilities.text: Each of your evolved Pokémon can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

attacks.name: Wind Whisk
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Eevee
flavorText: Current studies show it can evolve into an incredible eight different species of Pokémon.

abilities.name: Mirror Coat
abilities.text: If Suicune is Burned or Poisoned by an opponent's attack (even if Suicune is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now affected by the same Special Conditions (1 if there is only 1).

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Trash Burst
attacks.text: You may discard up to 5 Energy cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card you discarded.

attacks.name: Twister
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both are tails, this attack does nothing. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

abilities.name: Wishing Star
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Jirachi is your Active Pokémon, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. Jirachi and your other Active Pokémon, if any, are now Asleep. This power can't be used if Jirachi is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Metallic Blow
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Poké-Bodies, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Litten
flavorText: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

name: Alolan Exeggutor

attacks.name: Tropical Shake
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each type of basic Energy card in your discard pile. You can't add more than 100 damage in this way.

flavorText: As it grew taller and taller, it outgrew its reliance on psychic powers, while within it awakened the power of the sleeping dragon.

name: Magnezone

abilities.name: Magnetize
abilities.text: If you have any Metal Energy attached to your Active Pokémon, the Retreat Cost for that Pokémon is 0.

attacks.name: Metal Blast
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to Magnezone.

flavorText: It evolved from exposure to a special magnetic field. Three units generate magnetism.

name: Darumaka

attacks.name: Damage Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: When it sleeps, it pulls its limbs into its body and its internal fire goes down to 1,100 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Ninja Fang
attacks.text: If, before Gliscor does damage, the Defending Pokémon has no damage counters on it and is then damaged by this attack (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
