flavorText: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey… Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

name: Lotad
flavorText: It searches about for clean water. If it does not drink water for too long, the leaf on its head wilts.

name: Psyduck
flavorText: Using psychokinesis gives it a headache, so it normally passes the time spacing out and doing as little as possible.

name: Dark Typhlosion

attacks.name: Rushing Flames
attacks.text: You may discard any number of Fire Energy cards attached to your Pokémon. Flip a coin for each Fire Energy card discarded in this way. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When it's in combat, the air around it shimmers and warps from the intense heat it produces.

name: Centiskorch

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

name: Shaymin ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

attacks.name: Flower Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of basic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

name: Wormadam Sandy Cloak

abilities.name: Sandy Cloak
abilities.text: Prevent all effects, excluding damage, done to Wormadam Sandy Cloak.

attacks.name: Push Over
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Fighting Energy attached to Wormadam Sandy Cloak.

flavorText: When BURMY evolved, it cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.

name: Shiftry

attacks.name: Wicked Wind
attacks.text: Until the end of your opponent's next turn, each Stadium or Pokémon Tool card in play has no effect. (This includes cards that come into play on that turn.)

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: By flapping its leafy fan, it can whip up gusts of 100 ft/second that can level houses.

name: Pansage

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It's good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It's kind enough to share them with friends.

attacks.name: Giant Growth
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, Koga's Ditto's maximum HP is now 80 and Koga's Ditto's Pound attack's base damage is 30 instead of 10. (Benching Koga's Ditto ends this effect.)

name: Marill

attacks.name: Squirt
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

flavorText: This Pokémon's fur repels water like a duck's feathers, allowing it to stay dry and fluffy even in water.

name: Vespiquen

attacks.name: Intelligence Gathering
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Bee Revenge
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: Its abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by Combee.

name: Deerling

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokémon's fur. People use it to mark the seasons.

name: Ho-Oh

attacks.name: Scorching Wing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Fire Energy attached to Ho-Oh.

flavorText: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world's skies continuously on its magnificent seven-colored wings.

attacks.name: Flutter Trick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent's hand and choose 1 card. Your opponent discards the card you chose.

attacks.name: Triple Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

name: Team Magma's Houndoom
rules: This Pokémon is both Fire Darkness type.

attacks.name: Roasting Heat
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Burned, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Magma Spurt
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards from your deck. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Fire and Fighting basic Energy cards discarded in this way.

name: Masquerain

attacks.name: Struggle Bug
attacks.text: Move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its wings and antennae don't cope well with moisture. After a rain, it faces sunward to dry off.

name: Cascoon

attacks.name: Tangle Drag
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It never forgets any attack it endured while in the cocoon. After evolution, it seeks payback.

name: Dwebble
flavorText: It makes a hole in a suitable rock. If that rock breaks, the Pokémon remains agitated until it locates a replacement.

name: Yanmega

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It prefers to battle by biting apart foes' heads instantly while flying by at high speed.

name: Karrablast
flavorText: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

name: Gyarados δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Metal type.

abilities.name: Delta Reactor
abilities.text: As long as any Stadium card with Holon in its name is in play, each of your Pokémon that has δ on its card does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Charmeleon does 10 damage to itself.

name: Metagross δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Metal type.

abilities.name: Delta Control
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 4 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the 3 other cards on the bottom of your deck in any order. This power can't be used if Metagross is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Crush and Burn
attacks.text: You may discard as many Energy cards as you like attached to your Pokémon in play. If you do, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card you discarded.

name: Rayquaza

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

name: Cherubi

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

abilities.name: Energy Charge
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), if Lt. Surge's Magneton is your Active Pokémon, you may take 1 Lightning Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and attach it to Lt. Surge's Magneton. This power can't be used if Lt. Surge's Magneton is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mega Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Lt. Surge's Magneton does 20 damage to itself.

name: Charizard-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stoke
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Houndoom

attacks.name: Dark Flame
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Houndoom or this attack does nothing. If there are any Darkness Energy cards in your discard pile, attach 1 of them to Houndoom.

attacks.name: Black Fang
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Darkness Energy attached to Houndoom. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Panic Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

flavorText: In battle, it flaps its wings at great speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

abilities.name: Chain of Events
abilities.text: As long as Plusle is your Active Pokémon, whenever your other Active Pokémon, if any, attacks, you may use Cheer On after the first attack (you still need the necessary Energy to use Cheer On). You can't use Cheer On more than once in this way even if your other Active Pokémon has the Chain of Events Poké-Body.

attacks.name: Cheer On
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon (including Plusle).

attacks.name: Extra Circuit
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. If you choose Pokémon-ex, this attack does 40 damage instead. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Overzealous
abilities.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon-ex in play, each of Ludicolo's attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

name: Torterra LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Torterra. Torterra LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Forest Murmurs
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This power can't be used if Torterra is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Vigorous Dash
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Torterra does 30 damage to itself.

abilities.name: Withering Dust
abilities.text: As long as Beautifly is in play, do not apply Resistance for all Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Parallel Gain
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon, including Beautifly.

name: Ivysaur

attacks.name: Toxic Whip
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: There is a plant bulb on its back. When it absorbs nutrients, the bulb is said to blossom into a large flower.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magneton does 80 damage to itself.

flavorText: Formed by several Magnemites linked together. It frequently appears when sunspots flare up.

name: Dhelmise V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Anchor Anger
attacks.text: If any of your Grass Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Hammer
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Giga Hammer.

name: Sewaddle
flavorText: Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.

name: Lucario

attacks.name: Blocking Punch
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Lucario by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Striking Kick
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon

flavorText: By catching the aura emanating from others, it can read their thoughts and movements.

name: Heal Energy
rules: Heal Energy provides Colorless Energy. When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, remove 1 damage counter and all Special Conditions from that Pokémon. If Heal Energy is attached to Pokémon-ex, Heal Energy has no effect other than providing Energy.

name: Dark Slowbro

abilities.name: Reel In
abilities.text: When you play Dark Slowbro from your hand, choose up to 3 Basic Pokémon and/or Evolution cards from your discard pile and put them into your hand.

attacks.name: Fickle Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It loves nothing better than to be left alone, and so sometimes ends up being manipulated by unscrupulous people.

name: Rotom

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Telebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

name: Lopunny

attacks.name: Jump Kick
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Jazzed
attacks.text: If Lopunny evolved from Buneary during this turn, remove all damage counters from Lopunny.

flavorText: It is very conscious of its looks and never fails to groom its ears. It runs with sprightly jumps.

attacks.name: Sniff Out
attacks.text: Put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Beautifly

abilities.name: Miraculous Scales
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon-EX.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It has an aggressive nature. It stabs prey with its long, narrow mouth to drain the prey's fluids.

name: Electrode

attacks.name: Chain Lightning
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon isn't Colorless, this attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon of the same type as the Defending Pokémon (including your own).

flavorText: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

name: Genesect-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Red Signal
abilities.text: When you attach a Plasma Energy from your hand to this Pokémon, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Megalo Cannon
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Sleep Pendulum
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Hypno is your Active Pokémon, you may make the Defending Pokémon Asleep. This power can't be used if Hypno is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Spiral Aura
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon isn't Knocked Out by the damage from this attack, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch the Defending Pokémon with it.

abilities.name: Energy Connect
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may take a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon and attach it to your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Ampharos is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Lightning Strike
attacks.text: You may discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Ampharos. If you do, this attack's base damage is 80 instead of 40.

name: Sceptile

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Energy Bloom
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.

flavorText: The leaves that grow on its arms can slice down thick trees. It is without peer in jungle combat.

name: Azumarill

attacks.name: Bubble Shower
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: By keeping still and listening intently, it can tell what is in even wild, fast-moving rivers.

name: Paras
flavorText: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

name: Ambipom

attacks.name: Tail Influence
attacks.text: Your opponent flips a coin until he or she gets heads. For each tails, remove an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon and put it on the bottom of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Charity Tail
attacks.text: Before Ambipom does damage, your opponent may discard 2 cards from his or her hand. If he or she does, this attack's base damage is 10 instead of 80.

flavorText: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.

attacks.name: Judgement
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Random Curse
attacks.text: Put a total of 5 damage counters on all Defending Pokémon in any way you like.

abilities.name: Rainbow Float
abilities.text: If any basic Energy card attached to Flygon is the same type as any of your Pokémon, the Retreat Cost for those Pokémon is 0.

attacks.name: Sand Wall
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium card your opponent has in play. If you do, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Flygon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Power Swing
attacks.text: Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each Evolved Pokémon on your Bench.

name: Chikorita

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.

name: Dartrix

attacks.name: Sharp Blade Quill
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A bit of a dandy, it spends its free time preening its wings. Its preoccupation with any dirt on its plumage can leave it unable to battle.

name: Dark Hypno

attacks.name: Bench Manipulation
attacks.text: Your opponent flips a number of coins equal to the number of Pokémon on his or her Bench. This attack does 20 damage times the number of tails. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: Carries a pendulum with it always. Sometimes appears near less-traveled roads and schools at dusk.

name: Caterpie

abilities.name: Pupate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Perhaps because it would like to grow up quickly, it has a voracious appetite, eating a hundred leaves a day.

name: Ledyba

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They are timid and grow uneasy when not in a swarm with others of their kind. The pattern on their backs differs slightly from one to another.

name: Ho-Oh

attacks.name: Sacred Fire
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: A legend says that its body glows in seven colors. A rainbow is said to form behind when it flies.

name: Team Magma's Numel

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: No matter how heavy the cargo, we can transport it to our destination with ease, thanks to Numel.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Twin Play
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Scyther and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: While young, they live together deep in the mountains, training themselves in how to fight with their scythes and move at high speeds.

name: Weedle

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

name: Heracross

attacks.name: Riled Horn
attacks.text: If your opponent has any TAG TEAM Pokémon in play, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It's proud of its thick horn. In Alola, its biggest rival is Vikavolt, which it's always fighting with.

name: Dark Vaporeon

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

flavorText: A Pokémon capable of controlling water, so it is capable of creating giant whirlpools.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Gentle Song
attacks.text: If Lapras has any damage counters on it, remove 2 of them from it (1 if it only has 1). If the Defending Pokémon has any damage counters on it, remove 2 of them from it (1 if it only has 1). The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This gentle Pokémon can understand human language and allows people to ride on its back.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Barrier
attacks.text: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Mewtwo in order to use this attack. During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Mewtwo.

flavorText: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

name: Pokémon Fan Club
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Even Game
attacks.text: Count the number of your opponent's Pokémon. Search your deck for up to that number of Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Stadium Play
attacks.text: If you have a Stadium card in play, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Swing Dance
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card. This power can't be used if Ludicolo is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Healing Steps
attacks.text: You may discard as many cards as you like from your hand. If you do, remove that many damage counters from Ludicolo.

attacks.name: Circular Steps
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's), excluding Ludicolo.

abilities.name: Thick Skin
abilities.text: Roselia can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Speed Growth
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Psychic Exchange
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Draw up to 8 cards.

attacks.name: Big Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

name: Cascoon

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It never forgets any attack it endured while in the cocoon. After evolution, it seeks payback.

name: Snover

attacks.name: Ice Shard
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Fighting Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: In the spring, it grows berries with the texture of frozen treats around its belly.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Blinding Scythe attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its two sharp scythes are more than just weapons. It uses them with dexterity to dress its prey before eating.

name: Luxray

abilities.name: Intimidating Fang
abilities.text: As long as Luxray is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Luxray does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: When its eyes gleam gold, it can spot hiding prey–even those taking shelter behind a wall.

name: Infernape LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Infernape. Infernape LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Burning Head
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Discard the other 2 cards. This power can't be used if Infernape is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Flare Up
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for 8 Fire Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck. (This attack does nothing if you don't have 8 Fire Energy cards in your discard pile.)

name: Rowlet
flavorText: This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

name: Lombre
flavorText: It has a mischievous spirit. If it spots an angler, it will tug on the fishing line to interfere.

name: Swadloon

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

name: Crustle

attacks.name: Heavy Bullet
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It possesses legs of enormous strength, enabling it to carry heavy slabs for many days, even when crossing arid land.

name: Shaymin

abilities.name: Celebration Wind
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Shaymin from your hand onto your Bench, you may move as many Energy cards attached to your Pokémon as you like to any of your other Pokémon.

attacks.name: Energy Bloom
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

name: Manaphy

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Aqua Ring
attacks.text: Switch Manaphy with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Born on a cold seafloor, it will swim great distances to return to its birthplace.

attacks.name: Knockout Needle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 60 more damage. If 1 or both of them are tails, this attack does 30 damage.

name: Axew

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for Fraxure, reveal it, and put it into your hand, Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

name: Simisage

attacks.name: Fire Fling
attacks.text: Put 3 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Hand Fling
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.

flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

name: Foongus

abilities.name: Play Ball
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may put 3 Poké Ball cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It lures Pokémon with its pattern that looks just like a Poké Ball then releases poison spores.

name: Vespiquen

ancientTrait.name: θ Double
ancientTrait.text: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.

attacks.name: Bee Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by Combee.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Reprisal
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: The flame on its tail indicates Charmander's life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Flying Flare
attacks.text: You may do 40 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

abilities.name: Evolutionary Fan
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Shiftry from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may choose 1 of your Evolved Pokémon in play (excluding any Shiftry). Return that Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

attacks.name: Reactive Beating
attacks.text: If Shiftry has any React Energy cards attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Cherrim

abilities.name: Fair-Weather Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has any Grass Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Random Peck
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun's rays with its whole body.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Sonicboom
attacks.text: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magneton does 100 damage to itself.

flavorText: Formed by several Magnemites linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

abilities.name: Iron Skull
abilities.text: Rampardos's attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Head Smash
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon would be Knocked Out by this attack, Rampardos does 40 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Mold Breaker
attacks.text: Any damage done to Rampardos by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance) until the end of your next turn.

attacks.name: Drain Punch
attacks.text: Remove from Regigigas FB a number of damage counters equal to the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rainbow Lariat
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of different types of Pokémon SP you have in play.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Tauros.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Tauros does 10 damage to itself.

name: Celebi

attacks.name: Time Spiral
attacks.text: Devolve 1 of your opponent's evolved Pokémon by removing the highest Stage Evolution card from it. Your opponent shuffles that card into their deck.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: This Pokémon wanders across time. Grass and trees flourish in the forests in which it has appeared.

name: Houndoom

attacks.name: Fire Counterattack
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Fighting Pokémon in play, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Dark Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent discards a card from his or her hand.

flavorText: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

name: Venomoth

abilities.name: Poison Moth Wind
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, your Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can't be used if Venomoth is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The powder on its wings is poisonous if it is dark in hue. If it is light in hue, it causes paralysis.

name: Dark Steelix
rules: This Pokémon is both Darkness Metal type.

attacks.name: Energy Link
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Dark Steelix.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Golisopod

abilities.name: Armor
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Resolute Claws
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-GX or a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: With a flashing slash of its giant sharp claws, it cleaves seawater—or even air—right in two.

name: Bill's Maintenance
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
If you have any cards in your hand, shuffle 1 of them into your deck, then draw 3 cards.

name: Dark Celebi
rules: This Pokémon is both GrassDarkness type.

attacks.name: Dark Provide
attacks.text: Attach up to 1 Grass or Darkness Energy card from your hand to your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Slowking

abilities.name: Second Sight
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 3 cards of that player's deck and put them back on top of that player's deck in any order. This power can't be used if Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.

name: Lickilicky

attacks.name: Tongue Reel
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If you choose a Benched Pokémon, switch the Defending Pokémon with that Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to the Pokémon you chose. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, Lickilicky can't attack.

flavorText: It wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting to close to it will leave you soaked with drool.

name: Sceptile

abilities.name: Wild Growth
abilities.text: Each basic Grass Energy card attached to your Grass Pokémon provides GrassGrass Energy instead. You can't use more than 1 Wild Growth Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: The leaves that grow on its arms can slice down thick trees. It is without peer in jungle combat.

name: Team Magma's Rhydon
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Darkness type.

attacks.name: Magma Jab
attacks.text: This attack's damage is not affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Shoot Down
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has Team Aqua in its name, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Houndoom

attacks.name: Fire Counterattack
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Fighting Pokémon in play, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Dark Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent discards a card from his or her hand.

flavorText: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

abilities.name: Carry Off
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent's hand. If your opponent has any Baby Pokémon, Basic Pokémon, or Evolution cards there, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck. This power can't be used if Crobat is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Double Cross
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. If both of them are tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

name: Burmy Plant Cloak

abilities.name: Wear Cloak
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Burmy Plant Cloak is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for a basic Grass Energy card and attach it to Burmy Plant Cloak.

attacks.name: Plant Cloak Tackle
attacks.text: If Burmy Plant Cloak has any Grass Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

name: Darmanitan

attacks.name: Heat Assist
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Fire Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Darmani-Hands
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its internal fire burns at 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch.

name: Dark Tyranitar

attacks.name: Mountain Smasher
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Fighting Energy cards attached to Dark Tyranitar. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. Then, for each heads, discard the top card from your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Fling Away
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, this attack does 30 damage instead of 50 and choose 1 of those Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its power is such that not even mountains can stand in its way.

name: Mewtwo δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Fire Metal type.

abilities.name: Delta Switch
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Mewtwo from your hand onto your Bench, you may move any number of basic Energy cards attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon (excluding Mewtwo) in any way you like.

attacks.name: Energy Burst
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the total amount of Energy attached to Mewtwo and the Defending Pokémon.

name: Pikachu
rules: Oran Berry: Remove 1 damage counter from Pikachu at the end of your turn.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Healing Aroma
abilities.text: As long as Meganium is your Active Pokémon, remove 1 damage counter from each Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) (both yours and your opponent's) between turns.

attacks.name: Bouncy Move
attacks.text: You may put up to 5 damage counters on Meganium. If you do, this attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter you put on Meganium in this way.

name: Carvanha
flavorText: With its sturdy jaws and fangs, it can easily chomp wooden boats to splinters. It fights with Basculin over food.

name: Popplio

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It's famous for being a hard worker.

name: Chespin
flavorText: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

name: Nincada

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It grows underground, sensing its surroundings using antennae instead of its virtually blind eyes.

name: Meganium

abilities.name: Quick-Ripening Herb
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this Ability. Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it. You can use this Ability during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

flavorText: Meganium's breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Psyjack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Break Burn
attacks.text: Discard 2 Psychic Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was created by years of horrific gene splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

attacks.name: Kerzap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon and discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Lt. Surge's Raichu. If tails, this attack does 20 damage.

attacks.name: Thundertackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, Lt. Surge's Raichu does 20 damage to itself.

name: Cherubi

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It evolves by sucking the energy out of the small ball where it had been storing nutrients.

name: Grookey
flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

name: Venusaur-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frog Hop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Poison Impact
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

name: Bellsprout

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

abilities.name: Terraforming
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 5 cards from your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order. This power can't be used if Aggron is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Mix-Up
attacks.text: Your opponent discards the top card of his or her deck.

name: Deoxys Normal Forme

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys Normal Forme. (Any cards attached to Deoxys Normal Forme, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys Normal Forme on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

name: Sewaddle

attacks.name: Leaf Boomerang
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

name: Cacnea

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: By storing water in its body, this desert dweller can survive for 30 days without water.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Alolan home cooking involves drying Slowpoke tails and then simmering them into a salty stew.

name: Grookey

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

name: Yanmega

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

name: Victreebel

abilities.name: Tangling Tendrils
abilities.text: As long as Victreebel is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is ColorlessColorless more.

attacks.name: Acidic Drain
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned. Remove 3 damage counters from Victreebel.

flavorText: Acid that has dissolved many prey becomes sweeter, making it even more effective at attracting prey.

name: Jirachi

abilities.name: Stardust Song
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Jirachi from your hand onto your Bench, you may flip 3 coins. For each heads, search your discard pile for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to Jirachi.

attacks.name: Time Hollow
attacks.text: Choose a number of your opponent's Stage 1 or Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon up to the amount of Energy attached to Jirachi. Remove the highest Stage Evolution card from each of those Pokémon and put those cards back into your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Generations have believed that any wish written on a note on its head will come true when it awakens.

abilities.name: Dragon Wind
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Salamence is your Active Pokémon, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch. This power can't be used if Salamence is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Salamence during your opponent's next turn.

name: Lugia

attacks.name: Aeroblast
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is said that it quietly spends its time deep at the bottom of the sea because its powers are too strong.

attacks.name: Electro Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Energy cards attached to Misty's Golduck.

attacks.name: Super Removal
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Energy card attached to each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Energy cards and discard those Energy cards.

name: Ludicolo

abilities.name: Rain Dish
abilities.text: At any time between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Ludicolo.

attacks.name: Nature Power
attacks.text: If you have a Stadium Card in play, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage. If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If it hears festive music, all its muscles fill with energy. It can't help breaking out into a dance.

name: Leavanny

abilities.name: Leaf Tailor
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Cutting Arm
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

abilities.name: Firestarter
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Blaziken is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Fire Stream
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card attached to Blaziken. If you do, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Swadloon

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

name: Cherrim

abilities.name: Weather Guard
abilities.text: Your Grass Pokémon have no Weakness.

flavorText: If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun's rays with its whole body.

name: Dark Machamp

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.text: Your opponent shuffles his or her Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is almost impossible to defend against a Machamp's four powerful arms.

name: Charizard-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Combustion Blast
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Combustion Blast during your next turn.

name: Metapod

abilities.name: Emerge
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its shell is filled with a thick liquid. All of the cells throughout its body are being rebuilt in preparation for evolution.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

name: Dartrix

attacks.name: Sharp Blade Quill
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A bit of a dandy, it spends its free time preening its wings. Its preoccupation with any dirt on its plumage can leave it unable to battle.

name: Sudowoodo

attacks.name: Push Over
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Fighting Energy attached to Sudowoodo.

attacks.name: Rumble
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It disguises itself as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.

name: Jumpluff

attacks.name: Evolutionary Spore
attacks.text: Choose any number of your Hoppips and Skiplooms. Then, for each Pokémon you chose in this way, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and attach it to that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving those Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Drifts on seasonal winds and spreads its cotton-like spores all over the world to make more offspring.

name: Flareon

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Flareon or this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When storing thermal energy in its body, its temperature could soar to over 1600 degrees.

name: Mew

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Devolution Beam
attacks.text: Choose an evolved Pokémon (your own or your opponent's). Return the highest Stage Evolution card on that Pokémon to its player's hand. That Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned, or anything else that might be the result of an attack (just as if you had evolved it).

flavorText: So rare that it is still said to be a mirage by many experts. Only a few people have seen it worldwide.

name: Caterpie

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is covered with a green skin. When it grows, it sheds the skin, covers itself with silk, and becomes a cocoon.

name: Rayquaza δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Metal type.

abilities.name: Delta Guard
abilities.text: As long as Rayquaza has any Holon Energy cards attached to it, ignore the effect of Rayquaza's Lightning Storm attack.

attacks.name: Power Blow
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Rayquaza.

attacks.name: Lightning Storm
attacks.text: Put 7 damage counters on Rayquaza.

name: Pachirisu

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Poison Berry
attacks.text: If you have Croagunk in play, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It makes electricity with pouches in its cheeks and shoots charges from its tail. It live atop trees.

name: Rhyperior

attacks.name: Raging Drill
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Rhyperior.

attacks.name: Deep Scrap
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon would be Knocked Out by this attack, discard the top 3 cards from your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It can launch a rock held in its hand like a missile by tightening then expanding muscles instantly.

abilities.name: Super Connectivity
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a Psychic or Metal Energy card and attach it to your Active Pokémon. Then, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. This power can't be used if Metagross is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.text: If Metagross and the Defending Pokémon have a different amount of Energy attached to them, this attack's base damage is 40 instead of 70.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Sharpshooter
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Grass Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

flavorText: This Pokémon has a unique cry, as all three of its heads are always talking independently of one another.

attacks.name: Sleep Inducer
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Wake-Up Slap
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by any Special Conditions, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage. Then, remove all Special Conditions from the Defending Pokémon.

abilities.name: Flee
abilities.text: If an attack does damage to Misty's Tentacruel while it's your Active Pokémon, you may switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, which prevents all other effects of that attack on Misty's Tentacruel. This power can't be used if Misty's Tentacruel is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Jellyfish Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Larvesta

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Said to have been born from the sun, it spews fire from its horns and encases itself in a cocoon of fire when it evolves.

name: Spinarak

attacks.name: Spider Scram
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. Put this Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Some fishermen weave its sturdy thread into nets to catch fish Pokémon.

name: Articuno

abilities.name: Freezing Screech
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Articuno from your hand onto your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.

abilities.name: Shadow Command
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw 2 cards. If you have 7 or more cards in your hand, discard a number of cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. Then, put 2 damage counters on Dusknoir. This power can't be used if Dusknoir is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Damage Even
attacks.text: Count the number of damage counters on Dusknoir. Put that many damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Night Spin
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Dusknoir by your opponent's Pokémon that has 2 or less Energy attached to it during your opponent's next turn.

name: Probopass

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Triple Nose
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It exudes strong magnetism from all cover. It controls three small units called Mini-Noses.

name: Spinarak

attacks.name: Stun Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

flavorText: Some fishermen weave its sturdy thread into nets to catch fish Pokémon.

abilities.name: Synergy Effect
abilities.text: If Drake's Stadium is in play, remove 1 damage counter from Altaria between turns.

attacks.name: Surprise
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

name: Axew

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They mark their territory by leaving gashes in trees with their tusks. If a tusk breaks, a new one grows in quickly.

name: Heatmor

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Charring Breath
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't Burned, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It draws in air through its tail, transforms it into fire, and uses it like a tongue. It melts Durant and eats them.

name: Victini

abilities.name: Victory Star
abilities.text: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack, you may ignore all results of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again. You can't use more than 1 Victory Star Ability each turn.

flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

name: Ludicolo

abilities.name: Captivating Rhythm
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Solar Ray
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: The rhythm of bright, festive music activates Ludicolo's cells, making it more powerful.

name: Venusaur

abilities.name: Jungle Totem
abilities.text: Each basic Grass Energy attached to your Pokémon provides GrassGrass Energy. You can't apply more than 1 Jungle Totem Ability at a time.

flavorText: By spreading the broad petals of its flower and catching the sun's rays, it fills its body with power.

name: Team Magma's Camerupt

abilities.name: Burning Draft
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Fighting or Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flame Ball
attacks.text: Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Camerupt, with its strength against fire, played a big role in rescuing team members.

name: Wobbuffet

attacks.name: Double Return
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on Wobbuffet.

flavorText: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to build up its counterstrike.

name: Fennekin

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

name: M Charizard-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crimson Dive
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card attached to Flareon.

name: Flareon

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Flareon in order to use this attack.

flavorText: When storing thermal energy in its body, its temperature could soar to over 1600 degrees.

name: Azelf

abilities.name: Downer Material
abilities.text: If you have Uxie and Mesprit in play, the attack cost of each of your opponent's Basic Pokémon's attacks is Colorless more. You can't use more than 1 Downer Material Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Bind Pulse
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, your opponent can't attach any Special Energy cards from his or her hand to any of his or her Pokémon.

flavorText: Known as "The Being of Willpower." It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.

name: Nidoking

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Nidoking does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. It now takes 20 Poison damage instead of 10 after each player's turn (even if it was already Poisoned).

flavorText: Uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break its prey's bones.

name: Froakie
flavorText: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

name: Mismagius

attacks.name: Avenge
attacks.text: Count the number of your Pokémon that have any damage counters on them. Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its cries sound like incantations. Those hearing it are tormented by headaches and hallucinations.

abilities.name: Final Wish
abilities.text: Once during your opponent's turn, if Jirachi would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, you may search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Detour
attacks.text: If you have a Supporter card in play, use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Sewaddle
flavorText: Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Stoke
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon with a loyal nature, it will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.

name: Shroomish

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

name: Lileep

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: In ancient times, it lived in warm seas. It disguised itself as seaweed to ambush its prey and devoured them whole when they got close.

attacks.name: Fetch
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: One-Two Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Team Magma's Torkoal
rules: This Pokémon is both Fire Darkness type.

attacks.name: Magma Burn
attacks.text: Discard the top card from your opponent's deck, and flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Team Magma's Torkoal.

attacks.name: Hot Air
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Team Magma's Torkoal and your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

name: Quilladin

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It relies on its sturdy shell to deflect predators' attacks. It counterattacks with its sharp quills.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Staravia

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It flies around forests and fields in search of bug Pokémon. It stays within a huge flock.

name: Burmy Sandy Cloak

abilities.name: Wear Cloak
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Burmy Sandy Cloak is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for a basic Fighting Energy card and attach it to Burmy Sandy Cloak.

attacks.name: Sandy Cloak Tackle
attacks.text: If Burmy Sandy Cloak has any Fighting Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

name: Magcargo

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Lava Flow
attacks.text: You may discard any number of Fire Energy cards attached to Magcargo. If you do , this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage for each Fire Energy card you discarded.

flavorText: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

name: Kakuna

attacks.name: Multiply
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Kakuna and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

name: Bellossom

attacks.name: Sweet Nectar
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, remove all damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flower Dance
attacks.text: Does 30 damage times the number of cards with Bellossom in their names that you have in play (including this one).

flavorText: Plentiful in the tropics. When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.

name: Scramble Energy
rules: Scramble Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex). Scramble Energy provides Colorless Energy. While in play, if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, Scramble Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 3 in any combination at a time. If the Pokémon Scramble Energy is attached to isn't an Evolved Pokémon (or evolves into Pokémon-ex), discard Scramble Energy.

attacks.name: Kick Away
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

attacks.name: Strong Current
attacks.text: If Manectric has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon-ex. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: M Venusaur-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bloom Buster
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mass Destruction
attacks.text: Both Electrode and the Defending Pokémon are now Knocked Out. If Electrode has any Special Energy cards attached to it, this attack does nothing.

abilities.name: Super Suction Cups
abilities.text: As long as Octillery is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Standing By
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to Octillery. During your next turn, Octillery's Pulse Blast attack's base damage is 120.

attacks.name: Freeze Lock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't attach Energy cards from his or her hand to his or her Active Pokémon during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Crushing Ice
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Colorless Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost. Damage is calculated using the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost after applying effects to the Retreat Cost.

name: Amoonguss

attacks.name: Crazy Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Strange Reaction
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Confused, it is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

name: Sharpedo

attacks.name: Aqua Jet
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It drinks in seawater and jets it from its rear to propel itself. It's very sensitive to the scent of blood.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Barrier
attacks.text: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Mewtwo in order to prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Mewtwo during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

name: Weepinbell

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.

name: Torterra

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Remove from Torterra the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Ancient people imagined that beneath the ground, a gigantic Torterra dwelled.

name: Parasect

attacks.name: Mushroom Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

name: Fletchinder
flavorText: From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on the bewildered prey that pop out of the grass.

attacks.name: Vital Drain
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, remove all Special Conditions and 7 damage counters from Kabutops (all if there are less than 7).

attacks.name: Thunderous Blow
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Lightning Energy attached to Kabutops.

name: Swadloon

attacks.name: Double Razor Leaf
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It protects itself from the cold by wrapping up in leaves. It stays on the move, eating leaves in forests.

abilities.name: Fluff
abilities.text: During your opponent's turn, if Jumpluff would be damaged or affected by an opponent's attack and it already has at least 1 damage counter on it, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of that attack (including damage).

attacks.name: Cotton Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 times the number of heads.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.

name: Dustox

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Twilight Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: It violently flutters its wings to scatter toxic dust when attacked. It becomes active after sunset.

name: Toxicroak

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Convert Blow
attacks.text: If Toxicroak has any Psychic Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If Toxicroak has any Fighting Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: It has a poison sac at its throat. When it croaks, the stored poison is churned for more potency.

name: Abomasnow

abilities.name: Blessings of the Frost
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may attach a Grass Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hypno Hammer
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

name: Swampert

abilities.name: Wash Out
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Water or Fighting Energy attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Swampert is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card from your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

name: Simisage

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It attacks enemies with strikes of its thorn-covered tail. This Pokémon is wild tempered.

name: Dwebble
flavorText: When it finds a stone of a suitable size, it secretes a liquid from its mouth to open up a hole to crawl into.

name: Pineco

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: It sticks tree bark to itself with its saliva, making itself thicker and larger. Elderly Pineco are ridiculously huge.

name: Shaymin

attacks.name: Flower Aroma
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Shaymin. The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Damage Aid
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 30 damage plus 50 more damage. Then, remove all Special Conditions from the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives in flower patches and avoids detection by curling up to look like a flowering plant.

abilities.name: Wonder Guard
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Shedinja by your opponent's Evolved Pokémon and Pokémon-ex.

attacks.name: Damage Curse
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter, plus 1 more damage counter for each damage counter on Shedinja, on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Ariados

attacks.name: Reactive Poison
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spider Trap
attacks.text: You may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: It spins thread from both its rear and its mouth. Then it wraps its prey up in thread and sips their bodily fluids at its leisure.

name: Gengar

abilities.name: Curse
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another (even if it would Knock Out the other Pokémon). This power can't be used if Gengar is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Dark Mind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Under a full moon, this Pokémon like to mimic the shadows of people and laugh at their fright.

attacks.name: Wide Solarbeam
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Hard Plant
attacks.text: Venusaur can't use Hard Plant during your next turn.

name: Growlithe
flavorText: A Pokémon with a loyal nature, it will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.

name: Pichu

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Pikachu from your hand onto Pichu (this counts as evolving Pichu) and remove all damage counters from Pichu.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It plays with others by touching tails and setting off sparks. This appears to be a test of courage.

name: Leavanny

abilities.name: Leaf Tailor
