abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Cutting Arm
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

abilities.name: Emperor Aura
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you play Empoleon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Active Pokémon, you may use this power. Your opponent can't attach any Energy cards from his or her hand to his or her Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Empoleon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Dark Magneton

attacks.name: Sonicboom
attacks.text: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

attacks.name: Magnetic Lines
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any basic Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and attach that Energy card to it.

flavorText: The number of incidents of hackers using Magnetons to damage computer systems is steadily increasing.

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Chomp Chomp
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Loom Over
attacks.text: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

name: Fennekin

attacks.name: Invite Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

flavorText: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

abilities.name: Buffer
abilities.text: If Jumpluff would be Knocked Out by an opponent's attack, flip a coin. If heads, Jumpluff is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Shaymin

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Leaf Drain
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The flowers all over its body burst into bloom if it is lovingly hugged and senses gratitude.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Numbing Water
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: In many places, there are folktales of stardust falling into the ocean and becoming Staryu.

attacks.name: Lava Burn
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 basic Energy cards attached to Camerupt or this attack does nothing.

abilities.name: Nectar Pod
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch. This power can't be used if Victreebel is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Sharp Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Virizion

attacks.name: Bail Out
attacks.text: Put 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Prize Count
attacks.text: If you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Legends say this Pokémon confounded opponents with its swift movements.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The flame on its tail indicates Charmander's life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.

name: Volcarona

attacks.name: Solar Transporter
attacks.text: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck and put all Team Plasma cards you find there into your hand. Discard the other cards.

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Thought to be an embodiment of the sun, it appeared during a bitterly cold winter and saved Pokémon from freezing.

name: Cresselia

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of either player's deck and put them back on top of that player's deck in any order.

attacks.name: Healing Light
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Triple Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its wings are covered in toxic scales. If it finds bird Pokémon going after Caterpie, Butterfree sprinkles its scales on them to drive them off.

name: Salamence δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Fire Metal type.

attacks.name: Fire Dance
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Delta Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Metal Energy card attached to Salamence.

name: Slowking

attacks.name: Revelation
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its nonchalant reaction to everything somehow gives this Pokémon an imposing presence.

name: Torchic

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Kyogre

attacks.name: Destructive Tsunami
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 40 damage to each of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with Groudon.

attacks.name: Crosscounter
attacks.text: If an attack does damage to Rocket's Hitmonchan during your opponent's next turn (even if Rocket's Hitmonchan is Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, Rocket's Hitmonchan attacks your opponent's Active Pokémon for double that amount of damage. (If Rocket's Hitmonchan takes 20 damage, it does 40 damage to that Pokémon.)

name: Dragonite

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Draco Meteor
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each of your opponent's Pokémon. If that coin flip is heads, this attack does 50 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to make its home somewhere in the sea. It guides crews of shipwrecks to shore.

name: Cacturne

attacks.name: Rapid-Fire Needles
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Payback
attacks.text: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 60 more damage and discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It becomes active at night, seeking prey that is exhausted from the day's desert heat.

name: Magneton

abilities.name: Electromagnetic Power
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may take 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your Magnemites, Magnetons, or Dark Magnetons and attach it to a different 1 of your Magnemites, Magnetons, and Dark Magnetons. This power can't be used if Magneton is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: If there are any Lightning Energy cards in your discard pile, attach 1 of them to Magneton.

flavorText: Three Magnemites are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

name: Sewaddle

attacks.name: Multiply
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Sewaddle and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth.

name: Bayleef

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The buds that ring its neck give off a spicy aroma that perks people up.

name: Scorbunny

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon's body. Once that happens, it's ready to fight at full power.

name: Ninjask

abilities.name: Cast-off Shell
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for Shedinja and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Night Slash
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Because it moves so quickly, it sometimes becomes unseeable. It congregates around tree sap.

name: Jolteon

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It accumulates negative ions from the atmosphere to blast out 10,000-volt lightning bolts.

name: High Pressure System
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each player pays Colorless less to retreat his or her Fire and Water Pokémon.

name: Hitmontop

attacks.name: Detect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Hitmontop.

attacks.name: Triple Kick
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: If you become enchanted by its smooth, elegant, dancelike kicks, you may get drilled hard.

name: Pansage

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It shares the leaf on its head with weary-looking Pokémon. These leaves are known to relieve stress.

name: Decidueye

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Brave Bird
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It fires arrow quills from its wings with such precision, they can pierce a pebble at distances over a hundred yards.

name: Wimpod

abilities.name: Wimp Out
abilities.text: During your first turn, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake.

name: Chespin

attacks.name: Nosh
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Such a thick shell of wood covers its head and back that even a direct hit from a truck wouldn't faze it.

name: Karrablast

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

name: Light Arcanine

abilities.name: Drive Off
abilities.text: As long as Light Arcanine is your Active Pokémon, once during your turn (before your attack), if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. This power can't be used while Light Arcanine is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Gentle Flames
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Baby Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage instead of 50. If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 30 damage instead of 50.

flavorText: This legendary Chinese Pokémon is easily recognized by its large flowing mane.

abilities.name: Water Cyclone
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Water Energy card from your Active Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Kingdra is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Rapids
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon, if any.

name: Lugia

attacks.name: Silver Wing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon and return it to your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Psychic Destruction
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, this attack's base damage is 40 instead of 120.

flavorText: It sleeps in a deep-sea trench. If it flaps its wings, it is said to cause a 40-day storm.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Vengeance
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Basic Pokémon and each Evolution card in your discard pile. You can't add more than 60 damage in this way.

name: Litwick

attacks.name: Flickering Flames
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life force of any who follow it.

name: Reshiram

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Blue Flare
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 1 or Stage 2 card that evolves from that Pokémon in your hand, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.)

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Wildfire
attacks.text: You may discard any number of Fire Energy cards attached to Moltres when you use this attack. If you do, discard that many cards from the top of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Dive Bomb
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Known as the legendary bird of fire. Every flap of its wings creates a dazzling flash of flames.

abilities.name: Holon Veil
abilities.text: Treat each Basic Pokémon and Evolution card in your deck, in your discard pile, in your hand, and in play as a Pokémon that has δ on its card.

attacks.name: Delta Circle
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Pokémon you have in play that has δ on its card.

name: Chesnaught

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Its Tackle is forceful enough to flip a 50-ton tank. It shields its allies from danger with its own body.

name: Blissey

abilities.name: Kind Egg
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Happiny is anywhere under Blissey, you may choose a number of cards in your hand up to the amount of Energy attached to Blissey and put those cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. Then, draw that many cards. This power can't be used if Blissey is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Happy Chance
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 for each Energy attached to Blissey. Before doing damage, you may search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Blissey.

flavorText: This kindhearted Pokémon nurses sick Pokémon to health. It senses feelings of sadness.

name: Yanmega

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to Yanmega.

flavorText: By churning its wings, it creates shock waves that inflict critical internal injuries to foes.

attacks.name: Rock Blast
attacks.text: Discard up to 5 Fighting Energy cards attached to Armaldo. For each Energy card you discarded, choose an opponent's Pokémon in play and this attack does 20 damage to those Pokémon. (You may choose the same Pokémon more than once.) This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Xatu

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: In South America, it is said that its right eye sees the future and its left eye views the past.

abilities.name: Reactive Lift
abilities.text: As long as Wailord has any React Energy cards attached to it, the Retreat Cost for each of your Water Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) is 0.

attacks.name: Hypno Splash
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Rely on Friends
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Benched Stage 1 Evolved Pokémon.

abilities.name: Battle Aura
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has δ on its card does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

name: Shroomish

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

name: Victreebel

abilities.name: Wafting Scent
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

name: Machamp

attacks.name: Directing Traffic
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.

attacks.name: Cross Chop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It grasps its opponents with its four arms and twists them up in an intricate hold. People call it "the Machamp special."

abilities.name: Rebellion
abilities.text: Whenever Misty's Gyarados attacks, flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, that attack does nothing. Instead, shuffle Misty's Gyarados and all cards attached to it into your deck. (This power works even if Misty's Gyarados is Confused.)

attacks.name: Charge Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Zapdos G.

attacks.name: Lightning Strike
attacks.text: You may discard all Lightning attached to Zapdos G. If you do, this attack's base damage is 80 instead of 40.

name: Corsola

attacks.name: Rally
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 different types of Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Many live in the clean seas of the south. They apparently can't live in polluted waters.

name: Empoleon LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Empoleon. Empoleon LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Supreme Command
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose up to 2 cards from your opponent's hand without looking and put them face down next to the Defending Pokémon. (These cards are not in play or in your opponent's hand.) At the end of your opponent's next turn, return those cards to your opponent's hand. This power can't be used if Empoleon is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hydro Impact
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to that Pokémon (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Empoleon can't attack during your next turn.

name: Cradily

abilities.name: Swaying Strangle
abilities.text: Your opponent's Pokémon that are affected by Special Conditions can't retreat.

attacks.name: Poison Tentacles
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Normally, it lived on shallow sea shoals. When the tide went out, this Pokémon came up on land to search for prey.

name: Meganium

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.text: If this Pokémon's remaining HP is 50 or less, its attacks do 70 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Green Force
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Meganium's breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

name: Nidoking

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Nidoking does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. It now takes 20 Poison damage instead of 10 after each player's turn (even if it was already Poisoned).

flavorText: Uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break its prey's bones.

name: Sylveon

attacks.name: Disarming Voice
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It sends a soothing aura from its ribbonlike feelers to calm fights.

name: Fraxure

abilities.name: Grit
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, each of its attacks does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Since a broken tusk will not grow back, they diligently sharpen their tusks on river rocks after they've been fighting.

name: Bidoof

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Scavenge
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, an put it into your hand.

flavorText: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

name: Metapod

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can't withstand a harsh attack.

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Sudden Sting
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Kakuna during this turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

flavorText: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

name: Palkia

attacks.name: Spacial Rend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If there is any Stadium card in play, discard it.

attacks.name: Transback
attacks.text: You may flip a coin. If heads, discard all Energy attached to Palkia and put the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it on top of your opponent's deck. Your opponent shuffles his or her deck afterward.

flavorText: It has the ability to distort space. It is described as a deity in Sinnoh-region mythology.

abilities.name: Spiral Swirl
abilities.text: If Poliwrath is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, the Attacking Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Beatdown
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Darkness Pokémon or has Dark in its name, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Hyper Splash
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon, this attack does 60 damage plus 30 more damage.

abilities.name: Boundary Aura
abilities.text: Apply Weakness for each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) as ×2 instead.

attacks.name: Combo Throw
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Warp Sounds
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

abilities.name: Self-healing
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Fire Energy card from your hand to Flareon, remove all Special Conditions affecting Flareon.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Burn Booster
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to Flareon in order to use this attack. If the discarded card is a Fire Energy card, this attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Ariados

attacks.name: Trapping Thread
attacks.text: Whenever your opponent plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand during their next turn, prevent all effects of that card done to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spins thread from both its rear and its mouth. Then it wraps its prey up in thread and sips their bodily fluids at its leisure.

name: Scatterbug

abilities.name: Abnormal Outbreak
abilities.text: You can use this Ability only if you go second. Once during your first turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Spewpa and a Vivillon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

name: Muk

abilities.name: Toxic Gas
abilities.text: Ignore all Pokémon Powers other than Toxic Gases. This power stops working while Muk is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Sludge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

name: Swadloon

attacks.name: Swaddle Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Preferring dark, damp places, it spends the entire day eating fallen leaves that lie around it.

name: Snorlax-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Collapse
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Thunderous Snore
attacks.text: This attack can be used if this Pokémon is Asleep. If it is not Asleep, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Pulverizing Pancake-GX
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Feraligatr

abilities.name: Berserk
abilities.text: When you play Feraligatr from your hand, flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 5 cards from your opponent's deck. If tails, discard the top 5 cards from your deck.

attacks.name: Chomp
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of damage counters on Feraligatr. This attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon (1 if there is only 1). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Kerzap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage to the Defending Pokémon and discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Raichu.

name: Darkrai

abilities.name: Darkness Shade
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Darkrai from your hand onto your Bench, you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Dark Slumber
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Dark Resolve
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, remove 4 damage counters from Darkrai.

flavorText: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

name: Grookey
flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: As a newborn, it can barely stand. However, through galloping, its legs are made tougher and faster.

name: Dustox

ancientTrait.name: Δ Plus
ancientTrait.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Wind Shard
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It violently flutters its wings to scatter toxic dust when attacked. It becomes active after sunset.

name: Chespin
flavorText: Such a thick shell of wood covers its head and back that even a direct hit from a truck wouldn't faze it.

name: Ambipom

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose 2 cards from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the cards you chose, then have your opponent shuffle those cards into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Tail Spank
attacks.text: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can't discard 2 cards from your hand, this attack does nothing.)

flavorText: They live on large trees. They are said to communicate by connecting their tails to those of others.

abilities.name: Rebirth
abilities.text: When Rocket's Moltres is Knocked Out, you may return it to your hand after discarding it. This power can't be used if Rocket's Moltres is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed when it is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Fire Wall
attacks.text: If an attack does damage to Rocket's Moltres during your opponent's next turn (even if Rocket's Moltres is Knocked Out), Rocket's Moltres attacks your opponent's Active Pokémon for 10 damage. (Apply Weakness and Resistance.)

name: Ampharos

attacks.name: Acceleration Bolt
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Ampharos does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: The tail's tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

name: Wailord V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Draw Up
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Water Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ocean Waves
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 120 damage for each heads.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Recharge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Pikachu. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to Pikachu.

flavorText: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Lightning Strike
attacks.text: You may discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Jolteon. If you do, this attack's base damage is 70 instead of 40.

name: Scizor
flavorText: Its wings are not used for flying. They are flapped at high speed to adjust its body temperature.

name: Swadloon

attacks.name: Grass Cocooning
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

name: Leafeon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Breath of the Leaves
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 50 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Grand Bloom-GX
attacks.text: For each of your Benched Basic Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Reshiram-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Fire Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Scorching Column
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Vermilion-GX
attacks.text: You may attach up to 5 Fire Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Ledian

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: While it's believed that starlight provides it with energy, this Pokémon also loves to eat berries. In the daytime, it curls up in the grass to sleep.

name: Caterpie

abilities.name: Adaptive Evolution
abilities.text: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

flavorText: For protection, it releases a horrible stench from the antennae on its head to drive away enemies.

attacks.name: Mining
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If that card is a Pokémon Tool card, you may attach it to 1 of your Pokémon instead. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Bite Off
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Poison Reaction
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Talonflame

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Jet Shoot
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is increased by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: In the fever of an exciting battle, it showers embers from the gaps between its feathers and takes to the air.

name: Team Aqua's Spheal
flavorText: Spheal's Water Gun can put out any fire. Flames used by opponents have no effect!

name: Crustle

attacks.name: Hard Press
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When its boulder is broken in battles for territory, it feels unsure and begins to weaken.

name: Petilil

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage and heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Since they prefer moist, nutrient-rich soil, the areas where Petilil live are known to be good for growing plants.

name: Nidoqueen

attacks.name: Boyfriends
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Nidoking you have in play.

flavorText: Its hard scales provide strong protection. It uses its hefty bulk to execute powerful moves.

attacks.name: Super Hypno Wave
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Kyogre ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Major Flood
attacks.text: Discard a Water Energy card attached to Kyogre ex. Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Shining Horn
abilities.text: As long as Absol is the only Pokémon you have in play, your opponent's Basic Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Extra Call
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon-ex, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Breaking Sound
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Bass Control
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thunderous Roar
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Kingdra δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Fire Metal type.

abilities.name: Dragon Curse
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Kingdra is your Active Pokémon, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon with δ on its card. This power can't be used if Kingdra is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Extra Flame
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Very competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.

name: Boost Energy
rules: Boost Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon. Discard Boost Energy at the end of the turn it was attached. Boost Energy provides ColorlessColorlessColorless Energy. The Pokémon Boost Energy is attached to can't retreat. If the Pokémon Boost Energy is attached to isn't an Evolved Pokémon, discard Boost Energy.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of those Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Froslass

attacks.name: Destiny Bond
attacks.text: Discard a Psychic Energy attached to Froslass. During your opponent's next turn, if Froslass would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Icy Breath
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It freezes foes with an icy breath nearly -60 degrees F. What seems to be its body is actually hollow.

name: Arcanine

abilities.name: Blazing Mane
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.

name: Drednaw V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Solid Shell
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Powerful Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

name: Fennekin
flavorText: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Ninetales

attacks.name: Lure
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Ninetales in order to use this attack.

flavorText: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

name: Braixen

attacks.name: Destructive Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

name: Leafeon

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: With cells similar to those of plants, it performs photosynthesis inside its body and creates pure air.

name: Weedle

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. This Pokémon is now confused.

attacks.name: Leaf Storm
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from each of your Grass Pokémon.

flavorText: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect. It withers if a Trainer does not take good care of it.

abilities.name: 3-D Reset
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), return a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to your hand. This power can't be used if Porygon2 is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Data Retrieval
attacks.text: If you have less than 8 cards in your hand, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Scramble Trip
attacks.text: If Porygon2 has a Scramble Energy card attached to it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Spinning Blow
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. If both coins are heads, switch Forretress with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scatterbomb
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, do 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. For each tails, do 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Leafeon

attacks.name: Miasma Wind
attacks.text: Does 50 damage times the number of Special Conditions affecting the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sooting Scent
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you'll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bolt Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

abilities.name: Blaze Dance
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you play Infernape from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 4 Fire Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Close Combat
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Infernape by attacks is increased by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Spreading Fire
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to Infernape and this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Excavate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top card of your deck. Put that card back on top of your deck, or discard that card. This power can't be used if Sableye is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Disable
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Roselia

attacks.name: Double Whip
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Relaxing Fragrance
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage and remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The more healthy the Roselia, the more pleasant its flowers' aroma. Its scent deeply relaxes people.

name: Virizion

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Wallop
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Leaf Wallop does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Using whirlwind-like movements, it confounds and swiftly cuts opponents.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Gigashock
attacks.text: Choose 3 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and this attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If your opponent has fewer than 3 Benched Pokémon, do the damage to each of them.

flavorText: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

attacks.name: Shadow Images
attacks.text: Whenever Rocket's Scyther is attacked, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does no damage to Rocket's Scyther. (Any other effects of the attack still happen.) This effect lasts until Rocket's Scyther takes damage (or is Benched or is evolved).

abilities.name: Root Protector
abilities.text: Any damage done to Swampert by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that isn't an Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may switch your opponent's Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Push Over
attacks.text: Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each Fighting Energy attached to Swampert.

name: Simisage

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It attacks enemies with strikes of its thorn-covered tail. This Pokémon is wild tempered.

name: Smeargle

abilities.name: Portrait
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Smeargle is your Active Pokémon, you may look at your opponent's hand. If you do, choose a Supporter card you find there and use the effect of that card as the effect of this power. This power can't be used if Smeargle is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Once it becomes an adult, it has a tendency to let its comrades plant footprints on its back.

name: Kangaskhan

attacks.name: Fetch
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The infant rarely ventures out of its mother's protective pouch until it is three years old.

name: Slugma
flavorText: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

name: Celebi

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Amazing Bloom
attacks.text: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon has the ability to move through time. Records describing it as a forest deity can be found from many different eras.

name: Snivy
flavorText: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter.

name: Rhyperior

abilities.name: Earth Fissure
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Rhyperior from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may discard the top 3 cards from your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Rock Wrecker
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. Rhyperior can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: It puts rocks in holes in its palms and uses its muscles to shoot them. Geodude are shot at rare times.

name: Flareon

abilities.name: Flare Effect
abilities.text: Each of your Stage 1 Pokémon in play is now a Fire Pokémon in addition to its existing types.

attacks.name: Heat Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It has a flame bag inside its body. After inhaling deeply, it blows out flames of nearly 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

abilities.name: Power Gene
abilities.text: As long as Nidoking is in play, your attacks by Nidoran ♀, Nidorina, Nidoqueen, Nidoran ♂, and Nidorino do 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earth Poison
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Bound Crush
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Nidoking can't use Bound Crush during your next turn.

name: Larvesta
flavorText: The base of volcanoes is where they make their homes. They shoot fire from their five horns to repel attacking enemies.

name: Starmie-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Star Stream
attacks.text: Attach 2 Water Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Juxtapose
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch the number of damage counters on Rocket's Mewtwo with the number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon (even if it would Knock Out either Pokémon). (It's okay if 1 of the Pokémon has no damage counters on it.)

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It lashes about with its tail to knock down its foe. It then tears up the fallen opponent with sharp claws.

name: Heracross

abilities.name: Guts
abilities.text: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

attacks.name: Pitch
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This powerful Pokémon thrusts its prized horn under its enemies' bellies, then lifts and throws them.

name: Fraxure
flavorText: Their tusks can shatter rocks. Territory battles between Fraxure can be intensely violent.

name: Shelmet

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid.

abilities.name: Dragon Veil
abilities.text: As long as Kingdra is in play, each of your Active Pokémon has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Hyper Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Sonic
attacks.text: This attack's damage is not affected by Resistance.

abilities.name: Jet Stream
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Blastoise is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to Blastoise, if any. Then, if there are any Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon, choose 1 of them and discard it. This power can't be used if Blastoise is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Energy Cannon
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack's Energy Cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

name: Heracross-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Giga Power
attacks.text: You may do 40 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself. | When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

name: Leavanny

attacks.name: Cleave
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves by using the sticky silk secreted from its mouth.

name: Mew

attacks.name: Psychic Balance
attacks.text: If you have less cards in your hand than your opponent, draw cards until you have the same number of cards as your opponent. (If you have more or the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Re-creation
attacks.text: Choose an attack on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in his or her discard pile. Re-creation copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Mew performs that attack.

flavorText: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe MEW to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

name: Oddish

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: During the day, It stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

abilities.name: Rain Dish
abilities.text: At any time between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Ludicolo.

attacks.name: Hydro Punch
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Ludicolo but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

name: Nincada

attacks.name: Slight Intrusion
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can sometimes live underground for more than 10 years. It absorbs nutrients from the roots of trees.

name: Groudon ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Major Earthquake
attacks.text: Discard a Fighting Energy card attached to Groudon ex. Does 10 damage to your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Treecko

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: Small hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.

attacks.name: Hailstone
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Super Slash
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Sobble

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.

name: Low Pressure System
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each Grass and Lightning Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets +10 HP.

name: Lucario LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Lucario. Lucario LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Stance
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Lucario LV.X from your hand onto your Active Lucario, you may use this power. Prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Lucario during your opponent's next turn. (If Lucario is no longer your Active Pokémon, this effect ends.)

attacks.name: Close Combat
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Lucario by attacks is increased by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Wartortle

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Wartortle but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: When attacked, this Pokémon withdraws into its shell, although it can't quite pull in all of its long tail.

name: Ninetales

attacks.name: Lure
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Ninetales in order to use this attack.

flavorText: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

attacks.name: Power Generation
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.

attacks.name: Scorching Smoke
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned. Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Torkoal.

name: Houndoom

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Until the end of your next turn, if an attack damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Houndoom or this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

name: Banette

attacks.name: Lost Crush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and put it in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Breakdown
attacks.text: Count the number of cards in your opponent's hand. Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon developed from an abandoned doll that amassed a grudge. It is seen in dark alleys.

name: Tropius

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Leaf Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The bunches of fruit growing around the necks of Tropius in Alola are especially sweet compared to those in other regions.

name: Hitmonlee

attacks.name: Stretch Kick
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon, and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When in a hurry, its legs lengthen progressively. It runs smoothly with extra long, loping strides.

name: Decidueye-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Feather Arrow
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hollow Hunt-GX
attacks.text: Put 3 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Ion Coating
abilities.text: You may use this power once during each of your turns (before your attack). All Lightning Energy attached to your Active Pokémon becomes Water Energy for the rest of the turn. (This effect ends if your Active Pokémon retreats or is returned to your hand.) This power can't be used if Lanturn is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Electric Tackle
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage plus 10 damage for each Lightning Energy attached to Lanturn. Flip a coin. If tails, Lanturn does 10 damage to itself for each Lightning Energy attached to it.

name: Shuckle

attacks.name: Berry Picking
attacks.text: Shuffle up to 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.

name: Squirtle

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It shelters itself in its shell then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

name: Mow Rotom

abilities.name: Roto Motor
abilities.text: If you have 9 or more Pokémon Tool cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon's attacks.

attacks.name: Special Mow
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

name: Metapod

abilities.name: Adaptive Evolution
abilities.text: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.

flavorText: A steel-hard shell protects its tender body. It quietly endures hardships while awaiting evolution.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its six eggs use telepathy to communicate among themselves. It is believed to carry plant genes and the genes of other species.

abilities.name: Shining Horn
abilities.text: As long as Heracross is the only Pokémon you have in play, your opponent's Basic Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Dig Deep
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

attacks.name: Extra Claws
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Light Azumarill

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Bubble Jump
attacks.text: If you have any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, take 2 Energy cards attached to Light Azumarill and attach them to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then return Light Azumarill and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: It curls up its long ears when swimming to prevent water from entering them.

name: Starmie δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Water Metal type.

abilities.name: Metal Navigation
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Metal Energy card and attach it to Starmie. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Starmie is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Energy Loop
attacks.text: Return an Energy card attached to Starmie to your hand.

name: Ariados

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.text: Remove from Ariados the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poisonous Saliva
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It's hard to tell which end is which.

name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Night Eyes
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Perish Song
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep and was attacked with Night Eyes during your last turn, it is Knocked Out.

flavorText: It likes playing mischievous tricks such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.

name: Petilil

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Since they prefer moist, nutrient-rich soil, the areas where Petilil live are known to be good for growing plants.

name: Chespin

attacks.name: Tree Climb
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

name: Lampent
flavorText: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

name: Scatterbug

attacks.name: Ultra Evolution
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for Vivillon and put it onto this Scatterbug to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

name: Dialga
rules: Adamant Orb: If an Active Pokémon has Weakness to Metal type, Dialga's attacks do 20 more damage to that Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Time Shift
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Diamond Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Grudge
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

attacks.name: Bench Manipulation
attacks.text: Your opponent flips a number of coins equal to the number of his or her Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage times the number of tails. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Litten

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

abilities.name: Energy Draw
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard 1 Energy card from your hand. Then draw up to 3 cards from your deck. This power can't be used if Delcatty is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Max Energy Source
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Seek Out
attacks.text: Search your deck for 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Pull Away
attacks.text: If your opponent has 5 or more cards in his or her hand, your opponent discards a number of cards until your opponent has 4 cards left in his or her hand.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Healing Melody
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Recordings of Jigglypuff's strange lullabies can be purchased from department stores. These CDs can be found near the bedding area.

name: Tangrowth

attacks.name: Power Whip
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each basic Energy card attached to Tangrowth to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Stick and Absorb
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counters from Tangrowth. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.

attacks.name: Trash Bolt
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card from your hand. (If you can't discard a card from your hand, this attack does nothing.)

abilities.name: Miraculous Powder
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Butterfree is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 2 damage counters from Butterfree.

name: Chandelure

abilities.name: Shady Move
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 Pokémon to another Pokémon.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Being consumed in Chandelure's flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind.

name: Shaymin

attacks.name: Growth
attacks.text: Attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to Shaymin.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to Shaymin.

flavorText: The flowers all over its body burst into bloom if it is lovingly hugged and senses gratitude.

name: Pheromosa

attacks.name: White Ray
attacks.text: If you have only 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: One of the Ultra Beasts. It refuses to touch anything, perhaps because it senses some uncleanness in this world.

name: Seedot
flavorText: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

name: Breloom

attacks.name: Hibernation Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Your opponent flips 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep.

flavorText: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Paralyze Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is also Paralyzed.

flavorText: It can take down any opponent with its powerful poison stingers. It sometimes attacks in swarms.

name: Mareep

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its fluffy coat swells to double when static electricity builds up. Touching it can be shocking.

name: Cherrim

attacks.name: Worry Seed
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage and remove 3 damage counters from Cherrim.

flavorText: It blooms during times of strong sunlight. It tries to make up for everything it endured as a bud.

name: Rockruff
flavorText: It's considered to be a good Pokémon for beginners because of its friendliness, but its disposition grows rougher as it grows up.

name: Aerodactyl

abilities.name: Unearth
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for Helix Fossil, Dome Fossil, or Old Amber, show it your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Aerodactyl is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon that roamed the skies in the dinosaur era. Its teeth are like saw blades.

name: Wobbuffet

abilities.name: Tenacious Bind
abilities.text: As long as Wobbuffet is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is ColorlessColorless more.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

attacks.name: Poison Seed
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

abilities.name: Family Bonds
abilities.text: As long as Nidoqueen is in play, the Retreat Cost for Nidoran ♀, Nidorina, Nidoran ♂, Nidorino and Nidoking is 0.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Power Lariat
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Evolved Pokémon you have in play.

name: Roselia

attacks.name: Needling Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The more healthy the Roselia, the more pleasant its flowers' aroma. Its scent deeply relaxes people.

name: Surskit
flavorText: It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.

name: Xerneas

attacks.name: Geomancy
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Fairy Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Rainbow Spear
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Dizzy Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Buneary by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: When it senses danger, it perks up its ears. On cold nights, it sleeps with its head tucked into its fur.

name: Delphox

attacks.name: Flickering Flames
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Psystorm
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all Pokémon in play.

flavorText: Using psychic power, it generates a fiery vortex of 5,400 degree Fahrenheit, incinerating foes swept into this whirl of flame.

name: Lucario

attacks.name: Dimension Sphere
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each of your Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It's said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras. Even foes it could not otherwise see.

name: Magikarp
flavorText: In the distant past, they were fairly strong, but they have become gradually weaker over time.

name: Charizard

abilities.name: Roaring Resolve
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put 2 damage counters on this Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for up to 2 Fire Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Continuous Blaze Ball
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: When expelling a blast of superhot fire, the red flame at the tip of its tail burns more intensely.

abilities.name: Fin Luster
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Lumineon is your Active Pokémon, you may look at your opponent's hand. If your opponent's Bench isn't full, choose 1 Basic Pokémon from your opponent's hand, and put it onto his or her Bench. Then, switch it with the Defending Pokémon. This power can't be used if Lumineon is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Quick Swim
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Elegant Swim
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Lumineon during your opponent's next turn.

name: Torkoal

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: You find abandoned coal mines full of them. They dig tirelessly in search of coal.

name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Legend Ceremony
attacks.text: Search your deck for both halves of a Pokémon LEGEND, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Reflect Energy
attacks.text: Move an Energy card attached to Bronzong to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Ancient people believed that petitioning Bronzong for rain was the way to make crops grow.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Cat Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage. If tails and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Adores circular objects. Wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

name: Thwackey

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

name: Shaymin

attacks.name: Coax
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon of different types and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

name: Dark Slowbro

abilities.name: Reel In
abilities.text: When you play Dark Slowbro from your hand, choose up to 3 Basic Pokémon and/or Evolution cards from your discard pile and put them into your hand.

attacks.name: Fickle Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It loves nothing better than to be left alone, and so sometimes ends up being manipulated by unscrupulous people.

name: Leavanny

attacks.name: Nurturing
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
