flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Jump Kick
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It slams foes by sharply uncoiling is rolled ears. It stings enough to make a grown-up cry in pain.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Invisible Wall
abilities.text: Whenever an attack (including your own) does 30 or more damage to Mr. Mime (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.) This power can't be used if Mr. Mime is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Meditate
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: If interrupted while miming, it will slap around the enemy with its broad hands.

name: Seedot
flavorText: If it remains still, it looks just like a real nut. It delights in surprising foraging Pokémon.

name: Pansear

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Very intelligent, it roasts berries before eating them. It likes to help people.

name: Snivy

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: They photosynthesize by bathing their tails in sunlight. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.

name: Flareon

attacks.name: Vengeance
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees F.

name: Spewpa

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The beaks of birds can't begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder.

attacks.name: Extra Draw
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon-ex in play, search your deck for up to 2 Grass Energy cards and attach them to Heracross. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Sonicboom
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

abilities.name: Magnetic Flow
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Magneton is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon that have Energy cards attached to them. Choose 1 of the Energy cards attached to each of those Pokémon and switch them between the Pokémon. This power can't be used if Magneton is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Steel Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that are the same type (color) as the Defending Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: M Heracross-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Big Bang Horn
attacks.text: This attack does 180 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Tonnage
attacks.text: You may do 60 damage plus 40 more damage. If you do, Piloswine does 30 damage to itself.

name: Light Dragonite

abilities.name: Miraculous Wind
abilities.text: As long as Light Dragonite is your Active Pokémon, each Special Energy card provides Colorless Energy instead of its usual type and its other effects stop working. This power stops working while Light Dragonite is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Light Wave
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, other than damage, done to Light Dragonite during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It is said to fly constantly over the sea, looking for people in need of aid.

name: Pansage
flavorText: It's good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It's kind enough to share them with friends.

name: Roserade

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Spike Whip
attacks.text: If Budew is anywhere under Roserade, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It attracts prey with a sweet aroma, then downs it with thorny whips hidden in its arms.

name: Wartortle

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is said to live 10,000 years. Its furry tail is popular as a symbol of longevity.

abilities.name: Manipulate
abilities.text: When you play Gengar from your hand to evolve your Active Pokémon, you may put a Basic Pokémon (excluding Baby Pokémon) from your discard pile onto your Bench. Then, flip 3 coins. For each heads, choose a basic Energy card from your discard pile and attach it to that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydrokinesis
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Lickitung

attacks.name: Lick Wounds
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Pokémon (yours or your opponent's) with damage counters on it and remove 2 damage counters from it (1 if it only has 1).

attacks.name: Tongue Stretch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

flavorText: This Pokémon uses its six-foot tongue as if it were a hand. The origin of its anaesthetic properties is still a mystery.

abilities.name: Firestarter
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Blaziken is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Fire Stream
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card attached to Blaziken. This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Haxorus

attacks.name: Axe Slugger
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Colorless Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck.

flavorText: They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. They challenge foes with tusks that can cut steel.

name: Houndour

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Collect Fire
attacks.text: If there are any Fire Energy cards in your discard pile, choose 1 of them and attach it to Houndour.

flavorText: To corner prey, they check each other's location using barks that only they can understand.

name: Team Aqua's Sealeo

attacks.name: Splatter
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Hail Storm
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Team Magma Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It provides support by making paths of ice. It can win any battle as long as it's on ice.

name: Bronzong

abilities.name: Miracle Oracle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card. Then, discard a card from your hand. If you discard an Energy card, draw 1 more card. This power can't be used if Bronzong is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Shady Stamp
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: One caused a news sensation when it was dug up at a construction site after a 2,000-year sleep.

name: Servine

attacks.name: Double Slash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Skuntank.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: If there are any Lightning Energy cards in your discard pile, attach 1 of them to Rocket's Zapdos.

attacks.name: Electroburn
attacks.text: Rocket's Zapdos does damage to itself equal to 10 times the number of Lightning Energy cards attached to it.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Epic Splash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

name: Volcarona

attacks.name: Shimmering Scales
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Power Hurricane
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona's fire provided a replacement for the sun.

abilities.name: Green Aroma
abilities.text: Remove all Special Conditions from each of your Grass Pokémon. Each of your Grass Pokémon can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Desperate Pollen
attacks.text: If Venusaur already has 8 or more damage counters on it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Special Reaction
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by any Special Conditions, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage for each of those Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Pidgeot

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.text: Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, return the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your opponent's hand.

flavorText: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

name: Hoppip

attacks.name: Multiply
attacks.text: Search your deck for Hoppip and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It drifts on winds. It is said that when Hoppip gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

name: Cacnea

attacks.name: Sucker Punch
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in arid locations. Its yellow flowers bloom once a year.

name: Drednaw VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Solid Shell
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: G-Max Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.

name: Poliwrath

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Poliwrath but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Water Energy after the 2nd doesn't count.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

flavorText: An adept swimmer at both the front crawl and breaststroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.

attacks.name: Eruption
attacks.text: Each player discards the top card of his or her deck. This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

name: Chespin

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Plusle during your opponent's next turn.

name: Raikou

abilities.name: Thunder Rumble
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you attach a Lightning Energy card from your hand to Raikou, you may put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder Climb
attacks.text: Discard 3 cards from the top of your deck. This attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each Lightning Energy card you discarded. Then, attach those Lightning Energy cards to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to have fallen with lightning. It can fire thunderbolts from the rain clouds on its back.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Thunderstorm
attacks.text: For each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Then, Zapdos does 10 damage times the number of tails to itself.

flavorText: A legendary thunderbird Pokémon whose anger is said to cause storms. Some say it has lived above the clouds for thousands of years.

abilities.name: Dual Armor
abilities.text: As long as Slowbro has any Psychic Energy attached to it, Slowbro is both Water and Psychic type.

attacks.name: Parallel Gain
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon (including Slowbro).

abilities.name: Evolutionary Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Meganium from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards. Show them to your opponent and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Delta Reduction
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Buizel

attacks.name: Splash About
attacks.text: If Buizel has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It has a flotation sac that is like an inflatable collar. It floats on water with its head out.

name: Treecko
flavorText: Small hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.

name: Genesect

attacks.name: Gaia Blaster
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

name: Heracross

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It roams through forests searching for sweet nectar. Although it boasts fantastic physical strength, it's not that good at flying.

abilities.name: Dual Aura
abilities.text: As long as you have Latios or Latios ex in play, each player's Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) can't use any Poké-Bodies.

attacks.name: Spearhead
attacks.text: Draw a card.

name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Aromax
attacks.text: Heal all damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Windmill
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect. It withers if a Trainer does not take good care of it.

name: Ninjask

abilities.name: Wing Buzz
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may discard a card from your hand. If you do, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Afterimage Assault
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Ninjask and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Because it moves so quickly, it sometimes becomes unseeable. It congregates around tree sap.

abilities.name: Protective Dust
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, except damage, done to Dustox by the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

name: Togekiss

attacks.name: Blessed Wings
attacks.text: Remove all damage counters from each of your Pokémon. Shuffle Togekiss and all cards attached to it back into your deck.

attacks.name: Air Cutter
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: As everyone knows, it visits peaceful regions, bringing them gifts of kindness and sweet blessings.

name: Oddish
flavorText: During the day, It stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

name: Kakuna

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: This Pokémon is in a temporary stage while making its body. It is almost completely unable to move on its own.

name: Kricketot

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It chats with others using the sounds of its colliding antennae. These sounds are fall hallmarks.

name: Feraligatr

abilities.name: Downpour
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Water Energy card from your hand. This power can't be used if Feraligatr is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Riptide
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 damage times the number of Water Energy cards in your discard pile. Then, shuffle all Water Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

flavorText: It is hard for it to support its own weight out of water, so it gets down on all fours. But it moves fast.

name: Litleo

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The stronger the opponent it faces, the more heat surges from its mane and the more power flows through its body.

name: Entei

attacks.name: Burning Roar
attacks.text: Discard the top 4 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Fire Energy cards, attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Combat Blaze
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Quiver Dance
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward. If you attached Energy in this way, heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It loves the honey of flowers and can locate flower patches that have even tiny amounts of pollen.

name: Meowth V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.text: Draw a card.

name: Nidoqueen

attacks.name: Boyfriends
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Nidoking you have in play.

flavorText: Its hard scales provide strong protection. It uses its hefty bulk to execute powerful moves.

abilities.name: Holy Shield
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Togetic by your opponent's Pokémon that has Dark in its name.

attacks.name: Rainbow Moves
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon's attacks. Rainbow Moves copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) (No matter what type that Pokémon is, Togetic's type is still Colorless.) Togetic performs that attack.

name: Quilladin

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They strengthen their lower bodies by running into one another. They are very kind and won't start fights.

name: Heatran-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Heat Boiler
attacks.text: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Dynamite Press
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Celebi

attacks.name: Sprouting
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to Celebi. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Leaf Tornado
attacks.text: You may move any number of basic Grass Energy cards attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It has the power to travel across time, but is is said to appear only in peaceful times.

name: Fennekin
flavorText: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Quilladin

attacks.name: Scrunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Wood Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It relies on its sturdy shell to deflect predators' attacks. It counterattacks with its sharp quills.

abilities.name: Fast Protection
abilities.text: Prevent all effects, including damage, done to Ninjask by your opponent's attacks from his or her Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, Ninjask's Slash attack's base damage is 80.

name: Hoppip
flavorText: It drifts on winds. It is said that when Hoppip gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

name: Dwebble

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: The Pokémon can easily melt holes in hard rocks with a liquid secreted from its mouth.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Gloom

abilities.name: Irresistible Aroma
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if your opponent's Bench isn't full, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent reveals their hand. Put a Basic Pokémon you find there onto their Bench.

flavorText: Smells incredibly foul! However, around one out of a thousand people enjoy sniffing its nose-bending stink.

name: Pansage
flavorText: It shares the leaf on its head with weary-looking Pokémon. These leaves are known to relieve stress.

name: Snubbull

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It grows close to others easily and is also easily spoiled. The disparity between its face and its actions makes many young people wild about it.

name: Roserade

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage and remove 3 damage counters from Roserade.

flavorText: It attracts prey with a sweet aroma, then downs it with thorny whips hidden in its arms.

name: δ Rainbow Energy
rules: δ Rainbow Energy provides Colorless Energy. While attached to a Pokémon that has δ on its card, δ Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.)

name: Umbreon

attacks.name: Moonlight Fang
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects, including damage, done to Umbreon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 damage.

flavorText: When agitated, this Pokémon protects itself by spraying poisonous sweat from its pores.

name: Shedinja

attacks.name: Cursed Rain
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hopeless Scream
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

flavorText: A most peculiar Pokémon that somehow appears in a Poké Ball when a Nincada evolves.

name: Kricketot

attacks.name: Bug Hunch
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Grass Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

name: Poliwrath

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Poliwrath but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

flavorText: An adept swimmer at both the front crawl and breaststroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.

name: Jolteon

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Pin Missle
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It accumulates negative ions from the atmosphere to blast out 10,000-volt lightning bolts.

name: Grubbin
flavorText: They often gather near places frequented by electric Pokémon in order to avoid being attacked by bird Pokémon.

name: Porygon2

abilities.name: Energy Converter
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 1 basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and choose an Energy type. Treat that Energy card as that type until the end of your turn. This power can't be used if Porygon2 is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed. If Porygon2 becomes Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed, the Energy card goes back to its original type.

attacks.name: Delta Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose whether the Defending Pokémon becomes Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: This upgraded version of Porygon is designed for space exploration. It can't fly, though.

name: Lotad

attacks.name: Mini Drain
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It searches about for clean water. If it does not drink water for too long, the leaf on its head wilts.

name: Simisage

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

attacks.name: Poison Reaction
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Double Claw
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Dazzle Dance
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: When it moves rhythmically, it makes a sound similar to maracas, making the surprised Pokémon flee.

name: Tyranitar δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Fire Metal type.

abilities.name: Crush Draw
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may reveal the top card of your deck. If that card is a basic Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If not, put the card back on top of your deck. This power can't be used if Tyranitar is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Delta Crush
attacks.text: You may discard an Energy card attached to Tyranitar. If you do, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Slaking

abilities.name: Lazy Paunch
abilities.text: If Slaking used any attacks during your last turn, Slaking can't attack.

attacks.name: Best Dash
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Slaking by attacks is increased by 50 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The world's laziest Pokémon. When it is lounging, it is actually saving energy for striking back.

name: Cherubi

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: If this attack does any damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove 1 damage counter from Cherubi.

flavorText: The small ball holds the nutrients needed for evolution. Apparently, it is very sweet and tasty.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

abilities.name: Lunar Eclipse
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Solrock is in play, you may use this power. Until the end of your turn, Lunatone's type is Darkness. This power can't be used if Lunatone is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Cosmic Draw
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Evolved Pokémon in play, draw 3 cards.

abilities.name: Speed Boost
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Yanmega is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for a Grass Energy card and attach it to Yanmega. This power can't be used if Yanmega is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Wind Return
attacks.text: You may return all Grass Energy attached to Yanmega to your hand. If you do, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card you returned.

abilities.name: Quick Search
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose any 1 card from your deck and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Quick Search Poké-Power each turn. This power can't be used if Pidgeot is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Pick and Choose
attacks.text: Choose either Burned or Poisoned, and either Asleep or Confused. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by both Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Reactive Poison
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each Special Condition affecting the Defending Pokémon.

name: Roserade

attacks.name: Crosswise Whip
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Poison Point
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Each of its hands contains different toxins, but both hands can jab with near-fatal power.

name: Oricorio

attacks.name: Passionate Dance
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Fire Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Kindle
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It beats its wings together to create fire. As it moves in the steps of its beautiful dance, it bathes opponents in intense flames.

name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Serial Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Sleepy
attacks.text: If you have Piplup in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly. Even rain can't extinguish the fire.

name: Light Togetic

abilities.name: Gift
abilities.text: When you play Light Togetic from your hand, your opponent may search his or her deck for a Pokémon Tool card, show that card to you, and put it into his or her hand. Either way, you may do the same, and then each player who searched shuffles his or her deck.

attacks.name: Sweet Kiss
attacks.text: Your opponent may draw a card.

flavorText: It becomes depressed if it is not near kind-hearted people. Is is able to float in the air without moving its wings.

abilities.name: Rear Sensor
abilities.text: Each player's Active Basic Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) can't use any Poké-Powers.

attacks.name: Foresight
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards on either player's deck and put them back on top of that player's deck in any order you like.

attacks.name: Disorder
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Special Energy cards attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Draw In
attacks.text: Attach 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The fire on the tip of its tail is a measure of its life. If healthy, its tail burns intensely.

name: Jirachi
rules: This Pokémon is both Psychic Metal type.

attacks.name: Make a Wish
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it on that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) If you do, put 1 damage counter on Jirachi. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Glaceon

attacks.name: Snow Cloak
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Glaceon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Speed Slide
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: As a protective technique, it can completely freeze its fur to make its hairs stand like needles.

name: Blastoise

abilities.name: Wash Out
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Water Energy attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Blastoise is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hydro Launcher
attacks.text: Return 2 Water Energy attached to Blastoise to your hand. Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It deliberately makes itself heavy so it can withstand the recoil of the water jets it fires.

name: Pikipek

attacks.name: Nosedive
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can peck at a rate of 16 times a second to drill holes in trees. It uses holes for food storage and for nesting.

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Swallow Up
attacks.text: Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Carnivine. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Carnivine's, this attack does 60 damage instead.

attacks.name: Wring Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

abilities.name: Burning Energy
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may turn all basic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon into Fire Energy for the rest of the turn. This power can't be used if Charizard is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Scorching Whirlwind
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If 1 of them is tails, discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard. If both are tails, discard all Energy cards attached to Charizard.

name: Pyroar

abilities.name: Flare Command
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Inferno Onrush
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride.

name: Vileplume

attacks.name: Paradise Pollen
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 2 damage counters from Vileplume (1 if it only has 1). Then, if you have any Benched Pokémon with damage counters on them, choose 1 of them and remove 2 damage counters from it (1 if it only has 1).

attacks.name: Horrid Pollen
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes a loud jangling noise when it shakes its petals to spread poison powder.

name: Eevee

abilities.name: Chain Reaction
abilities.text: This power can only be used when a Pokémon evolves. Search your deck for a card that evolves from Eevee and attach it to Eevee. This counts as evolving Eevee. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Eevee is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from elemental stones.

abilities.name: Item Search
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Aftermath
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Pokémon Tool card in your discard pile. You can't add more than 60 damage in this way.

name: Hoothoot

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.text: Hoothoot can't be Asleep.

flavorText: It has a perfect sense of time. Whatever happens, it keeps rhythm by precisely tilting its head in time.

name: Gyarados-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam-GX
attacks.text: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Lugia

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

name: Heracross

abilities.name: Final Blow
abilities.text: If Heracross's remaining HP are 20 or less, you may make its Megahorn attack's base damage 120 instead of 60. This power can't be used if Heracross is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Megahorn
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This powerful Pokémon thrusts its prized horn under its enemies' bellies, then lifts and throws them.

name: Team Aqua's Walrein

attacks.name: Power Blow
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dual Blizzard
attacks.text: Discard 2 Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It's good at attacks that freeze opponents with a powerful cold snap. Then, it pulverizes a frozen foe with its sharp tusks!

abilities.name: Heavyweight
abilities.text: As long as there is a Grass Energy attached to Muk, you must pay an additional ColorlessColorless to retreat it.

attacks.name: Burning Sludge
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the amount of Grass Energy attached to Muk. If any of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned and Burned.

name: Dewpider

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It crawls onto the land in search of food. Its water bubble allows it to breathe and protects its soft head.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Rend
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

name: Delphox BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Delphox BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

abilities.name: Flare Witch
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

abilities.name: Psy Shadow
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Gardevoir is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Energy Burst
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the total amount of Energy attached to Gardevoir and the Defending Pokémon.

name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Oracle Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Antigravity
attacks.text: The Retreat Cost for Bronzong is 0 until the end of your next turn.

flavorText: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

abilities.name: Dual Aura
abilities.text: As long as you have Latias or Latias ex in play, each player's Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) can't use any Poké-Bodies.

attacks.name: Aqua Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Water Energy attached to Latios.

name: Accelgor

attacks.name: Deck and Cover
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

flavorText: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane.

attacks.name: Scary Face
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack or retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Kyogre but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

name: Kricketune

attacks.name: Improvisational Performance
attacks.text: If you have exactly 1 card in your hand, this attack does 100 more damage. If you have exactly 3 cards in your hand, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. If you have exactly 6 cards in your hand, this attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It crosses its knifelike arms in front of its chest when it cries. It can compose melodies ad lib.

name: Chatot

attacks.name: Me First
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Tone-Deaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

name: Blastoise

abilities.name: Deluge
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The jets of water it spouts from the rocket cannons on its shell can punch through thick steel.

name: Masquerain

attacks.name: Spiral Gyration
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It flaps its four wings to hover and fly freely in any direction–to and fro and sideways.

name: Cacturne

attacks.name: Spike Rend
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Hunt
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It becomes active at night, seeking prey that is exhausted from the day's desert heat.

abilities.name: Psylink
abilities.text: Sabrina's Alakazam always has a copy of every attack your Psychic Pokémon in play have (including their Energy costs and anything else required in order to use those attacks, such as discarding Energy cards). This power can't be used if Sabrina's Alakazam is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mega Burn
attacks.text: You can't use this attack during your next turn.

name: Grovyle

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Returning Flames
attacks.text: Put 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Its legs grow strong while it chases after its parent. It runs in fields and mountains all day.

attacks.name: Psychic Removal
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Grudge
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Tepig

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It can deftly dodge its foe's attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

name: Aerodactyl

abilities.name: Prehistoric Power
abilities.text: No more Evolution cards can be played. This power stops working while Aerodactyl is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: A ferocious prehistoric Pokémon that goes for the enemy's throat with its serrated saw-like fangs.

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Saliva Lure
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stick and Absorb
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counters from Carnivine. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It walks around on its tentacles in search of a tree branch where it can dangle down and ambush prey.

name: Togekiss

abilities.name: Serene Grace
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Togekiss from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at the top 10 cards of your deck, choose as many basic Energy cards as you like, and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Air Scroll
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage. If tails, remove 3 damage counters from Togekiss.

flavorText: It will never appear where there is strife. Its sightings have become rare recently.

name: Fletchinder

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on the bewildered prey that pop out of the grass.

name: Nuzleaf
flavorText: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

abilities.name: Magnetic Search
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Lightning or Metal Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Magnezone is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Speed Shot
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crush Volt
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to Magnezone.

name: Vivillon

attacks.name: Vivid Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

flavorText: The patterns on this Pokémon's wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

name: Giratina

attacks.name: Shadow Force
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Giratina during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Brutal Edge
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon that is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours. It appears in an ancient cemetery.

name: Serperior

attacks.name: Double Slash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can stop its opponents' movements with just a glare. It takes in solar energy and boosts it internally.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Raichu.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Raichu does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

name: Ralts

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It uses the horns on its head to sense human emotions. It is said to appear in front of cheerful people.

attacks.name: Pain Amplifier
attacks.text: Put a damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon has already has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Call of the Night
attacks.text: Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent shuffles his or her Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.

name: Shiftry

abilities.name: Darkness Fan
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Shiftry is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Evolved Pokémon on your opponent's Bench, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from that Pokémon, and put it back into his or her hand. This power can't be used if Shiftry is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Spirit Dance
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: By flapping its leafy fan, it can whip up gusts of 100 ft/second that can level houses.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its beautiful tails have made it very popular. However, if it's not brushed diligently, it will be a mass of tangles before you know it.

name: Thwackey

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

abilities.name: Flame Vapor
abilities.text: When you play Gyarados from your hand to evolve your Active Pokémon, you may flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon's Vengeance
attacks.text: If Gyarados has 7 or more damage counters on it, this attack's base damage is 100.

name: Skiddo

attacks.name: Growth
attacks.text: Attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

flavorText: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn't need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.

attacks.name: Mystic Fang
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Salandit

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Volcanoes or dry, craggy places are its home. It emanates a sweet-smelling poisonous gas that attracts bug Pokémon, then attacks them.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Constant Rattle
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 10 damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 30 damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 60 damage.

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Arid regions are their habitat. They move rhythmically, making a sound similar to maracas.

name: Lapras
flavorText: It likes swimming around with people on its back. In the Alola region, it's an important means of transportation over water.

name: Kabutops

attacks.name: Hydrocutter
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy cards attached to Kabutops. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. You can't flip more than 3 coins in this way.

flavorText: In the water, it tucks its limbs to become more compact, then it wiggles its shell to swim fast.

name: Entei

ancientTrait.name: θ Double
ancientTrait.text: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.

attacks.name: Flame Screen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

name: Meowth VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Gold Rush
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Vengeful Spikes
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Omanyte, Omastar, Kabuto, Kabutops, and Kabutops ex in your discard pile. You can't add more than 60 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Foongus

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.

flavorText: It lures Pokémon with its pattern that looks just like a Poké Ball then releases poison spores.

name: Salamence

attacks.name: Direct Hit
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Finish
attacks.text: Discard 2 basic Fire Energy cards or 2 basic Water Energy cards attached to Salamence. If you discarded 2 basic Fire Energy cards, this attack does 100 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If you discarded 2 Basic Water Energy cards, this attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (If you can't discard cards, this attack does nothing.)

flavorText: As a result of its long-held dream of flying, its cellular structure changes, and wings grew out.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Rampage
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Primeape. Flip a coin. If tails, Primeape is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: This Pokémon only calms down when there are no other animals or people around—which means no one has ever seen it when it's not angry.

name: Shiftry

abilities.name: Shifty Substitution
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, each Supporter card in your opponent's hand has the effect "Draw 3 cards." (This happens instead of the card's usual effect.)

attacks.name: Fan Tornado
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe's mind and take preemptive action.

name: Totodile

attacks.name: Fury Strikes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is small but rough and tough. It won't hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

name: Litleo
flavorText: The stronger the opponent it faces, the more heat surges from its mane and the more power flows through its body.

name: Jumpluff

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: If this attack damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove 1 damage counter from Jumpluff, if it has any.

flavorText: Once it catches the wind, it deftly controls its cotton-puff spores to float, even around the world.

name: Sceptile

abilities.name: Power of Nature
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Pokémon that have any Grass Energy attached to them by attacks from your opponent's Ultra Beasts.

attacks.name: Powerful Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It agilely leaps about the jungle and uses the sharp leaves on its arms to strike its prey.

name: Mr. Briney's Compassion
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play (excluding Pokémon-ex). Return that Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

name: Sentret

attacks.name: Grope
attacks.text: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other card on the bottom of your deck.

flavorText: It has a very nervous nature. It stands up high on its tail so it can scan wide areas.

name: Litten

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

name: Lombre

abilities.name: Top Entry
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you drew this Pokémon from your deck at the beginning of your turn and your Bench isn't full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is nocturnal and becomes active at nightfall. It feeds on aquatic mosses that grow in the riverbed.

name: Oshawott

attacks.name: Sleep Pulse
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The scalchop on its stomach is made from the same elements as claws. It detaches the scalchop for use as a blade.

name: Sewaddle

abilities.name: Swaddling Leaves
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, base damage of Swellow's Agility is 70 instead of 30.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Swellow during your opponent's next turn.

name: Cottonee
flavorText: Perhaps because they feel more at ease in a group, they stick to others they find. They end up looking like a cloud.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Raging Claws
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: In the rocky mountains where Charmeleon live, their fiery tails shine at night like stars.

abilities.name: Moonlight
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put a card from your hand back on your deck. If you do, search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Clefable is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 times the number of heads.

name: Umbreon δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Darkness Metal type.

abilities.name: Delta Moon
abilities.text: When your opponent attaches a Special Energy card from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. You can't use more than 1 Delta Moon Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It gallops at nearly 150 mph. With its mane blazing ferociously, it races as if it were an arrow.

name: Shinx

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads discard a Fire Energy attached to Chimchar.

flavorText: It agilely scales cliffs to live atop craggy mountains. Its fire is put out when it sleeps.

name: Skiploom

abilities.name: Floral Path to the Sky
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for Jumpluff, put this Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone, and put that Jumpluff in its place. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It spreads its petals to absorb sunlight. It also floats in the air to get closer to the sun.

name: Blaine
rules: During this turn, instead of attaching your free Energy card, you may instead attach 2 Fire Energy cards to 1 of your Pokémon with Blaine in its name. (Playing additional copies of this card this turn has no effect.)

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Burning Poison
attacks.text: Choose either Burned or Poisoned. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition. You may return Gliscor and all cards attached to it to your hand.

attacks.name: Pester
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.

flavorText: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

name: Charmeleon

abilities.name: Burning Fighter
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Fire Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it swings its burning tail, it elevates the air temperature to unbearably high levels.

abilities.name: Overzealous
abilities.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon-ex in play, each of Machamp's attacks do 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brick Smash
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cross Chop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Trample
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). If that coin flip is heads, this attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rend
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Braixen

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It has a twig stuck in its tail. With friction from its tail fur, it sets the twig on fire and launches into battle.

abilities.name: Sharing
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at your opponent's hand. You may use the effect of a Supporter card you find there as the effect of this power. (The Supporter card remains in your opponent's hand.) You can't use more than 1 Sharing Poké-Power each turn. This power can't be used if Milotic is affected by a Special Condition.

name: Dodrio

abilities.name: Retreat Aid
abilities.text: As long as Dodrio is on your Bench, your Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is ColorlessColorless less.

attacks.name: Incessant Peck
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It collects data and plans three times as wisely, but it may think too much and fall into a state of immobility.

name: Rillaboom

abilities.name: Voltage Beat
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Grass Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

name: Skuntank

attacks.name: Frustration
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that doesn't have any damage counters on it. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Toxic Cloud
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: It sprays a vile-smelling fluid from the tip of its tail to attack. Its range is over 160 feet.

name: Team Aqua's Kyogre-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Power Saver
abilities.text: If there are 4 or fewer Team Aqua Pokémon in play, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Aqua Impact
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

name: Chesnaught

abilities.name: Spiky Shield
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Touchdown
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its Tackle is forceful enough to flip a 50-ton tank. It shields its allies from danger with its own body.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Roserade

abilities.name: Le Parfum
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for any card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Squeeze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Each of its hands contains different toxins, but both hands can jab with near-fatal power.

name: Tropius

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The bunch of fruit around its neck ripens twice a year and is delicious. It's a highly favored tropical snack.

name: Deerling
flavorText: Their coloring changes according to the season and can be slightly affected by the temperature and humidity as well.

name: Karrablast

abilities.name: Shell On
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Shelmet from your hand. If you do, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

name: Paras

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Burrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Pokémon SP and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Team Attack
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Pokémon SP you have in play.

abilities.name: Extreme Speed
abilities.text: Arcanine G's Retreat Cost is Colorless less for each Fire Energy attached to Arcanine G.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Cure Powder
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Water-repellent powder on its wings enables it to collect honey, even in the heaviest of rains.

name: Altaria

attacks.name: Dragonbreath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Cotton Cloud
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Altaria by attacks from your opponent's Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It looks like a fluffy cloud when it is in flight. It hums with its soprano voice.

name: Luxio

attacks.name: Fasten Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its claws loose electricity with enough amperage to cause fainting. They live in small groups.

name: Karrablast

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its strange physiology reacts to electrical energy in interesting ways. The presence of a Shelmet will cause this Pokémon to evolve.

name: Druddigon

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Dragon Tail
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It races through narrow caves, using its sharp claws to catch prey. The skin on its face is harder than a rock.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Cleanse Away
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Able to understand human speech and very intelligent, it loves to swim in the sea with people on its back.

name: Krabby
flavorText: If it senses danger approaching, it cloaks itself with bubbles from its mouth so it will look bigger.

abilities.name: Empty Shell
abilities.text: When Shedinja is Knocked Out, your opponent doesn't take any Prize cards.

attacks.name: Extra Curse
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, put 4 damage counters instead.

name: Gible

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its nests is small, horizontal holes in cave walls. It pounces to catch prey that stray too close.

name: Seel
flavorText: Covered with light blue fur, its hide is thick and tough. It is active in bitter cold of -40 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Crustle

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle.

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Swarming Sting
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage times the number of Beedrill you have in play to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

name: Yveltal

attacks.name: Oblivion Wing
attacks.text: Attach a Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Darkness Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: When this legendary Pokémon's wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of all living creatures.

abilities.name: Reactive Booster
abilities.text: Each React Energy card attached to all of your Huntail and Gorebyss provides 2 Energy of every type but has no effect other than providing Energy.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Gorebyss but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 40 damage in this way.

name: Pansear
flavorText: When it is angered, the temperature of its head tuft reaches 600° F. It uses its tuft to roast berries.

name: Crobat

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Hurricane Wing
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The development of wings on its legs enables it to fly fast but also makes it tough to stop and rest.

abilities.name: Crush Chance
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Tauros from your hand onto your Bench, you may discard a Stadium card in play.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Vileplume

abilities.name: Varied Pollen
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, choose Asleep, Burned, Confused, or Poisoned. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

attacks.name: Giant Bloom
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.

name: Kricketune

attacks.name: Screech
attacks.text: During your next turn, any damage done to the Defending Pokémon by attacks is increased by 60 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It signals its emotions with its melodies. Scientists are studying these melodic patterns.

name: Venusaur

abilities.name: Energy Trans
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may take 1 Grass Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and attach it to a different one. This power can't be used if Venusaur is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Raichu.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Raichu does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

name: Magnezone

abilities.name: Super Connectivity
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a Lightning or Metal Energy card and attach it to your Active Pokémon. Then, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. This power can't be used if Magnezone is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Gyro Ball
attacks.text: You may switch Magnezone with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved from exposure to a special magnetic field. Three units generate magnetism.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Copy
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Copy copies that attack. This attack does nothing if Sudowoodo doesn't have the Energy necessary to use that attack. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Sudowoodo performs that attack.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: Does 50 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Sudowoodo.

name: Shroomish

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.

name: Ninetales

abilities.name: Nine Temptations
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard 2 Fire Energy cards from your hand. If you do, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It is vindictive and relentless by nature. Those who cross it even once will be cursed for a thousand years, along with their descendants.

name: Litwick

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Its flame is usually out, but it starts shining when it absorbs life force from people or Pokémon.

name: Cherrim

abilities.name: Cloudy Sky
abilities.text: If any of your Grass Pokémon or Fire Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, reduce that damage by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Worry Seed
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun's rays with its whole body.

name: Raikou

abilities.name: Lightning Burst
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Lightning Energy card from your hand to Raikou, if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. This power stops working while Raikou is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Lightning Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Raikou does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: The rain clouds it carries let it fire thunderbolts at will. They say that it descended with lightning.

name: Gardevoir V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swelling Pulse
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

abilities.name: Dark Trance
