abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Darkness Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Dark Dragonite is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Double Wing Attack
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to each Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Lonely Fang
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage plus 20 damage times the number of your opponent's Benched Pokémon minus the number of your Benched Pokémon. (For example, if your opponent has 3 Benched Pokémon and you have 1, this attack will do 30 damage plus 40 more damage.)

name: Kabutops

abilities.name: Primal Shell
abilities.text: As long as Kabutops is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play any Trainer cards from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Chop Up
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Ditto and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Ditto on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Duplicate Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Noctowl

abilities.name: Night Scope
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at your opponent's hand. This power can't be used if Noctowl is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When it needs to think, it rotates its head 180 degrees to sharpen its intellectual power.

abilities.name: Spiral
abilities.text: As long as Poliwrath is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Confused Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Mega Throw
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Flareon

attacks.name: Sand-Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Fire Slash
attacks.text: You may discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Inhaled air is heated in the flame sac in its body to an intense fire over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

abilities.name: Earth Rage
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Nidoking is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, put a damage counter on each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Nidoking is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Giant Horn
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Fomantis

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They give off a sweet and refreshing scent. Cutiefly often gather near the tall grass where Fomantis are hiding.

name: Machamp

abilities.name: Strikes Back
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent's attack damages Machamp (even if Machamp is Knocked Out), this power does 10 damage to the attacking Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.) This power can't be used if Machamp is already affected by a Special Condition when your opponent attacks.

flavorText: Using its amazing muscles, it throws powerful punches that can knock its victim clear over the horizon.

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Healing Wave
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Active Pokémon.

name: Ditto

abilities.name: Dittobolic
abilities.text: The number of Benched Pokémon your opponent can have is now 4. If your opponent has 5 Benched Pokémon, your opponent must discard 1 of them and all cards attached to it.

flavorText: Its transformation ability is perfect. However, if made to laugh, it can't maintain its disguise.

attacks.name: Scam
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand. You may have your opponent shuffle a Supporter card you find there into his or her deck. If you do, your opponent draws a card.

attacks.name: Metal Hook
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 20 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.

name: Lickitung

attacks.name: Continuous Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It checks out whatever's around it by licking everything. If you don't clean off a spot where it's licked you, you'll break out in a rash!

name: Rillaboom

attacks.name: Drum Roll
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Drum Beating
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Drum Beating.

flavorText: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

abilities.name: Sand Guard
abilities.text: Whenever Flygon would be damaged by your opponent's attack (after applying Weakness and Resistance), flip a coin. If heads, reduce that damage by 20.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy card attached to Flygon.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Blue plant vines cloak the Pokémon's identity in a tangled mass. It entangles anything that gets close.

name: Ludicolo

attacks.name: Spirited Rushdown
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Prize card you have taken.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The rhythm of bright, festive music activates Ludicolo's cells, making it more powerful.

name: Heatran

abilities.name: Flash Fire
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Fire Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to Heatran. This power can't be used if Heatran is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Magma Storm
attacks.text: You may discard as many basic Energy cards as you like attached to Heatran. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card you discarded.

flavorText: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

name: Drifblim

attacks.name: Balloon Tackle
attacks.text: Drifblim does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Take Away
attacks.text: Shuffle Drifblim and all cards attached to it back into your deck. Then, your opponent shuffles the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck. (You choose your new Active Pokémon first.)

flavorText: It can generate and release gas within its body. That's how it can control the altitude of its drift.

name: Parasect

attacks.name: Colorful Spores
attacks.text: Choose 3 of your Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a different type of basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.

name: Gengar

abilities.name: Curse
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon. This power can't be used if Gengar is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Shadow Skip
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) You may switch Gengar with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The leer that floats in darkness belongs to a Gengar delighting in casting curses on people.

name: Larvesta
flavorText: The base of volcanoes is where they make their homes. They shoot fire from their five horns to repel attacking enemies.

name: Chesnaught

attacks.name: Spike Lariat
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Adamantine Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When it takes a defensive posture with its fists guarding its face, it could withstand a bomb blast.

name: Simisage

attacks.name: Leaf Supply
attacks.text: You may attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It attacks enemies with strikes of its thorn-covered tail. This Pokémon is wild tempered.

name: Ditto

attacks.name: Copy Anything
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack. If this Pokémon doesn't have the necessary Energy to use that attack, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: While it can transform into anything, each Ditto apparently has its own strengths and weaknesses when it comes to transformations.

name: Shroomish
flavorText: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

name: Remoraid

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The water they shoot from their mouths can hit moving prey from more than 300 feet away.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Wide Laser
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Energy Control
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and attach it to another of your opponent's Pokémon of your choice.

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

name: Litleo
flavorText: The stronger the opponent it faces, the more heat surges from its mane and the more power flows through its body.

name: Simisear

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Stadium Burn
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body.

name: Scatterbug
flavorText: When under attack from bird Pokémon, it spews a poisonous black powder that causes paralysis on contact.

name: Exp. Share
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may move 1 basic Energy card that was attached to that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Shelmet

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it and Karrablast are together, and both receive electrical stimulation, they both evolve.

name: Gogoat

attacks.name: Milk Drink
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns.

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

name: Leavanny

attacks.name: Triple Cutter
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It keeps its eggs warm with heat from fermenting leaves. It also uses leaves to make warm wrappings for Sewaddle.

name: Dark Vileplume

abilities.name: Hay Fever
abilities.text: No Trainer cards can be played. This power stops working while Dark Vileplume is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Petal Whirlwind
attacks.text: Flip a coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. If you get 2 or more heads, Dark Vileplume is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: Perhaps because of their strong-smelling pollen, other Pokémon do not approach areas where Vileplumes live.

name: Amoonguss

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Miracle Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

flavorText: It moves the caps on both arms and does a dance to lure prey. It prefers damp places.

name: Dewgong

attacks.name: Freezing Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its streamlined body has little drag in water. The colder the temperature, the friskier it gets.

name: Pidgeot δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Metal type.

abilities.name: Delta Reserve
abilities.text: As long as Pidgeot has any Holon Energy cards attached to it, each player's Pokémon (excluding Pokémon that has δ on its card) can't use any Poké-Powers.

attacks.name: Rotating Claws
attacks.text: You may discard an Energy card attached to Pidgeot. If you do, search your discard pile for an Energy card (excluding the one you discarded) and attach it to Pidgeot.

name: Magmortar

attacks.name: Hard Crush
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards from your deck. This attack does 50 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.

attacks.name: Mantle Bazooka
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to Magmortar.

flavorText: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit out of its arms. Its breath also sears and sizzles.

name: Rillaboom

abilities.name: Voltage Beat
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Grass Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

name: Armaldo ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Twin-blade
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to each Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Supersonic Claws
attacks.text: This attack's damage is not affected by Resistance.

name: Araquanid

abilities.name: Water Bubble
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Fire Pokémon.

flavorText: It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.

name: Suicune

abilities.name: Aqua Recover
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Suicune from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your discard pile for up to 3 Water Pokémon, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.

attacks.name: Cure Stream
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It races around the world to purify fouled water. It dashes away with the north wind.

name: Spinda

attacks.name: Dish Out
attacks.text: Draw a card from the top and the bottom of your deck.

attacks.name: Synchro Punch
attacks.text: If any basic Energy card attached to Spinda is the same type as any Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: No two SPINDA have the same pattern of spots. Its tottering step fouls the aim of foes.

name: Brock
rules: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Crash Chant
attacks.text: Choose up to 4 in any combination of Pokémon Tool cards and Technical Machine cards in play (both yours and your opponent's) and discard them. This attack does 20 damage times the number of cards discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Horror Chant
attacks.text: If your opponent has 4 or more Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and return that Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your opponent's hand.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Numb
attacks.text: If Pikachu evolved from Pichu during this turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If it looses crackling power from the electric pouches on its cheeks, it is being wary.

name: Feraligatr

attacks.name: Energy Cyclone
attacks.text: Choose as many Energy cards from your hand as you like and show them to your opponent. This attack does 20 damage times the number of Energy cards you chose. Put those Energy cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Breaking Tail
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

flavorText: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Eevee does 10 damage to itself.

name: Swadloon

abilities.name: Swaddling Leaves
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

attacks.name: Miracle Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition (Asleep, Burned, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned). The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

attacks.name: Roasting Heat
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Burned, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Dragonite

attacks.name: Calming Wind
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions from Dragonite.

attacks.name: Dragon Stamp
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing. If both of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This marine Pokémon has an impressive build that lets it freely fly over raging seas without trouble.

name: Leafeon

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Leafeon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Just like a plant, it uses photosynthesis. As a result, it is always enveloped in clear air.

name: Kingdra

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Kingdra.

attacks.name: Dragon Tornado
attacks.text: If this attack doesn't Knock Out the Defending Pokémon, and if there are any Pokémon on your opponent's Bench, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said that it usually hides in underwater caves. It can create whirlpools by yawning.

attacks.name: Bursting Inferno
attacks.text: Each Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Vaporeon δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Water Metal type.

attacks.name: Delta Search
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Holon Energy card and attach it to Vaporeon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Return Wave
attacks.text: You may return an Energy card attached to Vaporeon to your hand. If you do, choose an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon and return it to your opponent's hand.

name: Jumpluff

attacks.name: Lost March
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon, except ◇ (Prism Star) Pokémon, in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Even in the fiercest wind, it can control its fluff to make its way to any place in the world it wants.

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Flick Poison
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

name: Lurantis-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flower Supply
attacks.text: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Solar Blade
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chloroscythe-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Dazzle Dance
attacks.text: Each Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Green Blast
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

name: Team Aqua's Grimer
flavorText: Its flexible body, which allows it to squeeze into any cracks, comes in handy when invading enemy hideouts.

name: Venonat

attacks.name: Radar Eyes
attacks.text: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At night, its kind is drawn by light.

name: Salandit

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It burns its bodily fluids to create a poisonous gas. When its enemies become disoriented from inhaling the gas, it attacks them.

name: Whimsicott

attacks.name: Fluffy Tag
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. During your next turn, the attacks of that Pokémon do 40 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: They appear along with whirlwinds. They pull pranks, such as moving furniture and leaving balls of cotton in homes.

name: Pikachu

abilities.name: Electro Recycle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Pichu is anywhere under Pikachu, you may search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. This power can't be used if Pikachu is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: BikaBika
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in forests with others. It stores electricity in the pouches on its cheeks.

name: Multi Technical Machine 01
rules: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card's attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Multi Technical Machine 01.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Petilil

attacks.name: Aromatherapy
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Since they prefer moist, nutrient-rich soil, the areas where Petilil live are known to be good for growing plants.

name: Sceptile

abilities.name: Power of Nature
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Pokémon that have any Grass Energy attached to them by attacks from your opponent's Ultra Beasts.

attacks.name: Powerful Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It agilely leaps about the jungle and uses the sharp leaves on its arms to strike its prey.

name: Pidgeot

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.text: Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, return the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your opponent's hand.

flavorText: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

name: Night Maintenance
rules: Search your discard pile for up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

name: Kingler

attacks.name: Guard Claw
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Lock-on
attacks.text: During your next turn, treat any tails flipped when using Magnemite's Electric Bolt attack as if they were heads. (Benching or evolving either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Electric Bolt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

flavorText: These Pokémon are attracted to electrical emissions and will often follow people using PokéGear.

name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: As a newborn, it can barely stand. However, through galloping, its legs are made tougher and faster.

abilities.name: Shell Retreat
abilities.text: As long as Squirtle has any Energy cards attached to it, damage done to Squirtle by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Salazzle

attacks.name: Ring of Fire
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned. It can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Filled with pheromones, its poisonous gas can be diluted to use in the production of luscious perfumes.

name: Staraptor

attacks.name: Accelerative Dive
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all damage done to Staraptor by attacks (both yours and your opponent's) until the end of your next turn.

attacks.name: Brave Heart
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Staraptor does 100 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has a savage nature. It will courageously challenge foes that are much larger.

attacks.name: Super Slap Push
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mega Throw
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-ex, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Midnight Eyes
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Perish Song
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep and was damaged or affected by Midnight Eyes during your last turn, the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Healing Song
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon.

name: Alolan Vulpix

attacks.name: Beacon
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It exhales air colder than -58 degrees Fahrenheit. Elderly people in Alola call this Pokémon by an older name—Keokeo.

name: Fearow

attacks.name: Repeating Drill
attacks.text: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It shoots itself suddenly high into the sky, then plummets down in one fell swoop to strike its prey.

name: Giovanni
rules: Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play with Giovanni in its name. For the rest of your turn, you may evolve that Pokémon even if you just played or evolved it this turn or if this is your first turn. This effect also applies to the Pokémon it evolves into.

name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, Scyther's Slash attack's base damage is 60 instead of 30.

flavorText: With ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one of it.

name: Delphox

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It gazes into the flame at the tip of its branch to achieve a focused state, which allows it to see into the future.

name: Muk

abilities.name: Toxic Gas
abilities.text: Ignore all Pokémon Powers other than Toxic Gases. This power stops working while Muk is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Sludge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

name: Vaporeon

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Clean, clear waters are its usual habitat. When it's about to be attacked by an invading enemy, it dives into the water to hide.

name: Karrablast

attacks.name: Mysterious Evolution
attacks.text: If Shelmet is in play, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When they feel threatened, they spit acidic liquid to drive attackers away. This Pokémon targets Shelmet.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dark Horn
attacks.text: You may discard a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from your hand. If you do, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and do 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Suicune

abilities.name: Crystal Body
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, other than damage, done to Suicune. This power stops working while Suicune is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Aurora Wave
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If only 1 is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Said to be the reincarnation of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

attacks.name: Meditate
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chakra Points
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

abilities.name: Reactive Generator
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Huntail has no React Energy cards attached to it, you may search your deck for a React Energy card and attach it to Huntail. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Huntail is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Bite Off
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

abilities.name: Self-healing
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Lightning Energy card from your hand to Jolteon, remove all Special Conditions affecting Jolteon.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Thunderspark
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon with at least 1 Energy card attached to it (yours and your opponent's).

name: Gardevoir VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Cure
attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Zapdos in order to use this attack.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon said to appear from clouds while wielding enormous lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Poison Bind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned and can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Long Whip
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by any Special Conditions, you may do 30 damage to any 1 Benched Pokémon instead. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Dark Muk
rules: This Pokémon is both Grass Darkness type.

abilities.name: Sticky Goo
abilities.text: As long as Dark Muk is your Active Pokémon, your opponent pays ColorlessColorless more to retreat his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Slimy Water
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of Colorless Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).

attacks.name: Acidic Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.

name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: The long hairs on its back act as lightning rods. The bolts of lightning it attracts are stored as energy in its electric sac.

abilities.name: Fluffy Fur
abilities.text: If Wigglytuff is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Wigglytuff is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Pester
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Deerling

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The color and scent of their fur changes to match the mountain grass. When they sense hostility, they hide in the grass.

abilities.name: Rain Dance
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Water Energy from your hand to 1 of your Water Pokémon. This power can't be used if Feraligatr is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hydro Crunch
attacks.text: Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Stun Needle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Reinforced Needle
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Arid regions are their habitat. They move rhythmically, making a sound similar to maracas.

name: Shelmet
flavorText: When attacked, it tightly shuts the lid of its shell. This reaction fails to protect it from Karrablast, however, because they can still get into the shell.

name: Articuno

attacks.name: Freeze Dry
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

name: Breloom

attacks.name: Dynamic Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

name: Piplup

attacks.name: Water Spash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

name: Cleffa

abilities.name: Sweet Sleeping Face
abilities.text: As long as Cleffa is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Cleffa by attacks.

attacks.name: Eeeeeeek
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 6 cards. Cleffa is now Asleep.

flavorText: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

attacks.name: Aqua Trance
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Super Hypnoblast
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Scraggy
flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling it skin up to its neck.

attacks.name: Gouge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Accelgor

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.

flavorText: Having removed its heavy shell, it becomes very light and can fight with ninja-like movements.

abilities.name: Temper Tantrum
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard as many cards as you like from your hand. If you do, put that many damage counters on Banette. This power can't be used if Banette is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Darkness Switch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to Banette, and then switch all damage counters on Banette with those on the Defending Pokémon. (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Loneliness
attacks.text: You may show your hand to your opponent. If you do and if you don't have any Pokémon in your hand, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

abilities.name: Unlucky Wind
abilities.text: As long as Shiftry is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent flips a coin during his or her turn, treat it as tails.

attacks.name: Conform
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Seal Off
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies during your opponent's next turn.

name: Blipbug
flavorText: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

name: Floatzel

abilities.name: Water Acceleration
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to Floatzel. This power can't be used if Floatzel is affected by a Special Condition.

flavorText: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

name: Politoed

abilities.name: Frog Song
abilities.text: Whenever Politoed's attack damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), if there are more than 3 Poliwags, Poliwhirls, Poliwraths, and/or Politoeds in play (including your opponent's), that attack does 40 more damage (no matter how many heads you get). This power stops working while Politoed is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Whenever three or more of these get together, they sing in a loud voice that sounds like bellowing.

name: Petilil

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The leaves on its head grow right back even if they fall out. These bitter leaves refresh those who eat them.

name: Azumarill δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Water Metal type.

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Hatenna
flavorText: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures' emotions. If you don't have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Irongrip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If it fails to crush the victim in its pincers, it will swing its victim around and toss it hard.

abilities.name: Leaf Trans
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Grass Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Meganium is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Supernatural
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand. You may use the effect of a Supporter card you find there as the effect of this attack. (The Supporter card remains in your opponent's hand.)

attacks.name: Dark Bind
attacks.text: You may discard a Darkness Energy card attached to Sableye. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Charizard

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Scorching Fire
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said that Charizard's fire burns hotter if it has experienced harsh battles.

name: Horsea

attacks.name: Splatter
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

name: Pyroar

abilities.name: Intimidating Mane
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scorching Fang
attacks.text: You may discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride.

name: Shiinotic

abilities.name: Illuminate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Grass Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Flickering Spores
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It emits flickering spores that cause drowsiness. When its prey succumb to sleep, this Pokémon feeds on them by sucking in their energy.

name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Foul Play
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

name: Slaking

attacks.name: Pitch
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The world's laziest Pokémon. It moves to another spot when there's no food left within its reach.

attacks.name: Dark Seed
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn. Put 5 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon at the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Spread Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Venusaur

abilities.name: Solar Power
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Your Active Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. This power can't be used if Venusaur is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Remove a number of damage counters from Venusaur equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Venusaur has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

name: Spewpa

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lives hidden within thicket shadows. When predators attack, it quickly bristles the fur covering its body in an effort to threaten them.

name: Leafeon

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you'll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

name: Dugtrio

attacks.name: Sand Impact
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Fighting Energy attached to Dugtrio. This attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Extremely powerful, they can dig through even the hardest ground to a depth of over 60 miles.

attacks.name: Attract Current
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Manectric does 10 damage to itself.

name: Sawsbuck

attacks.name: Nature Power
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to both your and your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Horn Leech
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck's horns.

name: Darumaka
flavorText: Darumaka's droppings are hot, so people used to put them in their clothes to keep themselves warm.

name: Pokémon Park
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Once during each player's turn, when that player attaches an Energy card from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, he or she removes 1 damage counter from that Pokémon.

name: Salazzle

abilities.name: Hot Poison
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may leave your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: For some reason, only females have been found. It creates a reverse harem of male Salandit that it lives with.

name: Victini

attacks.name: Me First
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being's entire body will be overflowing with power.

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 1 or Stage 2 card that evolves from that Pokémon in your hand, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.)

name: Surskit

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives in ponds and marshes that feature lots of plant life. It often fights with Dewpider, whose habitat and diet are similar.

name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Very competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.

name: Piplup

attacks.name: Water Sport
attacks.text: If Piplup has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus more 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Wavelet
attacks.text: If you have Buizel in play, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lives along shores in northern countries. A skilled swimmer, it dives for over 10 minutes to hunt.

name: Dewpider

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It crawls onto the land in search of food. Its water bubble allows it to breathe and protects its soft head.

name: Totodile

attacks.name: Fury Strikes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is small but rough and tough. It won't hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

abilities.name: Lazy Aura
abilities.text: As long as Slaking is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon-ex is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Lazy Headbutt
attacks.text: Slaking is now Asleep.

name: Frosmoth

abilities.name: Ice Dance
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Water Pokémon.

flavorText: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

name: Wooper
flavorText: When the temperature cools in the evening, they emerge from water to seek food along the shore.

name: Seedot

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 40 or less.

flavorText: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

name: Manaphy

abilities.name: Aqua Skin
abilities.text: When you attach a Water Energy card from your hand to Manaphy, remove 2 damage counters from Manaphy.

attacks.name: Chase Up
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Fountain
attacks.text: You may attach up to 2 basic Water Energy cards from your hand to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Born on a cold seafloor, it will swim great distances to return to its birthplace.

abilities.name: Dual Armor
abilities.text: As long as Lanturn has any Water Energy attached to it, Lanturn is both Water and Lightning type.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Lanturn but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more then 40 damage in this way.

name: Sawsbuck

abilities.name: Seasonal Blessings
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card.

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck's horns.

attacks.name: Synchronized Search
attacks.text: If Girafarig and the Defending Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, pick any 1 card from your deck and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Breaking Impact
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each Colorless Energy in that Pokémon's Retreat Cost to that Pokémon (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Flies at high speed and attacks using the large, venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

name: Erika
rules: You may draw up to 3 cards, then your opponent may draw up to 3 cards.

name: Volcarona

attacks.name: Solar Birth
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona's fire provided a replacement for the sun.

name: Venusaur

abilities.name: Energy Trans
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may take 1 Grass Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and attach it to a different one. This power can't be used if Venusaur is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

abilities.name: Evolutionary Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Cradily from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards. Show them to your opponent and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Torchic.

name: Xerneas-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Break Through
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: X Blast
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use X Blast during your next turn.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

name: Pheromosa

attacks.name: White Ray
attacks.text: If you have only 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: One of the Ultra Beasts. It refuses to touch anything, perhaps because it senses some uncleanness in this world.

name: Monferno
flavorText: It uses ceilings and walls to launch aerial attacks. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.

name: Ninetales

abilities.name: Roast Reveal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Fire Energy from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards. This power can't be used if Ninetales is affected by a Special Condition.

flavorText: Its nine beautiful tails are filled with a wondrous energy that could keep it alive for 1,000 years.

name: Foongus
flavorText: For some reason, this Pokémon resembles a Poké Ball. They release poison spores to repel those who try to catch them.

name: Team Aqua's Muk

abilities.name: Sludge Festival
abilities.text: The Retreat Cost of each Pokémon in play (except for Team Aqua Pokémon) is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Pester
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It immobilizes its opponents by wrapping them in its huge, poisonous body. It'll smoosh you, too!

name: Lugia

attacks.name: Elemental Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card, a Water Energy card, and a Lightning Energy card attached to Lugia in order to use this attack.

flavorText: It is said that it quietly spends its time deep at the bottom of the sea, because its powers are too strong.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Pikachu.

name: Dark Weezing

attacks.name: Mass Explosion
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the total number of Koffings, Weezings, and Dark Weezings in play (Apply Weakness and Resistance.). Then, this attack does 20 damage to each Koffing, Weezing, and Dark Weezing (even your own). Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

attacks.name: Stun Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned; if tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Researchers have observed these Pokémon reproducing in industrial waste sites.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to Exeggutor.

attacks.name: Big Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: If a head drops off, it emits a telepathic call in search of others to form an Exeggcute cluster.

attacks.name: Zzzap
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies (both yours and your opponent's). Don't apply Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Metallic Thunder
attacks.text: You may discard 2 Metal Energy attached to Raichu. If you do, this attack's base damage is 90 instead of 50.

abilities.name: Water Pressure
abilities.text: As long as Blastoise's remaining HP is 40 or less, Blastoise does 40 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 40 damage in this way.

abilities.name: Primal Aura
abilities.text: As long as Kabutops is your Active Pokémon, neither player can play Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from his or her hand to evolve Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dual Cut
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Auto Fire
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

abilities.name: Invitation
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Nidoqueen is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Vengeance
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Basic Pokémon and each Evolution card in your discard pile. You can't add more than 60 damage in this way.

name: Miracle Energy
rules:
You can't have more than 1 Miracle Energy in your deck. Attach Miracle Energy to 1 of your Shining or Light Pokémon. At the end of your turn, discard Miracle Energy.
While in play, Miracle Energy counts as every type of Energy but provides only 2 Energy at a time.

name: Articuno

attacks.name: Sharp Beak
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.

name: Aerodactyl

abilities.name: Prehistoric Memory
abilities.text: Whenever an Evolved Pokémon attacks (even if it's your opponent's), it can use any attack from its Basic Pokémon card or any Evolution card attached to it. It still has to pay for that attack's Energy cost. This power stops working while Aerodactyl is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Aerodactyl. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

flavorText: A vicious Pokémon from the distant past, it appears to have flown by spreading its wings and gliding.

abilities.name: Suction Cups
abilities.text: As long as Octillery is your Active Pokémon, whenever the Defending Pokémon retreats, discard all Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon when it goes to the Bench.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Shaymin

attacks.name: Flippity Flap
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Rally Back
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

name: Chesnaught BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Chesnaught BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Tough Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself. This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Slice
attacks.text: Raichu can't use Slice during your next turn.

attacks.name: Split Ball
attacks.text: Move an Energy card attached to Raichu to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Burst Ball
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy attached to any of your Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Venusaur

abilities.name: Miracle Aroma
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, choose either Asleep, Burned, or Poisoned. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition. This power can't be used if Venusaur is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Giant Bloom
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 4 damage counters from Venusaur.

flavorText: After a rainy day, the flower on its back smells stronger. The scent attracts other Pokémon.

name: Koga
rules: If an attack from a Pokémon with Koga in its name does damage to a Defending Pokémon this turn, that Pokémon is then Poisoned.

name: Ditto

abilities.name: Transform
abilities.text: If Ditto is your Active Pokémon, treat it as if it were the same card as the Defending Pokémon, including type, Hit Points, Weakness, and so on, except Ditto can't evolve, always has this Pokémon Power, and you may treat any Energy attached to Ditto as Energy of any type. Ditto isn't a copy of any other Pokémon while Ditto is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: Capable of copying an enemy's genetic code to instantly transform itself into a duplicate of the enemy.

name: Starly

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: They flock in great numbers. Though small, they flap their wings with great power.

name: Dialga LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Dialga. Dialga LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Time Skip
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your turn ends. If both of them are tails, after your opponent draws a card at the beginning of his or her next turn, his or her turn ends. This power can't be used if Dialga is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Metal Flash
attacks.text: During your next turn, Dialga can't use Metal Flash.

name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Boo-Hoo
attacks.text: If your opponent attaches an Energy card from his or her hand to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn, that Pokémon will be Asleep.

attacks.name: Leaf Slice
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

name: Garchomp

abilities.name: Rainbow Scale
abilities.text: If an Active Pokémon has Weakness to any of the types of Energy attached to Garchomp, Garchomp's attacks do 40 more damage to that Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Rainbow Scale Poké-Body can't be used if Garchomp has any Special Energy cards attached to it.

flavorText: When it folds up its body and extends its wings, it looks like a jet plane. It flies at sonic speed.

attacks.name: Recharge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to Raichu. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunder Reflection
attacks.text: You may move any number of Lightning Energy cards attached to Raichu to another of your Pokémon.

name: Kingdra

attacks.name: Aqua Stream
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for as many Water Energy cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and this attack does 10 damage for each Water Energy card you chose. Put those cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Dragon Pump
attacks.text: You may discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lives in caves on the seafloor and creates giant whirlpools every time it moves.

name: Nincada

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Because it lived almost entirely underground, it is nearly blind. It uses its antennae instead.

name: Cottonee

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: They go wherever the wind takes them. On rainy days, their bodies are heavier, so they take shelter beneath big trees.

name: Magmar

attacks.name: Controlled Burn
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When angered, it spouts brilliant fire from all over its body. It doesn't calm down until its opponent has burned to ash.

attacks.name: Shining Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: First Ticket
rules: Before you flip a coin to decide who goes first in a game, you may play this card. Don't flip that coin, and you go first. If both players play First Ticket, flip the coin as normal. (You may play only 1 First Ticket before you flip that coin.)
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Grass Basic Pokémon or Basic Pokémon with Team Aqua in its name and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thick Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Foongus

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: For some reason, this Pokémon resembles a Poké Ball. They release poison spores to repel those who try to catch them.

name: Leavanny

abilities.name: Blanket Weaver
abilities.text: Your Grass Pokémon take 40 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't apply more than 1 Blanket Weaver Ability at a time.

flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves by using the sticky silk secreted from its mouth.

name: Moltres-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Destructive Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Power Flame
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.

name: Forretress

attacks.name: Spike Spread
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Gyro Ball
attacks.text: You may switch Forretress with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It remains immovably rooted to its tree. It scatters pieces of its hard shell to drive its enemies away.

name: Octillery

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It has a tendency to want to be in holes. It prefers rock crags or pots and sprays ink from them before attacking.

name: Morelull

attacks.name: Flickering Spores
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: As it drowses the day away, it nourishes itself by sucking from tree roots. It wakens at the fall of night, wandering off in search of a new tree.

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Band Attack
attacks.text: Does 30 damage times the number of Beedrill you have in play.

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its best attack involves flying around at high speed, striking with poison needles, then flying off.

abilities.name: Burning Aura
abilities.text: As long as Typhlosion is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) between turns.

attacks.name: Flickering Flames
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Typhlosion.

name: Accelgor
flavorText: It moves with blinding speed and lobs poison at foes. Featuring Accelgor as a main character is a surefire way to make a movie or comic popular.

abilities.name: Sol Shade
abilities.text: As long as you have Solrock in play, each player's Fire Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) can't use any Poké-Powers.

attacks.name: Moon Guidance
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Trainer card (excluding Supporter cards), show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Litwick
flavorText: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leaches off the life force of any who follow it.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It lashes about with its tail to knock down its foe. It then tears up the fallen opponent with sharp claws.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Wobbuffet.

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Wobbuffet is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Panpour

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. It waters plants with it using its tail.

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Top Burner
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy from this Pokémon. Then, discard a card from the top of your opponent's deck for each Energy you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to be the legendary bird Pokémon of fire. Every flap of its wings creates a dazzling flare of flames.

name: Pachirisu

abilities.name: Self-Generation
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Pachirisu from your hand onto your Bench, you may attach up to 2 Lightning Energy cards from your hand to Pachirisu.

attacks.name: Shocking Bolt
attacks.text: Put all Energy cards attached to Pachirisu in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It's one of the kinds of Pokémon with electric cheek pouches. It shoots charges from its tail.

name: Shiinotic

abilities.name: Illuminate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Grass Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Flickering Spores
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It emits flickering spores that cause drowsiness. When its prey succumb to sleep, this Pokémon feeds on them by sucking in their energy.

name: Lilligant

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very difficult.

name: Amoonguss

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: They show off their Poké Ball caps to lure prey, but very few Pokémon are fooled by this.

abilities.name: Magnetic Field
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have basic Energy cards in your discard pile, you may discard any 1 card from your hand. Then search for up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. You can't return the card you first discarded to your hand in this way. This power can't be used if Magneton is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Magnetic Force
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon (including Magneton).

name: Dark Sandslash
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Darkness type.

abilities.name: Poison Payback
abilities.text: If Dark Sandslash is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Dark Sandslash is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It controls balls of fire. As it grows, its six tails split from their tips to make more tails.

name: Skiddo
flavorText: Thought to be one of the first Pokémon to live in harmony with humans, it has a placid disposition.

attacks.name: Hypno Splash
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Turtonator

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The shell on its back is chemically unstable and explodes violently if struck. The hole in its stomach is its weak point.

name: Luxray

abilities.name: Rivalry
abilities.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon LV.X in play, each of Luxray's attacks does 50 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Luxray.

attacks.name: Shock Bolt
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy attached to Luxray.

flavorText: It has eyes that can see through anything. It spots and captures prey hiding behind objects.

name: Torterra

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Leaf Storm
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from each of your Grass Pokémon.

flavorText: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to Mewtwo.

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard a Psychic Energy attached to Mewtwo and remove 6 damage counters from Mewtwo.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

abilities.name: Poison Resistance
abilities.text: Scizor can't be Poisoned.

attacks.name: Snatch
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with no damage counters on it and switch the Defending Pokémon with it.

attacks.name: Heavy Metal
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the amount of Metal Energy attached to Scizor. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

name: Croconaw

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

name: Araquanid

attacks.name: Bubble Net
attacks.text: Energy can't be attached to the Defending Pokémon from your opponent's hand during their next turn.

flavorText: It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.

name: Magmortar

abilities.name: Incandescent Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Fire Blaster
attacks.text: You may discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: From its arm, it launches fireballs hotter than 3,500 degrees Fahrenheit. Its arm starts to melt when it fires a whole barrage.

name: Roseanne's Research
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Basic Pokémon and basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Super Rod
rules: Shuffle 3 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Farfetch'd

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Spin Turn
attacks.text: Switch Farfetch'd with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: If it eats the plant stalk it carries as emergency rations, it runs off in search of a new stalk.

name: Lampent

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Absorb
