attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, Scyther's Slash attack's base damage is 60 instead of 30.

flavorText: With ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one of it.

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Parallel Drain
attacks.text: After your attack, remove from 1 of your Pokémon the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dozing Scales
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It loves the honey of flowers and can locate flower patches that have even tiny amounts of pollen.

name: Hariyama

attacks.name: Backward Belt Throw
attacks.text: You may do 80 damage plus 20 more damage. If you do, Hariyama does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It loves challenging others to tests of strength. It has the power to stop a train with a slap.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Heartfelt Song
attacks.text: Discard a Darkness Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It captivates foes with its huge, round eyes, then lulls them to sleep by singing a soothing melody.

name: Tepig
flavorText: It can deftly dodge its foe's attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

name: Hattrem
flavorText: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

name: Ninetales

attacks.name: Lure
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Ninetales or this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

name: Axew

attacks.name: Dragon Rage
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

name: Metagross
rules: This Pokémon is both Psychic Metal type.

abilities.name: Metal Juncture
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Metal Energy card attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Metagross is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Squared Attack
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Absol

attacks.name: Baleful Wind
attacks.text: Choose a card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it. If you discarded a Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium card, choose 1 more card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

attacks.name: Raid
attacks.text: If you played Absol from your hand during this turn, this attack's base damage is 40 instead of 10.

flavorText: It senses disasters coming and appears before people only to warn them of impending danger.

abilities.name: Volunteer
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 4 damage counters from Ninetales and discard Ninetales from Vulpix. If you do, search your deck for Ninetales or Ninetales ex and put it onto Vulpix (this counts as evolving Vulpix). Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Trick Tail
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

name: Turtwig
flavorText: Made from soil, the shell on its back hardens when it drinks water. It lives along lakes.

name: Bruxish

attacks.name: Vivid Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic Fang
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It stuns its prey with psychokinesis and then grinds them to mush with its strong teeth. Even Shellder's shell is no match for it.

name: Meganium

abilities.name: Herbal Scent
abilities.text: When you play Meganium from your hand, you may flip a coin. If heads, remove all damage counters from all Grass Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Meganium's breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

abilities.name: Regi Form
abilities.text: If you have Regirock, Regice, and Registeel in play, the attack cost of Regigigas's attacks is Colorless less.

attacks.name: Giga Power
attacks.text: You may do 60 damage plus 40 more damage. If you do, Regigigas does 40 damage to itself.

name: Dark Ariados

attacks.name: Entangle
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon, then flip a coin. If heads, the new Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Poison Bind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat until the end of your opponent's next turn and if the effect of an attack, Pokémon Power, or Trainer card would change that player's Active Pokémon, that part of the effect does nothing.

flavorText: Wherever it goes, it spins a thread that always leads back to its web.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 Energy cards from your discard pile and attach them to Mewtwo.

flavorText: Years of genetic experiments resulted in the creation of this never-before-seen violent Pokémon.

name: Masquerain

attacks.name: Threatening Pattern
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, Energy can't be attached from your opponent's hand to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its thin, winglike antennae are highly absorbent. It waits out rainy days in tree hollows.

name: TV Reporter
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw 3 cards. Then discard any 1 card from your hand.

attacks.name: Heavy Metal
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Metal Energy attached to Pikachu. This attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

name: Celebi

attacks.name: Psychic Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads. Remove a number of damage counters from Celebi equal to the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). If Celebi has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: When Celebi disappears deep in a forest, it is said to leave behind an egg it brought from the future.

name: Qwilfish

abilities.name: Counterattack Quills
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: To fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of water all at once.

name: Turtwig

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Turtwig.

attacks.name: Parboil
attacks.text: If you have Chimchar in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

name: Galarian Perrserker

abilities.name: Steely Spirit
abilities.text: Your Metal Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

name: Rowlet

attacks.name: Hide and Seek
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

name: Fletchinder

attacks.name: Peck Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The hotter the flame sac on its belly, the faster it can fly, but it takes some time to get the fire going.

attacks.name: Pollen Shield
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, Ledian can't become affected by a Special Condition. (Any other effects of attacks, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, and Trainer cards still happen.)

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

abilities.name: Compound Eyes
abilities.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Poké-Bodies, each of Butterfree FB's attacks does 30 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Select Powder
attacks.text: Choose either Burned or Poisoned. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

name: Yveltal-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Evil Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Y Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Volbeat

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Pester
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It lives around clean ponds. At night, its rear lights up. It converses with others by flashing its light.

name: Seedot
flavorText: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

name: Tyranitar ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Critical Crush
attacks.text: Discard 2 Basic Energy cards attached to Tyranitar ex or this attack does nothing.

name: Palkia LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Palkia. Palkia LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Restructure
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, you switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Palkia is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hydro Reflect
attacks.text: You may move all Energy cards attached to Palkia to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. (Ignore this effect if you don't have any Benched Pokémon.)

name: Sabrina
rules: Take all Energy cards attached to 1 of your Pokémon with Sabrina in its name and attach them to another 1 of your Pokémon with Sabrina in its name.

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys. (Any cards attached to Deoxys, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.text: If Deoxys and the Defending Pokémon have a different amount of Energy attached to them, this attack's base damage is 20 instead of 40.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Mountain Munch
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Scrape Down
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, discard the top 4 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

abilities.name: Afterburner
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. This power can't be used if Typhlosion is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Flare Destroy
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to Typhlosion and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Omanyte
rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Helix Fossil Omanyte
flavorText: Revived from an ancient fossil, this Pokémon uses air stored in its shell to sink and rise in water.

name: Poliwrath

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.text: Poliwrath does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Although an energetic, skilled swimmer that uses all of its muscles, it lives on dry land.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Marshtomp does 10 damage to itself.

name: Dragonite

abilities.name: Step In
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Dragonite is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: An extremely rarely seen marine Pokémon. Its intelligence is said to match that of humans.

name: Cottonee

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

name: Here Comes Team Rocket!
rules: Each player plays with his or her Prize cards face up for the rest of the game.

name: Entei-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Grand Flame
attacks.text: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Pineco

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It looks just like a pinecone. Its shell protects it from bird Pokémon that peck it by mistake.

name: Ninjask

abilities.name: Cast-off Shell
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for Shedinja and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its cry leaves a lasting headache if heard for too long. It moves so quickly that it is almost invisible.

name: Volcarona

ancientTrait.name: θ Stop
ancientTrait.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Burning Scales
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Wind Wheel
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona's fire provided a replacement for the sun.

name: Honchkrow

abilities.name: Dark Genes
abilities.text: As long as Honchkrow has the Energy necessary to use its attack, each of your Murkrow can use Honchkrow's attack as its own without the Energy necessary to use that attack.

attacks.name: Dark Wing Flaps
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

flavorText: Becoming active at night, it is known to swarm with numerous MURKROW in tow.

attacks.name: Slow-Acting Poison
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Aqua Smash
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Vivillon

attacks.name: Conversion Powder
attacks.text: Choose either Asleep or Poisoned. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

attacks.name: Colorful Wind
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Vivillon with many different patterns are found all over the world. These patterns are affected by the climate of their habitat.

name: Venonat

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At night, its kind is drawn by light.

name: Team Aqua's Seviper

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venom Tail
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, discard an Energy attached to that Pokémon.

flavorText: Seviper's tail is a sharp blade. It can even poison you!

abilities.name: Energy Draw
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard 1 Energy card from your hand. Then draw up to 3 cards from your deck. This power can't be used if Delcatty is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Max Energy Source
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Active Pokémon.

name: Infernape

attacks.name: Flare Blitz
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flare Up
attacks.text: If you have fewer than 10 Fire Energy cards in your discard pile, this attack does nothing. Shuffle 10 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

flavorText: It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.

abilities.name: Spike Storm
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Cacturne is your Active Pokémon, you may put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that already has any damage counters on it. This power can't be used if Cacturne is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Triple Needle
attacks.text: Choose 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of those Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The body is soft and rubbery. When angered, it will suck in air and inflate itself to an enormous size.

abilities.name: Intimidating Fang
abilities.text: As long as Mightyena is your Active Pokémon, any damage done to your Pokémon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Shakedown
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

abilities.name: Tailwind
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Dragonite is on your Bench, you may reduce your Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost to 0.

attacks.name: Dragon Tail
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

name: Kingdra

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Stream Pump
attacks.text: You may do 50 damage plus 30 more damage. If you do, return an Energy card attached to Kingdra to your hand.

flavorText: It sleeps deep on the ocean floor to build its energy. It is said to cause tornadoes as it wakes.

name: Blipbug

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A constant collector of information, this Pokémon is very smart. Very strong is what it isn't.

name: Pikachu
flavorText: Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.

attacks.name: Return Blow
attacks.text: If Aggron was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does the same amount of damage done to Aggron to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Fang
attacks.text: You may discard the top card of your deck. If you do, remove 2 damage counters and all Special Conditions from Aggron.

name: Chimchar
flavorText: It is very agile. Before going to sleep, it extinguishes the flame on its tail to prevent fires.

name: Virizion

attacks.name: Wrapped in Wind
attacks.text: You may attach a basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pike
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Using whirlwind-like movements, it confounds and swiftly cuts opponents.

name: Suicune

abilities.name: Safeguard
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Pokémon-EX.

flavorText: It races around the world to purify fouled water. It dashes away with the north wind.

name: Quagsire

abilities.name: Wash Out
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Water Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This carefree Pokémon has an easygoing nature. While swimming, it always bumps into boat hulls.

name: Raichu-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spark Ball-GX
attacks.text: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Shining Genesect

abilities.name: Energy Reload
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Grass Energy from 1 of your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gaia Blaster
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This ancient bug Pokémon was altered by Team Plasma. They upgraded the cannon on its back.

name: Snover
flavorText: It lives on snowy mountains. It sinks its legs into the snow to absorb water and keep its own temperature down.

name: Skiddo

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Thought to be one of the first Pokémon to live in harmony with humans, it has a placid disposition.

name: Wimpod

abilities.name: Wimp Out
abilities.text: During your first turn, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake.

name: Gogoat

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.text: This Pokémon's attacks do 80 more damage to your opponent's Grass Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Horn Leech
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can tell how its trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in the grip on its horns. This empathetic sense lets them run as if one being.

name: Azumarill

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Azumarill during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Bubble Pump
attacks.text: If Azumarill has 3 or more Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage. If Azurill is anywhere under Azumarill, flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lives in rivers and lakes. In water, its coloring and patterns trick the vision of foes.

name: Monferno

attacks.name: Loud Howl
attacks.text: Your opponent Switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Tail Slap
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

name: Chandelure

abilities.name: Flare Navigate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Absorb Life
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The spirits burned up in its ominous flame lose their way and wander this world forever.

name: Alolan Marowak

attacks.name: Limbo Limbo
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Alolan Club
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has Alolan in its name.

flavorText: Its custom is to mourn its lost companions. Mounds of dirt by the side of the road mark the graves of the Marowak.

name: Lt. Surge
rules: You can play this card only if you have at least 1 Basic Pokémon card in your hand. Put a Basic Pokémon card from your hand into play as your Active Pokémon. Put your old Active Pokémon onto your Bench. (You can't play this card if your Bench is full.)

attacks.name: Magnitude
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Deadly Poison
attacks.text: You may discard a Grass Energy card attached to Seviper. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Extra Poison
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

name: Moltres

abilities.name: Flame Charge
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Moltres from your hand onto your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for up to 3 Fire Energy cards and attach them to Moltres.

attacks.name: Scorching Wing
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy attached to Moltres.

flavorText: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

abilities.name: Energy Trans
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), move a Grass Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Sceptile is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Poison Leaf
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Slice Drain
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Sceptile.

abilities.name: Mist
abilities.text: Any damage done to Pelipper by attacks from Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Lightning Wing
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Chimchar
flavorText: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

name: Electivire

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Electivire.

attacks.name: Thunder Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Energy cards attached to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: As its electric charge amplifies, blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.

name: Hatterene

abilities.name: Hazard Sensor
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Life Sucker
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It emits psychic power strong enough to cause headaches as a deterrent to the approach of others.

name: Dwebble
flavorText: It makes a hole in a suitable rock. If that rock breaks, the Pokémon remains agitated until it locates a replacement.

name: Bounsweet

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: A delectable aroma pours from its body. They are often swallowed whole by Toucannon lured by that wafting deliciousness.

abilities.name: Healing Fire
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Fire Energy card from your hand to Blaine's Ninetales, remove 1 damage counter from it, if it has any. This power stops working when Blaine's Ninetales is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Burn Up
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Fire Energy cards attached to Blaine's Ninetales.

name: Ninetales

attacks.name: Color Coordination
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any basic Energy attached to it that is the same type as the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Each of its nine tails is imbued with supernatural power, and it can live for a thousand years.

name: Scatterbug

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

name: Feebas

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It eats anything, so it can even live in polluted streams and lakes. No one pays any attention to it.

abilities.name: Loose Shell
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Ninjask from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for Shedinja and put it onto your Bench. Treat the new Benched Pokémon as a Basic Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Quick Touch
attacks.text: You may switch Ninjask with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, you may move any number of Grass Energy cards attached to Ninjask to the new Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Stages of Evolution
abilities.text: As long as Magmar is an Evolved Pokémon, all Energy attached to Magmar are Fire Energy instead of their usual types.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Burning Sensation
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Talonflame

attacks.name: Grand Loop
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards. You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brave Bird
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: When attacking prey, it can reach speeds of up to 310 mph. It finishes its prey off with a colossal kick.

name: Torchic

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees F.

name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Zapdos in order to use this attack.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon said to appear from clouds while wielding enormous lightning bolts.

name: Dottler

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Thick Skinned
abilities.text: Snorlax can't become Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. This power can't be used if Snorlax is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Very lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.

name: Pansage

attacks.name: Bullet Seed
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It shares the leaf on its head with weary-looking Pokémon. These leaves are know to relieve stress.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Torchic.

name: Venomoth

attacks.name: Assassin Flight
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition. This attack does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The scales it scatters will paralyze anyone who touches them, making that person unable to stand.

name: Cool Porygon

attacks.name: Texture Magic
attacks.text: You may change Cool Porygon's Resistance to a type of your choice other than Colorless. If the Defending Pokémon has a Weakness, you may change it to a type of your choice other than Colorless. (Benching either Pokémon ends the effect on that Pokémon.)

attacks.name: 3-D Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A Pokémon that consists entirely of programming code. Capable of moving freely in cyberspace.

abilities.name: Healing Shower
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Milotic from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may remove all damage counters from all of your Pokémon and your opponent's Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex).

attacks.name: Distorted Wave
attacks.text: Before doing damage, remove 3 damage counters from the Defending Pokémon (all if there are less than 3).

name: Rowlet

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

name: Venusaur

abilities.name: Energy Trans
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may take 1 Grass Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon and attach it to a different one. This power can't be used if Venusaur is affected by a Special Condition.

flavorText: This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

name: Shelmet

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: When it and Karrablast are together, and both receive electrical stimulation, they both evolve.

name: Arcanine

abilities.name: Flame Dash
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Arcanine from your hand to evolve 1 of your Benched Pokémon, you may switch Arcanine with 1 of your Active Pokémon. If you do, you may move any number of Energy cards attached to that Pokémon to Arcanine.

attacks.name: Inferno Onrush
attacks.text: Arcanine does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.

name: Nuzleaf

attacks.name: Clear the Room
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there. Your opponent shuffles that card into their deck.

flavorText: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

name: Feraligatr

attacks.name: Hyper Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Second Strike
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

name: Lumineon

abilities.name: Lure Ring
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Lumineon is your Active Pokémon, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has a maximum HP of 100 or more and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This power can't be used if Lumineon is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Reverse Stream
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Lumineon. Then, return all Water Energy attached to Lumineon to your hand.

flavorText: It lives on the deep-sea floor. It attracts prey by flashing the patterns on its four tail fins.

name: Scizor

attacks.name: False Swipe
attacks.text: Does damage equal to half the Defending Pokémon's remaining HP (rounded down to the nearest 10).

attacks.name: Double Claw
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It swings its eye patterned pincers up to scare its foes. This makes it look like it has three heads.

name: Mamoswine

attacks.name: Ice Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Charge Through
attacks.text: You may do 60 damage plus 40 more damage and 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If you do, Mamoswine does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its impressive tusks are made of ice. The population thinned when it turned warm after the ice age.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Turtonator-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shell Trap
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Nitro Tank-GX
attacks.text: Attach 5 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks done to Machamp.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, ignore this effect.

attacks.name: Hurricane Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Darkrai LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Darkrai. Darkrai LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Dark Shadow
abilities.text: Each basic Darkness Energy card attached to your Darkness Pokémon now has the effect "If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance)." You can't use more than 1 Dark Shadow Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Endless Darkness
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, the Defending Pokémon is still Asleep. If both of them are tails, the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out.

name: Kyurem

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can produce ultracold air. Its body is frozen.

name: Whimsicott

attacks.name: Helping Hand
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.

name: Rocket's Sneak Attack
rules: Look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Trainer cards, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.

name: Froakie
flavorText: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

name: Growlithe
flavorText: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

name: Camerupt

attacks.name: Moving Fire
attacks.text: You may move a Fire Energy card attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to Camerupt.

attacks.name: Volcanic Crash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each tails, discard the top card of your deck. Ignore this effect if your opponent has any Water Pokémon in play.

flavorText: It has volcanoes on its back. If magma builds up in its body, it shudders, then erupts violently.

name: Tepig

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can deftly dodge its foe's attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

abilities.name: Intimidating Ring
abilities.text: As long as Ursaring is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Basic Pokémon can't attack or use any Poké-Powers.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 20 damage to the new Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Epic Splash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, Combusken's High Jump Kick attack's base damage is 70.

name: Rowlet

abilities.name: Sky Circus
abilities.text: If you played Bird Keeper from your hand during this turn, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon's attack costs.

attacks.name: Wind Shard
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.

name: Passimian

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Team Play
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Passimian.

flavorText: They form groups of roughly 20 individuals. Their mutual bond is remarkable—they will never let down a comrade.

name: Feraligatr

attacks.name: Spinning Tail
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

name: Froakie

abilities.name: Frubbles
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Water Energy attached to it, it has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

name: Ninetales

abilities.name: Bright Look
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hexed Flame
attacks.text: Does 50 more damage for each Special Condition affecting the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its nine tails are said to be imbued with a mystic power. It can live for a thousand years.

name: Honchkrow

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Blindside
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. This attack does 50 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

name: Claydol

abilities.name: Cosmic Power
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose up to 2 cards from your hand and put them on the bottom of your deck in any order. If you do, draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. This power can't be used if Claydol is affected by a Special Condition.

flavorText: An ancient clay figurine that came to life as a Pokémon from exposure to a mysterious ray of light.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Draw In
attacks.text: Attach 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The fire on the tip of its tail is a measure of its life. If healthy, its tail burns intensely.

name: Shuckle

abilities.name: Fresh Squeezed
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and discard them. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Energy Drink
attacks.text: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.

abilities.name: Overheat
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Team Magma's Camerupt. Put 2 damage counters on Team Mamga's Camerupt. This power can't be used if Team Magma's Camerupt is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Flame Ball
attacks.text: You may move a Fire Energy card attached to Team Magma's Camerupt to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Armor
abilities.text: If your opponent has 5 of more cards in his or her hand, any damage done to Pinsir by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Delta Call
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon that has δ on its card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

abilities.name: Fiery Aura
abilities.text: As long as Rapidash is your Active Pokémon, put 4 damage counters instead of 2 on Burned Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys. (Any cards attached to Deoxys, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Palkia

attacks.name: Wormhole
attacks.text: Switch Palkia with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its total control over the boundaries of space enable it to transport itself to faraway places or even other dimensions.

name: Cinccino

abilities.name: Make Do
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body secrets oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won't tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

name: Garchomp-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dual Chop
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Lampent

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It arrives near the moment of death and steals spirit from the body.

attacks.name: Lizard Poison
attacks.text: If 1 Energy is attached to Sceptile, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If 2 Energy is attached to Sceptile, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If 3 Energy is attached to Sceptile, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned. If 4 or more Energy is attached to Sceptile, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep, Burned, and Poisoned.

name: Gengar

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The leer that floats in darkness belongs to a Gengar delighting in casting curses on people.

name: Charizard

abilities.name: Energy Burn
abilities.text: All Energy attached to this Pokémon are Fire Energy instead of their usual type.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its wings can carry this Pokémon close to an altitude of 4,600 feet. It blows out fire at very high temperatures.

name: Meganium

abilities.name: Wild Growth
abilities.text: As long as Meganium is in play each Grass Energy card attached to your Grass Pokémon instead provides GrassGrass. This power stops working while Meganium is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The aroma that rises from its petals contains a substance that calms aggressive feelings.

name: Latias

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Infinite Wind
attacks.text: If Latios is on your Bench, remove 2 damage counters from each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It communicates using telepathy. Its body is covered in down that refracts light to make it invisible.

name: Lanturn

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Reflect Energy
attacks.text: Move an Energy card attached to Lanturn to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea's surface from a depth of over three miles.

name: Togepi

attacks.name: Sweet Kiss
attacks.text: Your opponent draws a card.

flavorText: A proverb claims that happiness will come to anyone who can make a sleeping Togepi stand up.

name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Chimchar.

flavorText: It agilely scales sheer cliffs to live atop craggy mountains. Its fire is put out when it sleeps.

name: Litten

attacks.name: Fasten Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It doesn't allow its emotions to be easily seen. Earning its trust takes time. It prefers solitude.

name: Shelmet

attacks.name: Mysterious Evolution
attacks.text: If Karrablast is in play, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It evolves when bathed in an electric-like energy along with Karrablast. The reason is still unknown.

name: Snorunt

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It is said that several Snorunt gather under giant leaves and live together in harmony.

name: Team Magma's Baltoy

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: They all cry out at once to let us know when they spot an enemy. It really helps out!

abilities.name: Psy Shadow
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Gardevoir is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Energy Burst
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the total amount of Energy attached to Gardevoir and the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bustle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. If either coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Talonflame BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Talonflame BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Flare Blitz
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Deoxys ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Deoxys ex and switch it with Deoxys ex. (Any cards attached to Deoxys ex, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys ex on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Fastwave
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Beedrill

abilities.name: Flutter Wings
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Grass Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Beedrill is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Needle Shock
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. Ignore this effect if any of your Pokémon used Needle Shock during your last turn.

flavorText: Its best attack involves flying around at high speed, striking with poison needles, then flying off.

attacks.name: Grind
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Dark Tyranitar.

attacks.name: Spinning Tail
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Bite Off
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 70 damage plus 50 more damage.

name: Omastar

abilities.name: Restoring Beam
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Restored Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Once wrapped around its prey, it never lets go. It eats the prey by tearing at it with sharp fangs.

name: Rayquaza δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Water Metal type.

abilities.name: Hydro Barrier
abilities.text: As long as Rayquaza has any Holon Energy cards attached to it, each of your Water Pokémon has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Delta Search
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Holon Energy card and attach it to Rayquaza. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Dark Magcargo

abilities.name: Hot Plate
abilities.text: As long as Dark Magcargo is your Active Pokémon, whenever a player puts a Baby Pokémon or Basic Pokémon onto his or her Bench from his or her hand, this power does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.) This power stops working if Dark Magcargo is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ball of Flame
attacks.text: You may discard a Fire Energy card attached to Dark Magcargo when you use this attack. If you do and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The shell on its back comes off easily, exposing the flames running through its body.

name: Illumise

attacks.name: Helping Hand
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Twirling Sign
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: With its sweet aroma, it guides Volbeat to draw signs with light in the night sky.

name: Magmortar

attacks.name: Smoke Bomb
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Flame Drum
attacks.text: If Magby isn't anywhere under Magmortar, discard 2 Energy cards from your hand. (If you can't discard 2 Energy cards from your hand, this attack does nothing.)

flavorText: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees F from the ends of its arms. It lives in volcanic craters.

name: Entei-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Brave Burn-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Milotic

abilities.name: Energy Grace
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack) you may Knock Out this Pokémon. If you do, attach 3 basic Energy from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-EX).

flavorText: Milotic is breathtakingly beautiful. Those that see it are said to forget their combative spirits.

name: Venomoth-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shinobi Mastery
attacks.text: If you played Koga's Trap from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage. If you played Janine from your hand during this turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Ten-Card Return-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 10 cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Vileplume δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Psychic Metal type.

abilities.name: Poison Pollen
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can't be used if Vileplume is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Trainer card in your opponent's hand.

abilities.name: Lazy
abilities.text: As long as Slaking is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Pokémon can't use any Poké-Powers.

attacks.name: Critical Move
attacks.text: Discard a basic Energy card attached to Slaking or this attack does nothing. Slaking can't attack during your next turn.

name: Misty
rules: Discard 2 of the other cards in your hand in order to play this card. If this turn's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and if the attacking Pokémon has Misty in its name, the attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Magmar
flavorText: When it breathes deeply, heat waves form around its body, making it hard to see clearly.

name: Furret

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It makes a nest to suit its long and skinny body. The nest is impossible for other Pokémon to enter.

name: Gogoat

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Supporter cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Charge Dash
attacks.text: You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can tell how its trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in the grip on its horns. This empathetic sense lets them run as if one being.

name: Oranguru

abilities.name: Instruct
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Deep in the jungle, high in the lofty canopy, this Pokémon abides. On rare occasions, it shows up at the beach to match wits with Slowking.

name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Sketch
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon attacked last turn, and Smeargle was in play during that attack, Smeargle copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack.

flavorText: A special fluid oozes from the tip of its tail. It paints the fluid everywhere to mark its territory.

name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Supersonic Flight
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Heatran

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Boiling Impact
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

name: Rayquaza

attacks.name: Inferno Spear
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy and a Lightning Energy attached to Rayquaza.

flavorText: It flies in the ozone layer, way up high in the sky. Until recently, no one had ever seen it.

name: Frogadier

abilities.name: Gale Shuriken
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute's time.

name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Zapdos or this attack does nothing.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon said to appear from clouds while wielding enormous lightning bolts.

name: Computer Error
rules: You may draw up to 5 cards, then your opponent may draw up to 5 cards. Your turn is over now (you don't get to attack).

name: Ho-Oh ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Golden Wing
abilities.text: If Ho-Oh ex would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may move up to 2 Energy attached to Ho-Oh ex to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Rainbow Burn
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 20 more damage for each type of basic Energy card attached to Ho-Oh ex.

name: Honchkrow

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Vengeance
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Darkness Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Marill from your hand onto Azurill (this counts as evolving Azurill) and remove all damage counters from Azurill.

attacks.name: Type Match
attacks.text: Choose a basic Energy card in your hand and show it to your opponent. Then, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card of that Energy type, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Horsea

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

name: Team Magma's Claydol

abilities.name: Magma Switch
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your Team Magma Pokémon.

flavorText: Claydol's beam can seize opponents and keep them from moving. Long-range attacks are its specialty!

name: Dragonair

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

flavorText: A mystical Pokémon that exudes a gentle aura. Has the ability to change climate conditions.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Raging Claws
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: In the rocky mountains where Charmeleon live, their fiery tails shine at night like stars.

name: Abomasnow

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: If it sees any packs of Darumaka going after Snover, it chases them off, swinging its sizable arms like hammers.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Steenee

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The sepals on its head developed to protect its body. These are quite hard, so even if pecked by bird Pokémon, this Pokémon is totally fine.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Overhead Throw
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Heavy Suplex
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It swings its long antlers wildly to attack. During cold periods, it hides deep in forests.

abilities.name: Primal Stare
abilities.text: As long as Kabutops is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play any Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from his or her hand to evolve his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Luring Antenna
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 20 damage to the new Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

name: Glalie

attacks.name: Wreck
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.

attacks.name: Avalanche
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It prevents prey from escaping by instantaneously freezing moisture in the air.

name: Torchic
flavorText: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees F.

abilities.name: Intimidating Fang
abilities.text: As long as Salamence is your Active Pokémon, any damage done to your Pokémon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Dragon Flame
attacks.text: You may discard an Energy card attached to Salamence. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Dialga

attacks.name: Time Bellow
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Flash Cannon
attacks.text: You may return all Energy cards attached to Dialga to your hand. If you do, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from the Defending Pokémon and shuffle that card into your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

name: Accelgor

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike during your next turn.

flavorText: Having removed its heavy shell, it becomes very light and can fight with ninja-like movements.

name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Berserker Splash
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Aqua Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

abilities.name: Crust
abilities.text: Any damage done to Pinsir by attacks from your opponent's Basic Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Sonicboom
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

abilities.name: Metal Trait
abilities.text: As long as Bastiodon has a Pokémon Tool attached to it, remove 1 damage counter from Bastiodon between turns.

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Bastiodon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Iron Tackle
attacks.text: Bastiodon does 30 damage to itself.

name: Torracat

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It boasts powerful front legs. With a single punch, it can bend an iron bar right over.

name: Gengar

abilities.name: Curse
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another (even if it would Knock Out the other Pokémon). This power can't be used if Gengar is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Dark Mind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Under a full moon, this Pokémon likes to mimic the shadows of people and laugh at their fright.

name: Orbeetle

abilities.name: Bug's Radar
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

attacks.name: Brainwave
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what's around it—and what's around it can include things over six miles away.

name: Whimsicott

attacks.name: Encore
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can only use that attack.

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Riding whirlwinds, they appear. These Pokémon sneak through gaps into houses and cause all sorts of mischief.

name: Shuckle-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Protective Shell
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have 2 or fewer Energy attached to them.

attacks.name: Triple Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Wrap-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Lie Low
attacks.text: All damage done to Brock's Dugtrio during your opponent's next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Earthdrill
attacks.text: This attack can't be used unless Brock's Dugtrio used its Lie Low attack last turn.

name: Alolan Marowak

attacks.name: Dance of Flames
attacks.text: For each Energy attached to your opponent's Pokémon, attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Burning Bonemerang
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads. If either of them is heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The bones it possesses were once its mother's. Its mother's regrets have become like a vengeful spirit protecting this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Supply
attacks.text: You may attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Multi Bubble
attacks.text: If Vaporeon has 3 or more different types of basic Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Spewpa

attacks.name: Protect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: The beaks of birds can't begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder.

name: Gossifleur

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It anchors itself in the ground with its single leg, then basks in the sun. After absorbing enough sunlight, its petals spread as it blooms brilliantly.

abilities.name: Strange Behavior
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to Slowbro as long as you don't Knock Out Slowbro. This power can't be used if Slowbro is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Zorua

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

name: Latios

abilities.name: Luster Float
abilities.text: If you have Latias in play, the Retreat Cost for Latios is 0.

attacks.name: Infinite Wing
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to Latios.

flavorText: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

name: Dartrix
flavorText: It throws one knifelike feather after another at its enemies, and each one precisely strikes a weak point. These feathers are known as blade quills.

name: Glalie

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Reflect Energy
attacks.text: Move a Water Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It prevents prey from escaping by instantaneously freezing moisture in the air.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its magnificent bark conveys a sense of majesty. Anyone hearing it can't help but grovel before it.

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Flies at high speed and attacks using the large, venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

name: Shiftry-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Den of Iniquity-GX
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Your opponent shuffles that Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Charizard-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Combustion Blast
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Combustion Blast during your next turn.

attacks.name: Bursting Hand
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand. This attack does 10 damage times the number of Energy cards there.

attacks.name: Shuffle Attack
attacks.text: Look at a number of cards on top of your opponent's deck equal to the number of Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon. Put those cards in any order, and then put them back on top of your opponent's deck.

name: Alolan Sandshrew

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It lives on snowy mountains. Its steel shell is very hard—so much so, it can't roll its body up into a ball.

name: Reshiram

attacks.name: Purifying Flame
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fusion Flare
attacks.text: If Zekrom is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.

name: Dartrix

attacks.name: Wing Flick
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak. If you don't groom it diligently, it will stop listening to you.

abilities.name: Cursed Glare
abilities.text: As long as Dusclops is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't attach any Special Energy cards (except for Darkness and Metal Energy cards) from his or her hand to his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic Shield
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Dusclops by your opponent's Pokémon-ex during your opponent's next turn.

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys. (Any cards attached to Deoxys, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Barrier Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Deoxys by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Accelgor

attacks.name: Raid
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Shelmet during this turn, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Afterimage Strike
