attacks.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

flavorText: Having removed its heavy shell, it becomes very light and can fight with ninja-like movements.

name: Articuno

attacks.name: Find Ice
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Water Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

name: Chandelure

attacks.name: Flame Burst
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Inferno
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Being consumed in Chandelure's flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind.

name: Elekid

abilities.name: Sweet Sleeping Face
abilities.text: As long as Elekid is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Elekid by attacks.

attacks.name: Sparking Ball
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. Elekid is now Asleep.

flavorText: Even in the most vicious storm, this Pokémon plays happily if thunder rumbles in the sky.

name: Crustle

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 80 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: It possesses legs of enormous strength, enabling it to carry heavy slabs for many days, even when crossing arid land.

name: Pignite

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.

name: Entei

abilities.name: Legendary Body
abilities.text: As long as Entei is your Active Pokémon, Entei and Energy cards attached to it aren't affected by effects from Trainer cards other than Stadium cards. As long as this Power is active, discard any Trainer cards attached to Entei. (This power works even if Entei is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.)

attacks.name: Mega Flame
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each tails, discard 1 Fire Energy card from Entei, if it has any.

flavorText: A Pokémon that races across the land. It is said that one is born every time a new volcano appears.

abilities.name: Bedhead
abilities.text: As long as Snorlax remains Asleep between turns, put 2 damage counters on 1 of the Defending Pokémon.

abilities.name: Dozing
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Snorlax is your Active Pokémon, you may remove 2 damage counters from Snorlax and Snorlax is now Asleep. This power can't be used if Snorlax is affected by a Special Condition.

name: Beautifly

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: After your attack, remove from Beautifly the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It has an aggressive nature. It stabs prey with its long, narrow mouth to drain the prey's fluids.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

attacks.name: Hand Refresh
attacks.text: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws up to 4 cards. (You draw your cards first.)

attacks.name: Payback
attacks.text: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 10 damage plus 50 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

abilities.name: Reactive Recharge
abilities.text: If Magneton would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may move any number of React Energy cards from Magneton to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Multiple Force
attacks.text: If Magneton has any React Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Magnemite and Magneton (both yours and your opponent's) in play.

name: Golisopod-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: First Impression
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Armor Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Crossing Cut-GX
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Pichu
rules: If this Baby Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and your opponent tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything required in order to use that attack). If tails, your opponent's turn ends without an attack.

attacks.name: Zzzap
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon in play that has a Pokémon Power. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

flavorText: Despite its small size, it can zap even adult humans. However, if it does so, it also surprises itself.

name: Litleo

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They set off on their own from their pride and live by themselves to become stronger. These hot-blooded Pokémon are quick to fight.

name: Lucario

attacks.name: Bulk Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, each of Lucario's attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It's said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras, even foes it could not otherwise see.

name: Shaymin

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flower Bearing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all attached cards and puts them on the bottom of their deck.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

name: Voltorb
flavorText: It was discovered when Poké Balls were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.

name: Dragalge

abilities.name: Poison Barrier
abilities.text: Your opponent's Poisoned Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker, and they spit it indiscriminately at anything that enters their territory.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Ghastly Sound
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Supporter cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Finger Poke
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its cheeks hold poison sacs. It tries to catch foes off guard to jab them with toxic fingers.

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

name: Frogadier

attacks.name: Aqua Wave
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

attacks.name: Bite Off
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Vaporeon

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Vaporeon but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Water Energy after the 2nd doesn't count.

flavorText: Lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin that is often mistaken for a mermaid's.

name: Dark Omastar

attacks.name: Prehistoric Water
attacks.text: If your opponent has any evolved Pokémon in play, choose 1 of them and flip a coin. If heads, your opponent takes the highest Stage Evolution card on that Pokémon and shuffles it into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Dark Tentacle
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't evolve except from effects of attacks or Pokémon Powers. (Benching that Pokémon ends this effect.)

flavorText: Nothing can escape once wrapped in its tentacles, which it uses for self-defense as well as to catch food.

name: Trevenant-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Forest
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Wood Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Finneon
flavorText: The line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.

name: Metagross

abilities.name: Magnetic Reversal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This power can't be used if Metagross is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Extra Comet Punch
attacks.text: During your next turn, Metagross's Extra Comet Punch attack's base damage is 100.

flavorText: Metang combined to form it. With four brains, it has the intelligence of a supercomputer.

attacks.name: Eruption
attacks.text: Each player discard the top card of his or her deck. This attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Fire Energy card discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Fire Stream
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card attached to Magcargo in order to use this attack. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Banette

attacks.name: Ghost Head
attacks.text: Put as many damage counters as you like on Banette. (You can't put more than Banette's remaining HP.) Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spiteful Pain
attacks.text: If Banette is in your discard pile, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage, then search your discard pile for Banette, show it to your opponent, and shuffle it into your deck.

flavorText: A doll that became a Pokémon over its grudge from being junked. It seeks the child that disowned it.

name: Omastar BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Omastar BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

abilities.name: Dangerous Tentacle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon-EX with his or her Active Pokémon.

name: Infernape

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attack. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Malevolent Fire
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses unique fighting moves with fire on its hand and feet. It will take on any opponent.

name: Magmar

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Found near the mouth of a volcano. This fire-breather's body temperature is nearly 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Exploud

attacks.name: Ambient Noise
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If the first coin is heads, this attack does 50 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If the first coin is tails, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If the second coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If the second coin is tails, your opponent can't play any Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its howls can be heard over six miles away. It emits all sorts of noises from the ports on its body.

name: Glaceon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Second Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crystal Ray
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.

name: Froslass

abilities.name: Cursed Glare
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't attach any Special Energy cards from his or her hand to his or her Pokémon.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It freezes prey by blowing its -58 degrees F breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

attacks.name: Earth Rift
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Then, flip a coin. If tails, this attack can't be used during your next turn.

attacks.name: Iron Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage. If both are tails, this attack does nothing. If 1 is heads and 1 is tails, this attack just does 50 damage.

abilities.name: Delta Sign
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Pokémon that has δ on its card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Delta Sign Poké-Power each turn. This power can't be used if Fearow is affected by a Special Condition.

name: Holon's Electrode
rules: You may attach this as an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon that already has an Energy card attached to it. When you attach this card, return an Energy card attached to that Pokémon to your hand. While attached, this card is a Special Energy card and provides every type of Energy but 2 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.)

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Numel

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its humped back stores intensely hot magma. In rain, the magma cools, slowing its movement.

name: The Rocket's Trap
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, choose up to 3 cards at random from your opponent's hand (don't look at them). Your opponent shuffles those cards into his or her deck.

name: Haunter

abilities.name: Transparency
abilities.text: Whenever an attack does anything to Haunter, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of that attack, including damage, done to Haunter. This power stops working while Haunter is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Because of its ability to slip through block walls, it is said to be from another dimension.

name: Palkia
rules: Lustrous Orb: If an Active Pokémon has Weakness to Water type, Palkia's attacks do 20 more damage to that Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Zone Shift
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pearl Blast
attacks.text: You may return an Energy card attached to Palkia to your hand. If you do, choose an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon and return it to your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Love Lariat
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 50 more damage if you have at least 1 Giovanni's Nidoking on your Bench. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Reshiram

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Blue Flare
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

name: Articuno

ancientTrait.name: Δ Plus
ancientTrait.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Chilling Sigh
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Tri Edge
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

name: Dragonair

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

flavorText: A mystical Pokémon that exudes a gentle aura. Has the ability to change climate conditions.

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Flies at high speed and attacks using the large, venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

name: Charizard

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Scorching Fire
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said that Charizard's fire burns hotter if it has experienced harsh battles.

name: Rainbow Energy
rules: Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy counts as every type of basic Energy but only provides 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, it does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.)

name: Venomoth

abilities.name: Shift
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may change the type of Venomoth to the type of any other Pokémon in play other than Colorless. This power can't be used if Venomoth is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Venom Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: The dust-like scales covering its wings are color coded to indicate the kinds of poison it has.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Earth Power
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Happiny

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Chansey from your hand onto Happiny (this counts as evolving Happiny) and remove all damage counters from Happiny.

attacks.name: Lively
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It loves round white things. It carries an egg-shaped rock in imitation of CHANSEY.

attacks.name: Delta Copy
attacks.text: Choose an attack on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play that has δ on its card. Delta Copy copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Togetic performs that attack.

name: Slugma

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

name: Fennekin

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

name: Petilil

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The leaves on its head are very bitter. Eating one of these leaves is known to refresh a tired body.

name: Victini

abilities.name: Victory Star
abilities.text: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack, you may ignore all effects of those coin flips and being flipping those coins again. You can't use more than 1 Victory Star Ability each turn.

attacks.name: Stored Power
attacks.text: Move all Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

name: Wooloo

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

name: Decidueye

abilities.name: Deep Forest Camo
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX.

attacks.name: Splitting Arrow
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye's shots never miss.

attacks.name: Hyper Healing
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter and all Special Conditions from each of your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Tack
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Defending Pokémon.

name: Karrablast

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Bite Off
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Team Magma's Aron
flavorText: Aron, which even devour metal, can eat and destroy enemy ships in an instant.

abilities.name: Fan Away
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, return 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to your opponent's hand. This power can't be used if Shiftry is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Light Touch Throw
attacks.text: Does 80 damage minus 10 damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Dark Slowking

abilities.name: Cunning
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, look at the top card of your opponent's deck. Then, you may have your opponent shuffle his or her deck. This power can't be used if Dark Slowking is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.text: Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for this attack.

flavorText: Each time it yawns, the Shellder on its head releases an enzyme that makes it grow even smarter.

name: Bellsprout
flavorText: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

name: Leafeon

attacks.name: Miasma Wind
attacks.text: Does 50 damage damage times the number of Special Conditions affecting the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you'll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

abilities.name: Sunshine Song
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you play Torterra from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may choose as many of your Grass Pokémon in play as you like. For each Grass Pokémon you choose, search your deck for an Evolution card that evolves from that Pokémon and evolve it. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Crash Impact
attacks.text: Torterra does 20 damage to itself. Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

attacks.name: Land Shake
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, when your opponent puts a Basic Pokémon from his or her hand onto his or her Bench, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Dark Tyranitar
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Darkness type.

abilities.name: Sand Damage
abilities.text: As long as Dark Tyranitar is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon between turns. You can't use more than 1 Sand Damage Poké-Body between turns.

attacks.name: Second Strike
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has at least 2 damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Virizion

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Wallop
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Leaf Wallop attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Using whirlwind-like movements, it confounds and swiftly cuts opponents.

name: Magmortar

attacks.name: Flame Screen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When launching 3,600 degrees F fireballs, its body takes on a whitish hue from the intense heat.

attacks.name: Scrunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Shelgon during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is capable of swimming backwards by rapidly flapping its winglike pectoral fins and stout tail.

name: Ho-oh

attacks.name: Rainbow Burn
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each type of Basic Energy card, if any, attached to Ho-Oh.

flavorText: A legend says that its body glows in seven colors. A rainbow is said to form behind it when it flies.

name: Gyarados

ancientTrait.name: θ Double
ancientTrait.text: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.

attacks.name: Full Retaliation
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on each of your Benched Magikarp.

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

name: Genesect

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Techno Blast
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

name: Decidueye

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Tracking Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It nocks its arrow quills and shoots them at opponents. When it simply can't afford to miss, it tugs the vine on its head to improve its focus.

name: Numel
flavorText: The magma in its body reaches 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit. Its hump gets smaller when it uses Fire-type moves.

attacks.name: Detect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Zangoose during your opponent's next turn.

name: Charmander
flavorText: The flame on its tail indicates Charmander's life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.

name: Beautifly

attacks.name: Colorful Powder
attacks.text: If Beautifly has 2 or less Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If Beautifly has 3 Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned. If Beautifly has 4 or more Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep, Burned, and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Silver Scale
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Resistance, this attack's base damage is 60 damage instead of 30.

flavorText: Despite its looks, it is aggressive. It jabs with its long, thin mouth if disturbed while collecting pollen.

name: Golduck

abilities.name: Natural Remedy
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Water Energy card from your hand to Golduck, remove 2 damage counters from Golduck.

attacks.name: Powerful Splash
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.

name: Magmortar

attacks.name: Smoke Bomb
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Flame Drum
attacks.text: If Magby isn't anywhere under Magmortar, discard 2 Energy cards from your hand. (If you can't discard 2 Energy cards from your hand, this attack does nothing.)

flavorText: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees F from the ends of its arms. It lives in volcanic craters.

name: Dugtrio

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A team of Diglett triplets. It triggers huge earthquakes by burrowing 60 miles underground.

abilities.name: Water Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Swampert is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hypno Splash
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

abilities.name: Happy Dance
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. You can't use more than 1 Happy Dance Poké-Power each turn. This power can't be used if Ludicolo is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Water Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Water Energy attached to Ludicolo. This attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Vivillon

abilities.name: Energy Color
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The patterns on this Pokémon's wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

name: Greninja

attacks.name: Mat Block
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

name: M Charizard-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crimson Dive
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

name: Spheal
flavorText: They can't swim well yet, and they move much faster by rolling. When they're happy they clap fins.

name: Phantump

attacks.name: Dark Guidance
attacks.text: Put a Basic Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.

flavorText: After a lost child perished in the forest, their spirit possessed a tree stump, causing the spirit's rebirth as this Pokémon.

name: Meganium

attacks.name: Ultra Powder
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If the first coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If the second coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. If the third coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fire Counterattack
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Fire Pokémon in play, this attack does 60 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its breath has the fantastic ability to revive dead plants and flowers.

name: Pignite

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.

name: Dugtrio

attacks.name: Dig Trap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Dugtrio during your opponent's next turn. If Dugtrio is your Active Pokémon at the end of your opponent's next turn, put 6 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pit Trap
attacks.text: Your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this attack does 50 damage plus 50 more damage.

flavorText: Its three heads moves alternately, driving it through tough soil to depths of over 60 miles.

name: Monferno

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It uses ceilings and walls to launch aerial attacks. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.

abilities.name: Rest Up
abilities.text: If Snorlax remains Asleep between turns, remove 2 damage counters from Snorlax (remove 1 if there is only 1).

attacks.name: Collapse
attacks.text: Snorlax is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Toss and Turn
attacks.text: If Snorlax is Asleep, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage. (This attack can be used even if Snorlax is Asleep.)

abilities.name: Major Tsunami
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Feraligatr is your Active Pokémon and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. Either way, if you have any Benched Pokémon, switch Feraligatr with 1 of them. This power can't be used if Feraligatr is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Rending Jaws
attacks.text: If here are no damage counters on the Defending Pokémon, this attack's base damage is 40 instead of 70.

name: Dark Persian

attacks.name: Tempt
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. if heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Popular with women because of its beautiful fur. The leader of the Rockets keeps one as a pet.

name: Granbull

attacks.name: Timid Tackle
attacks.text: Granbull does 20 damage to itself. Switch Granbull with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chomp
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Granbull.

flavorText: Because its fangs are too heavy, it always keeps its head tilted down. However, its bite is powerful.

name: Heracross

attacks.name: Powerful Friends
attacks.text: If you have any Stage 2 Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: With its Herculean powers, it can easily throw around an object that is 100 times its own weight.

name: Victini-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Turbo Energize
attacks.text: Search your deck for 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Intensifying Burn
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Foresight
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of either player's deck and put them back on top of that player's deck in any order.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, all damage done by attacks to Skarmory during your opponent's next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its sturdy wings look heavy, but they are actually hollow and light, allowing it to fly freely in the sky.

name: Tyranitar

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Trample
attacks.text: For each Benched Pokémon in play (yours and your opponent's), flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its body can't be harmed by any sort of attack, so it is very eager to make challenges against enemies.

abilities.name: Overzealous
abilities.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon-ex in play, each of Machamp's attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brick Smash
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cross Chop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Alolan Sandshrew

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It lives on snowy mountains. Its steel shell is very hard—so much so, it can't roll its body up into a ball.

name: Smeargle

abilities.name: Portrait
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Smeargle is your Active Pokémon, you may look at your opponent's hand. If you do, choose a Supporter card you find there and use the effect of that card as the effect of this power. This power can't be used if Smeargle is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Once it becomes an adult, it has a tendency to let its comrades plant footprints on its back.

name: Leafeon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Nature's Breath
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

name: Tsareena

abilities.name: Queenly Majesty
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have your opponent reveal their hand. Then, discard a card from it.

attacks.name: Trop Kick
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage and remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its long, striking legs aren't just for show but to be used to kick with skill. In victory, it shows off by kicking the defeated, laughing boisterously.

name: Bibarel

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions and 4 damage counters from Bibarel. Bibarel is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Bibarel does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

name: Surskit

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When this Pokémon senses danger, a sweet fluid oozes from the tip of its head. The taste of it disgusts bird Pokémon.

attacks.name: Extra Claws
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Horsea

attacks.name: Fin Smack
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

name: Lumineon

attacks.name: Neon Sign
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It lives on the deep-sea floor. It attracts prey by flashing the patterns on its four tail fins.

attacks.name: Tumble Down
attacks.text: Discard as many Fighting Energy cards as you like from your hand. The attack does 30 damage times the number of Fighting Energy cards you discarded.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Combusken

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its kicking mastery lets it loose 10 kicks per second. It emits sharp cries to intimidate foes.

name: Entei-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Brave Burn-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Electrode

attacks.name: Electroblast
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It explodes in response to even minor stimuli. It is feared, with the nickname of "The Bomb Ball."

name: Galarian Cursola V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Gnawing Aura
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hollow Missile
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Push Aside
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand and choose 1 Basic Pokémon or Evolution card you find there. Your opponent puts it at the bottom of his or her deck.

attacks.name: Supernatural Power
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Act Tough
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

name: Pyroar

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Incinerate
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: With fiery breath of more than 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, they viciously threaten any challenger. The females protect the pride's cubs.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

abilities.name: Streaming Mantle
abilities.text: When you play Magcargo from your hand to evolve your Active Pokémon, you may discard the top 3 cards of your deck and and then shuffle 3 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck. If you do, your opponent does the same.

attacks.name: Crushing Lava
attacks.text: You may discard a Fire or Fighting basic Energy card attached to Magcargo. If you discard a Fire Energy card in this way, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. If you discard a Fighting Energy card in this way, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Ninetales

attacks.name: Heat Acceleration
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 3 Fire Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Some legends claim that each of its nine tails has its own unique type of special mystical power.

name: Skiddo

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Thought to be one of the first Pokémon to live in harmony with humans, it has a placid disposition.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Raichu.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Raichu.

name: Cleffa

abilities.name: Sweet Sleeping Face
abilities.text: As long as Cleffa is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Cleffa by attacks.

attacks.name: Eeeeeeek
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 6 cards. Cleffa is now Asleep.

flavorText: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Wailord does 20 damage to itself.

name: Umbreon

attacks.name: Moonlight Fang
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects, including damage, done to Umbreon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon than has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: When agitated, this Pokémon protects itself by spraying poisonous sweat from its pores.

name: Hitmonlee

attacks.name: Stretch Kick
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When in a hurry, its legs lengthen progressively. It runs smoothly with extra long, loping strides.

name: Pyroar BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Pyroar BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Kaiser Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

name: Magmortar

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Twin Bursts
attacks.text: If Electivire is on your Bench, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit from the ends of its arms. It lives in volcanic craters.

name: Clauncher
flavorText: They knock down flying prey by firing compressed water from their massive claws like shooting a pistol.

name: Kingdra-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Reverse Thrust
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Maelstrom-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Masquerain

attacks.name: Surprising Pattern
attacks.text: Discard all Special Energy from each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hurricane Wing
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its wings and antennae don't cope well with moisture. After a rain, it faces sunward to dry off.

name: Buzzwole

abilities.name: Beast Boost
abilities.text: This Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Prize card you have taken (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Touchdown
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Although it's alien to this world and a danger here, it's apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

name: Emboar

attacks.name: Flare Blitz
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

name: Charizard-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flame Cloak
attacks.text: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Burning Breath
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

name: Ninetales

abilities.name: Barrier Shrine
abilities.text: Each player can't play any Stadium cards from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Flickering Flames
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

name: Lycanroc-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lycanfang-GX
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Minccino
flavorText: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

name: Dark Alakazam

attacks.name: Teleport Blast
attacks.text: You may switch Dark Alakazam with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.text: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: Almost as if it were being controlled by something else, it never changes expressions, even in the middle of battle.

name: Skiddo

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn't need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.

name: Trevenant

attacks.name: Shadow Cage
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it's kind.

name: Fennekin
flavorText: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

abilities.name: Ancient Fang
abilities.text: As long as you have Kabuto, Kabutops, or Kabutops ex in play, Omastar's attacks do 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

abilities.name: Gift Exchange
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Delibird is your Active Pokémon, you may shuffle 1 card from your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card. This power can't be used if Delibird is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Souvenir
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, put 4 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. If 2 of them are heads, remove 1 damage counter from the Defending Pokémon. If all of them are heads, put 10 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. If all of them are tails, remove all damage counters from the Defending Pokémon.

name: Victini

abilities.name: Victory Star
abilities.text: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack, you may ignore all effects of those coin flips and being flipping those coins again. You can't use more than 1 Victory Star Ability each turn.

attacks.name: Stored Power
attacks.text: Move all Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

name: Morpeko

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

name: Grumpig

attacks.name: Psychic Lock
attacks.text: Your opponent can't use any Poké-Powers on his or her Pokémon during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Bench Manipulation
attacks.text: Your opponent flips a coin for each of his or her Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage times the number of tails. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: It can perform odd dance steps to influence foes. Its style of dancing became hugely popular overseas.

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose up to 3 of them. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Clefairy from your hand onto Cleffa (this counts as evolving Cleffa) and remove all damage counters from Cleffa.

attacks.name: Eeeeeeek
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 6 cards.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Electromagnetic Induction
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Explosive Thunder
attacks.text: Discard all basic Lightning Energy cards attached to Raichu. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Lightning Energy cards you discarded.

flavorText: It turns aggressive if it has too much electricity in its body. It discharges power through its tail.

name: Houndoom

attacks.name: Dark Fang
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

attacks.name: Black Fire
attacks.text: You may discard a Fire or Darkness Energy attached to Houndoom. If you discard a Fire Energy, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. If you discard a Darkness Energy, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: Long ago, people imagined its eerie howls to be the call of the grim reaper.

name: Espeon ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

abilities.name: Purple Ray
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Espeon Star from your hand onto your Bench, you may use this power. Each Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) is now Confused.

attacks.name: Psychic Boom
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Electivire

abilities.name: Motor Drive
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Electivire. This power can't be used if Electivire is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy attached to Electivire. Flip a coin for each Lightning Energy you discarded. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.

abilities.name: Solar Eclipse
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Lunatone is in play, you may use this power. Until the end of your turn, Solrock's type is Fire. This power can't be used if Solrock is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Cosmic Draw
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Evolved Pokémon in play, draw 3 cards.

name: Jolteon

abilities.name: Electromagnetic Wall
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its lungs contain an organ that creates electricity. The crackling sound of electricity can be heard when it exhales.

name: Mesprit

abilities.name: Upper Material
abilities.text: If you have Uxie and Azelf in play, the Retreat Cost for each Uxie, Mesprit, and Azelf (both yours and your opponent's) is 0.

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.text: You may switch Mesprit with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Known as "The Being of Emotion." It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

attacks.name: Knock Over
attacks.text: You may discard any Stadium card in play.

attacks.name: Rampage
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Tauros. After doing damage, flip a coin. If tails, Tauros is now Confused.

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its spines provide protection. Its fins and bones are prized as traditional-medicine ingredients.

name: Phione

attacks.name: Evolution Wish
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: A Pokémon that lives in warm seas. It inflates the flotation sac on its head to drift and search for food.

abilities.name: Smooth Over
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a card. Shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of your deck. This power can't be used if Magcargo is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Knock Over
attacks.text: You may discard any Stadium card in play.

abilities.name: Attract Energy
abilities.text: When you play Magneton from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may move any number of basic Energy cards attached to your other Pokémon to Magneton.

attacks.name: Electric Blast
attacks.text: You may discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Magneton when you use this attack. If you do, put damage counters equal to the amount of Energy cards removed in this way on any number of your opponent's Benched Pokémon in the way you like. (For example, if you discard 3 Lightning Energy cards, you can put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and 2 on another.)

attacks.name: Snapping Pincers
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Overhead Toss
attacks.text: If you have any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If tails, choose 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Swallow Up
attacks.text: Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Carnivine. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Carnivine's, this attack does 60 damage instead.

attacks.name: Wring Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It's both clever and loyal, but if a stranger tries to invade its territory, it barks threateningly.

name: Gossifleur

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

name: Ho-Oh-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Rebirth
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may flip a coin. If heads, put this Pokémon onto your Bench and attach 3 different types of basic Energy cards from the discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rainbow Burn
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each different type of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Blaziken

attacks.name: Blaze Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Flames spout from its wrists, enveloping its knuckles. Its punches scorch it foes.

abilities.name: Fellowship
abilities.text: Bellossom can use the attacks of all Oddish, Gloom, Vileplume, Vileplume ex, or other Bellossom you have in play as its own. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

attacks.name: Aqua Flower
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, Bellossom has no Weakness.

name: Victini-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Turbo Energize
attacks.text: Search your deck for 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Intensifying Burn
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.

name: Weepinbell

attacks.name: Burning Venom
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.

flavorText: The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.

name: Numel

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The magma in its body reaches 2,200 degrees F. Its hump gets smaller when it uses Fire-type moves.

name: Charmander
flavorText: The flame on its tail indicates Charmander's life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.

name: Foongus

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

name: Umbreon
flavorText: When darkness falls, the rings on the body begin to glow, striking fear in the hearts of anyone nearby.

attacks.name: Power Blow
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Breloom.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
